Job Category All Clear Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Create Alert × Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Apply now » Singapore Health Services Pte Ltd Assistant Director (Regulatory Affairs) Medical Technology Office (Contract) Job Category: Administration Posting Date: 20 Jun 2026 Join SingHealth and play a pivotal role in transforming healthcare innovation across Singapore's largest public healthcare cluster. You will lead our regulatory strategy as we advance towards product ownership and work at the intersection of cutting-edge medical technology and regulatory excellence to bring life-changing innovations to patients. We are seeking an Assistant Director to develop and execute comprehensive regulatory strategies that accelerate our innovation pipeline from concept to market. You will provide strategic leadership for our regulatory affairs function, build relationships with health authorities and stakeholders whilst establishing SingHealth as a thought leader in healthcare regulatory innovation. What You Will Do: You will develop and implement strategic regulatory frameworks for innovation projects across multiple jurisdictions, providing executive-level oversight to ensure alignment with SingHealth's product ownership objectives. Your role involves leading high-level negotiations and strategic discussions with health authorities, managing relationships with senior executives across institutions and business partners, and representing the cluster at industry regulatory forums and advisory committees. You'll oversee comprehensive regulatory portfolio management for innovation projects, coordinate submissions and post-approval activities whilst ensuring maintenance of all product registrations. Additionally, you will lead and develop the regulatory affairs department, establish departmental processes and provide mentorship to team members, whilst driving regulatory intelligence activities to anticipate and influence regulatory developments affecting our innovations. You'll also contribute to quality assurance initiatives, work with stakeholders to establish regulatory document control repositories and support adverse event investigations to ensure post-market surveillance compliance. Job Requirements: Bachelor’s Degree preferably in Life Sciences, Engineering, Computer Science or Business with 10 years of relevant working experience in healthcare innovation Industry experience in regulatory submissions with familiarity in ISO 13485, ISO 14971, IEC 62304, IEC 60601, and ISO 27001 standards and Health Sciences Authority (HSA) regulatory submissions Proven track record in regulatory registration of medical devices and medical-use software and managing international certification projects and engaging with health authorities Experience with medical device and Software as Medical Device (SaMD) regulatory requirements and maintenance plans Excellent project management abilities and organisational skills with strong attention to detail Strong network and understanding of medtech ecosystem and public healthcare landscape Strong leadership attributes with excellent communication and collaboration skills across cross-functional teams Req ID: 4870 Apply now » Find similar jobs: SingHealth HQ - All Opportunities, SingHealth - Management / Admin, SingHealth - All Opportunities © 2025 SingHealth Group. This exciting opportunity calls for an experienced professional to establish and lead a dedicated Conflict of Interest Unit within our Technology Development & Commercialisation (TD&C) team. The role offers the chance to shape institutional policy and practice while supporting our healthcare professionals in their research, innovation and industry partnerships. As Assistant Director, you will establish comprehensive frameworks and processes to identify, assess and manage conflict of interest matters across the institution. You will lead a team that sits at the heart of data-driven HR decision-making to support the building of a dynamic workforce for a transformative healthcare system. We are seeking a Senior Assistant Director to lead the Manpower Planning & Analytics team. You will oversee workforce data analytics, manpower establishment governance, and manpower reporting to senior leadership and the Ministry of Health (MOH). As Assistant Director, you'll lead our diagnostic development efforts, working to bring SingHealth's innovative diagnostic technologies to market. Guided by our vision of "Patients. At the Heart of All We Do", OST drives programmes and projects that tackle the fundamental challenges of delivering world-class patient care through the efficient use of limited resources — creating meaningful impact in care quality, productivity, and sustainability. We are seeking an Assistant Director to support the Head of Department (HOD) and leadership team in driving service transformation and organisational excellence. You will lead end-to-end project delivery, engage diverse stakeholders, and contribute to strategic planning efforts that advance OST's mission of impactful, sustainable change. What You Will Do: You will lead the planning, initiation, execution, and monitoring of transformation projects, ensuring they are delivered on time, within scope, and aligned with established milestones and key performance indicators. Working closely with internal and external stakeholders, you will coordinate to ensure policy and operational alignment, map process workflows, analyse baseline data, identify performance gaps, and develop actionable solutions that drive meaningful improvement. A key part of your role involves keeping senior leadership informed and equipped to make timely decisions. You will prepare and present project updates and recommendations for Core Leadership, CEO-CMB meetings, and other senior management forums, and provide secretariat support for task forces and departmental meetings — setting agendas and ensuring effective follow-through on decisions and action items. You will drive horizon scanning of emerging technologies, innovative care models, and industry trends, analysing and recommending new approaches to improve service delivery and business processes. You will also oversee the development of communication materials and collaterals across various formats, including copywriting, concept visualisations, and presentations that build engagement and momentum for transformation initiatives. You will support the division's and department's strategic planning processes, ensuring alignment with organisational priorities through robust planning frameworks, and contribute to departmental performance management through regular review of work plans and the Balanced Scorecard. You will also supervise and mentor one direct report, supporting their growth and development, and may be called upon to lead or support special projects and events as directed by the HOD. Job Requirements: Bachelor's Degree with at least 10 years of relevant working experience A relevant Master's Degree (e.g. Master of Public Health) would be an advantage Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage and influence diverse stakeholders at all levels Strategic and critical thinker who is adept at navigating complex, ambiguous, and fast-changing environments Proven ability to lead projects end-to-end and deliver results under pressure or within tight timelines Collaborative team player who works effectively across diverse teams, dynamic reporting lines, and multiple stakeholders Demonstrated interest in and ability to lead, coach, and develop junior staff Req ID: 4966 Apply now » Find similar jobs: SingHealth HQ - All Opportunities, SingHealth - Management / Admin, SingHealth - All Opportunities © 2025 SingHealth Group. Job Category All Clear Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Create Alert × Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Apply now » Singapore Health Services Pte Ltd Assistant Director (Tech, GAIMx), AI in Medicine Institute (Contract) Job Category: Administration Posting Date: 27 Jun 2026 Join SingHealth's AI in Medicine Institute as a technical leader driving the future of healthcare through artificial intelligence. You'll architect and oversee the GAIMx Generative AI platform, bringing together multiple AI initiatives across SingHealth–Duke-NUS to create a robust, trusted, and scalable ecosystem that transforms clinical care and research. We are seeking an Assistant Director to design, build, and oversee the technical architecture of the GAIMx platform. You'll establish AI development best practices, lead the creation of reusable and secure AI components including domain-specific foundation models, and ensure interoperability across diverse healthcare systems whilst maintaining the highest standards of security and compliance. What You'll Do: You will lead the end-to-end development of the GAIMx solution, managing both internal and external technical development teams whilst mentoring engineering and data science teams in healthcare technology best practices. Your role involves collaborating with clinicians, data stewards, and compliance teams to align technology with clinical outcomes, driving vendor evaluations and proof-of-concepts, and establishing development standard operating procedures and solution specifications. You'll architect scalable, secure, cloud-based solutions across AWS, Azure, or Google Cloud Platform, ensuring high availability and data compliance with HIPAA, Personal Data Protection Act, and General Data Protection Regulation standards. Additionally, you'll integrate diverse clinical, research, and operational systems through healthcare standards including Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources APIs, Health Level Seven version 2, openEHR, and Observational Medical Outcomes Partnership Common Data Model. Your responsibilities include designing architectures aligned with Zero Trust Security and National Institute of Standards and Technology frameworks, overseeing implementation of identity and access management, encryption, and security monitoring, whilst conducting periodic security risk assessments. You'll guide teams in microservices and API-driven architectures, lead engineering of data pipelines for healthcare data using technologies like Spark, Kafka, and Databricks, and partner with data scientists to deploy Generative AI and large language models in clinical workflows. Furthermore, you'll implement AI safety guardrails, privacy-preserving techniques such as federated learning, and drive responsible AI practices ensuring model explainability and bias mitigation. Job Requirements: Bachelor's or Master's degree in Computer Science, Biomedical Informatics, or related field 15+ years in IT/Software architecture with 5+ years specifically in healthcare technology Proven expertise across software engineering, data architecture, cybersecurity, and healthcare interoperability Strong working knowledge of FHIR, OMOP, openEHR, and healthcare data standards Cloud certifications such as AWS Solutions Architect, Azure Architect Expert, or GCP Professional Architect Familiarity with large language models and Generative AI frameworks including LangChain, OpenAI, Hugging Face, or Vertex AI Experience with clinical informatics systems such as Epic, Cerner, or Meditech is advantageous Knowledge of healthcare regulatory standards including HIPAA, HITECH, PDPA, or ISO 27001 is beneficial Published work or open-source contributions in healthcare AI or interoperability is preferred Strong technical leadership abilities with excellent communication skills to explain complex AI concepts to non-technical stakeholders Proactive and detail-oriented approach with ability to thrive in multi-stakeholder, fast-moving environments High integrity and commitment to safe, ethical AI design Req ID: 4688 Apply now » Find similar jobs: SingHealth HQ - All Opportunities, SingHealth - Management / Admin, SingHealth - All Opportunities © 2025 SingHealth Group. As Deputy Director, HR, you will sit at the centre of a bold People First agenda. Reporting directly to the Chief Human Resource Officer, you will partner senior leaders to translate strategy into meaningful people outcomes — from advancing forward-looking compensation strategies, to strengthening talent pipelines and succession, and embedding a culture of high performance through robust performance management practice; ensuring SGH continues to attract, develop, reward, and retain the talent that underpins its leadership in healthcare. If you are a seasoned HR leader who thrives in complexity, believes deeply in the power of people, and is driven to make a meaningful impact — this is the role for you. What You Will Do You will lead a high-impact portfolio shaping the full employee experience: Reward and Recognition — You will define how SGH values its people. From building a compelling Pay Philosophy to leading annual compensation cycles and overseeing strategic remuneration, to ensure competitiveness, equity and financially sustainability. Talent and Succession — You will build the pipelines that future-proof SGH. Build strong leadership pipelines. Drive institutional talent management and succession planning to ensure readiness for the future. Performance and Growth — You will champion a culture of accountability and excellence. Governing SGH's appraisal and promotion frameworks, you will ensure consistency, integrity, and meaningful recognition. Engagement and Wellbeing — You will Shape the employee experience through data-driven engagement and wellbeing strategies that foster a supportive and energising workplace. HR Operations and Governance — You will Elevate HR service delivery with strong governance, digital enablement, ensuring PDPA compliance, and and sound handling of employee relations and compliance. People Leadership — You will develop and inspire your team while building a strong internal HR talent pipeline the next generation of HR leaders. What Makes This Role Exceptional You will work alongside senior leaders across SGH and the SingHealth cluster, with the mandate and platform to create real impact. In SGH, HR is a strategic partner to the domains in building a resilient, future-ready workforce anchored in a culture of trust, kindness and excellence. What You Bring You are a seasoned HR professional with at least 20 years of experience, including 10 years in a managerial capacity and at least 5 years leading portfolios in compensation and rewards, performance management, talent management or HR operations. You think strategically but execute with precision; analytically sharp, data-literate, and comfortable synthesising complex information into clear, actionable insights. You lead with integrity, handle sensitive matters with the utmost discretion, and bring out the best in the people around you. This will be done through various activities across vaccine development, including vaccine development projects, exploring technology platforms, and clinical trial efforts. Job Summary The Deputy Director will be part of the leadership team of PrepVax. Together with Director PrepVax and incumbent Deputy Director, you would be involved in directing and operationalising the programmes of the PrepVax Office. This includes managing stakeholders and partners, overseeing the team running the administrative aspects of the Office such as project management and Finances, strategic planning of PrepVax’s programmes, and leading the operationalisation of PrepVax’s vaccine development-related activities. You will be working in a dynamic environment that would require the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders at the same time. MAIN DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES SPECIFIC The Deputy Director will: Be involved in leading the planning and implementation of PrepVax’s work; Work together with Director and incumbent Deputy Director PrepVax to lead the shaping, development and implementation of all of PrepVax’s programmes in vaccine development, clinical trials and people development. This would include both strategising overall direction and operationalising the workstreams. Play a leading role in the coordination of various activities to further PrepVax’s work. This includes working with relevant parties such as collaborators, funding agencies, regulators, academia, and biomedical industry partners, from local as well as overseas institutions and agencies; Take on a leading role to guide team in ensuring smooth running of administrative activities from Finance to Secretariat functions and others. This would include multiple party coordination, preparing and clearing meeting agenda and presentation materials, taking/vetting minutes, monitoring and processing of invoice payments, claims, procurement ,and others; GENERAL Any other projects or tasks assigned. JOB REQUIREMENTS A) EDUCATION, TRAINING AND EXPERIENCE Minimum Bachelor’s Degree in preferably regulatory, manufacturing, public health, microbiology, epidemiology, biostatistics, biological, biomedical or chemical sciences, or a related discipline. Advanced/post-graduate degrees preferred. Minimally 10-15 years of relevant experience ideally in a research, healthcare or public health-related environment Experience in running vaccine development projects will be a plus. Specific expertise in Chemistry, Manufacturing and Control, or Clinical Trials would be advantageous. Experience in overseeing handling research budgets and finance. Experience in managing NMRC-awarded grants will be advantageous. At least 5 years of demonstrated experience managing and executing projects independently At least 5 years of demonstrated experience managing people and project teams. Excellent written and oral communication skills. Strong experience in providing guidance to team. Flexibility and ability to work in a fast-paced and fluid working environment, with an ability to engage effectively with all levels of seniority. Meticulous, detail-oriented and responsible. Independent, organized and self-driven. Excellent interpersonal skills, pleasant personality with positive working and learning attitude. Make a meaningful difference whilst growing in a supportive environment that nurtures your wellbeing and career aspirations Job Description: We are looking for a dynamic and experienced leader to join us as Deputy Director, Campus Planning & Development at the Institute of Mental Health (IMH). In this role, you will lead the infrastructure planning and development function, overseeing the full spectrum of facility works from minor renovations to major new developments. A key focus of this role is to coordinate and drive the overall development of IMH and the larger Buangkok Green Medical Park (BGMP), transforming it into a thriving hub for wellness within the community. Key Responsibilities Lead and oversee infrastructure planning and development, covering the full project lifecycle from space planning and master-planning through to construction and completion, ensuring projects are delivered on time, within budget, and to the required quality standards. Lead the overall master-planning of spaces across the campus, ensuring that existing land and new spaces are well planned, optimally utilised, and aligned with the organisation's long-term strategic objectives. This includes overseeing the Campus Development Secretariat, which serves as the key interface between clinical and operational needs and infrastructure planning — translating hospital requirements into actionable development briefs and driving the campus redevelopment of IMH forward. Oversee all physical upgrading works and new developments within IMH land, ensuring seamless execution and minimal disruption to hospital operations. Develop and maintain strong relationships with key stakeholders, including government agencies, consultants, contractors, and internal teams, to facilitate smooth project delivery. Provide strategic advice and recommendations to senior leadership on facility development matters, including space planning, infrastructure upgrades, and sustainability initiatives. Manage and monitor project budgets, procurement processes, and contract administration in accordance with organisational policies. Ensure compliance with all relevant statutory requirements, building codes, and safety regulations throughout the project lifecycle. Lead and develop a team of project management professionals, fostering a culture of excellence, collaboration, and continuous improvement. Requirements: Competency Proven track record in managing large-scale, complex facility or infrastructure development projects, preferably within a healthcare or institutional setting, will be a strong advantage Strong knowledge of construction management, procurement frameworks, and contract administration. Excellent stakeholder management, communication, and leadership skills. Ability to think strategically while managing operational details effectively. Educational and Professional Requirements A degree in Architecture, Civil Engineering, Building, Real Estate, or related discipline. Minimum 15 years of relevant experience in facility development, project management, or a related field, with at least 5 years of leadership capacity. About IMH: The Institute of Mental Health (IMH) in Singapore, established in 1928, is a premier 2,000-bed psychiatric hospital nestled in Buangkok Green Medical Park, providing comprehensive mental health services across 50 inpatient wards and 7 outpatient clinics, while also playing a pivotal role in training the next generation of mental health professionals through the NHG-AHPL Residency Programme and collaborations with local academic institutions. Institution All Family Group All Clear Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Create Alert × Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Apply now » Asst Director, Technology Strategic Planning and Integration Institution: Tan Tock Seng Hospital Family Group: Administration Purpose The Assistant Director supports TTSH's digital transformation agenda by leading the planning, design and implementation of technology-enabled services that enhance patient and workforce experiences. The role is responsible for driving technology and operations informatics initiatives, developing future-state operating models, and ensuring technology investments translate into meaningful operational, service and organisational outcomes. Key Responsibilities 1. Digital Experience and Service Transformation Lead the redesign and transformation of patient, caregiver and staff journeys through technology-enabled services. Develop future-state digital service blueprints and identify opportunities for digitalisation, self-service and process simplification. Drive adoption and optimisation of digital services and platforms to improve accessibility, convenience and user experience. Partner clinical, operational and support services to translate business priorities into transformation roadmaps and initiatives. Collaborate with patient experience units, frontline champions and other stakeholders to ensure services remain user-centred and outcome-driven. 2. Informatics, Smart Systems and Workflow Enablement Lead the planning and design of technology-enabled workflows, smart hospital systems and enterprise digital capabilities. Drive operations informatics initiatives to improve workflow efficiency, resource utilisation, capacity planning and service delivery. Partner stakeholders to define business requirements, future operating models and implementation roadmaps for strategic technology initiatives. Support the implementation and optimisation of enterprise systems, digital technologies, smart systems and workflow enablement solutions. Coordinate technology planning and requirements development for smart hospital initiatives and future healthcare development programmes. 3. Performance Analytics, Benefits Realisation and Continuous Improvement Develop analytics and reporting capabilities to identify service bottlenecks, pain points and transformation opportunities. Leverage operational, service and technology data to support prioritisation and decision-making. Establish performance measurement frameworks and benefits realisation approaches for digital transformation and technology initiatives. Drive continuous improvement through data-driven evaluation of service performance, adoption and operational outcomes. Translate user feedback and operational insights into service enhancements and transformation priorities. 4. Change Management and Stakeholder Engagement Lead change management efforts for digital transformation, operations informatics and technology-enabled service initiatives to ensure successful adoption and sustained outcomes. Coordinate cross-divisional collaboration across operational, clinical, facilities and technology teams. Build and manage relationships with technology vendors, implementation partners and ecosystem stakeholders. Facilitate stakeholder engagement, requirements development, business case formulation and consensus-building for complex transformation programmes. Requirements Bachelor's degree in Healthcare Management, Information Technology, Business, Engineering or a related discipline. Master's degree preferred. Minimum 12-15 years of relevant experience in healthcare operations, digital transformation, technology implementation, service redesign, enterprise systems, smart hospital planning or related fields. Demonstrated experience leading complex cross-functional transformation, technology-enabled change or large-scale operational improvement initiatives. Strong understanding of healthcare operations, workflow redesign, service delivery and change management. Experience working across multidisciplinary stakeholder groups, including operational, clinical, facilities and technology teams. Strong programme management, stakeholder engagement, strategic planning and problem-solving capabilities. Experience in healthcare technology, enterprise systems, operations informatics, smart hospital planning, patient experience improvement or digital service transformation would be advantageous. Institution All Family Group All Clear Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Create Alert × Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Apply now » Deputy Director / Assistant Director, Medical Tower Planning and Development Institution: Tan Tock Seng Hospital Family Group: Administration The HealthCity Novena (HCN) Master Plan was developed by Tan Tock Seng Hospital (TTSH), the flagship tertiary hospital of NHG Health, to transform the 17-hectare campus into Singapore's premier integrated hub for healthcare, education, and research - anchored in the vision of advancing patient-centric care for the 1.5 million residents of Central and North Singapore. Following the successful delivery of Phase 1, Phase 2 was launched on 9 September 2025 and marks the critical intensification of the campus. Phase 2 is anchored by two flagship developments: the new TTSH Medical Tower and the operationalization of the TTSH Pavilion. The hallmark of Phase 2, the TTSH Medical Tower will add approximately 600 acute beds, significantly strengthening TTSH's healthcare capacity for high-intensity, specialist care. It will house an expanded Emergency Department, additional operating theatres, and procedural suites, and specialist outpatient clinics, all purpose-designed to advance frailty-ready and technology-enabled care. In addition, to sustain full operational continuity throughout construction, the former Pavilion wards on campus have been transformed and reactivated as the TTSH Pavilion - a modernized facility comprising specialist clinics, laboratories, and offices. THE ROLE You will lead the technical oversight of the TTSH Medical Tower and related infrastructure developments, ensuring that the developer (MOHH Dev Sec), consultants, and contractors deliver in full accordance with the approved Schedule of Accommodation, technical specifications, and master programme. Operating at the interface of user requirements, construction delivery, and government frameworks, you will support TTSH management on all matters relating to the built environment. KEY RESPONSIBILITIES 1. Design, Construction & Post-Construction Management Lead and direct the full project lifecycle across design, construction, and post-construction phases, ensuring that the developer, consultants, and contractors deliver in accordance with the approved Schedule of Accommodation, technical specifications, and master timeline. Oversee the translation of clinical requirements into engineering and architectural reality. Ensure all MEP, structural, and specialised medical systems - including medical gas, radiation shielding, and infection control infrastructure - meet international healthcare standards and regulatory requirements. Lead the TTSH technical team in monitoring site progress, identifying technical risks, and proposing mitigation strategies to prevent delays and protect the Medical Tower development timeline. Review milestone documents, design submissions, and progress reports, ensuring that all records of decisions and approvals are accurately captured for management sign-off authorities and audit-ready. Manage the technical handover process from construction to operations. Lead joint inspections with users and oversee the defects-rectification process to ensure the facility is fully fit for purpose on Day 1. Administer project contracts in accordance with PSSCOC and relevant government frameworks, including the management of variations, claims, extensions of time, and contractor obligations throughout the project lifecycle. 2. Project Management Co-own the master programme with MOHH, tracking progress against key milestones and proactively identifying risks to schedule, cost, or scope before they escalate to leadership. Lead the project governance structure, including the development secretariat function - ensuring that action logs, variation records, and approval trails are current, attributable, and aligned with MOH/H and cluster policy requirements. Maintain oversight of budget planning, capital grant documentation, and project variations, providing technical justifications and ensuring all submissions meet PSSCOC and government procurement frameworks. 3. Stakeholder Coordination Direct the requirements-gathering process across clinical users, consultants, employer's agents, and contractors, ensuring that operational and technical requirements are captured early and remain aligned through each phase of development. Guide clinical and operational users through the end-to-end planning process, facilitating productive discussions on functional design, medical equipment integration, and facilities maintenance considerations. Act as the authoritative technical voice for TTSH in huddles with MOHH and contractors, ensuring that all technical queries and site variations are resolved promptly and without compromising clinical functionality. 4. Technical Advisory Provide expert guidance to leadership on design decisions, buildability, regulatory compliance, and healthcare planning standards. Stay abreast of emerging healthcare infrastructure trends, technologies, and best practices - including advances in frailty-ready design, technology-enabled care environments, and sustainable building standards - to inform and strengthen TTSH's development direction. Undertake any other responsibilities in support of the hospital development as assigned by leadership. REQUIREMENTS Degree in Architecture, Civil & Structural, Mechanical or Electrical Engineering, Construction Management, or a related built-environment discipline. At least 18-20 years (DD) or 12-15 years (AD) of relevant experience in public sector infrastructure or major healthcare facility development. Must have seen at least one major development project through from design to practical completion. Prior experience on a public hospital project is a distinct advantage. Sound working knowledge of PSSCOC, capital grant frameworks, BCA regulations, and healthcare infrastructure standards. Demonstrated experience in contract administration, including the management of variations, claims, and extensions of time under PSSCOC or equivalent government contract frameworks. Familiarity with medical equipment planning and FFE procurement frameworks as they relate to capital grant justifications. Strong leadership capabilities with the ability to influence, guide, and align diverse stakeholders across clinical, technical, and government domains. Proven ability to co-manage complex programmes with multiple concurrent workstreams, balancing schedule, budget, and quality demands without losing governance rigour. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, with the credibility and composure to represent the institution at senior forums and government engagements. Highly organised, resourceful, and self-directed, with the ability to operate with a high degree of autonomy in a fast-paced and evolving project environment. Institution All Family Group All Clear Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Create Alert × Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Apply now » Group Chief Information Security Officer, Group Data Protection & Governance Officer and Director Institution: National Healthcare Group Family Group: Administration JOB DESCRIPTION Job Role : Group Chief Information Security Officer, Group Data Protection & Governance Officer and Director (Special Projects) Department : NHG Group Digital Health Job Summary The role incumbent is responsible for establishing and maintaining the enterprise vision, strategy and security program to ensure information assets and technologies are adequately protected, and business continuity. This role involves leading the information security team developing security and contingency policies and ensuring compliance with regulatory requirements. The role incumbent will work closely with senior management to align security initiatives with business objectives and manage risk effectively. Role and Responsibilities Develop and implement a comprehensive information security strategy that aligns with the organization’s goals while ensuring compliance with public healthcare IT security policies and standards. Lead and support the information security team, fostering a culture of security awareness among IT staff and end users to keep them informed of security threats and appropriate responses. Own and drive the integration of cybersecurity with business continuity planning (BCP), IT disaster recovery (DR), and operational resilience frameworks , ensuring end-to-end protection of critical clinical, operational, and corporate services for service resiliency. Define, assess, and validate enterprise-wide cyber and technology disruption scenarios (e.g. ransomware, data corruption, system outages, third-party failures) and their downstream impact on patient care, command centres, finance, supply chain, and population health services. Partner clinical, operations, BCM, and technology leaders to design mitigation strategies that preserve essential service delivery during cyber or technology incidents. Ensure incidence response, disaster recovery and crisis management playbook are aligned, tested and executable with input from business and tech team . Conduct thorough risk assessments and manage security incidents, serving as the primary point of contact for all security-related activities and ensuring timely response and recovery. Establish, maintain, and regularly update security policies, standards, and procedures to safeguard the organization’s information assets and ensure compliance with relevant laws and regulations. Collaborate with IT and other departments to integrate security measures into all organizational operations, while conducting compliance checks for new projects and assessing their impact on the overall risk profile. Recommend and implement compensating controls for any deviations from established IT security policies and standards, continuously improving security practices across the organization. Monitor emerging threats and technologies, advising stakeholders on appropriate actions to mitigate risks and adapt the security strategy as necessary . Provide IT security advisory and consulting services to project teams ensuring that security considerations are integrated into all initiatives and projects. Participates and contributes as a member of Senior Management team at C-level, contributing to the organization’s overall strategic initiatives beyond just IT. on overall organisation’s initiatives, not limited to IT only. Extend cybersecurity governance, risk management and assurance framework to new technology e.g. AI enabled systems, and oversee security, resilience and trustworthiness of AI systems e.g. Data poisoning, adverbial threats, in collaboration with industrial partners. Ensure AI systems’ compliance relevant national and cluster framework and requirements and collaborate with external partners to align NHG’s position with best practices. Advise on secure adoption of new digital health technologies and embed secure-by-design and resilient-by-design principles into new technologies programme. Lead future technology risk scanning and planning, including cybersecurity implications of emerging technologies, and assess and prepare the organisation for relevant risk. Requirements / Qualifications Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology or related field. Relevant certifications such as CISA, CISM and/or CISSP are highly desirable. Proven experience in a senior Information Security role, with at least 8 years of IT security experience in IT security infrastructure design and operations, and/or in an IT security compliance and assurance role. Demonstrated experience in business continuity planning, disaster recovery, and operational resilience , preferably in complex, mission-critical environments. Hands-on experience in cybersecurity architecture, governance and systems integration. Experience governing or securing AI, data, or emerging digital health technologies is highly desirable. Ability to translate complex cyber and technology risks into clear business, clinical, and service impact narratives for senior leadership and Boards. Strong knowledge of information security frameworks, risk management and compliance requirements. Proficiency in security technologies and tools. Ability to engage confidently with senior stakeholders and translate technical risks into actionable business recommendations. Ability to work collaboratively across departments and with external partners. Strong analytical skills and attention to details. Strategic thinking and problem-solving abilities. Institution All Family Group All Clear Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Create Alert × Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Apply now » Asst Director, Corporate Partnership Institution: Tan Tock Seng Hospital Family Group: Administration Position Overview The Assistant Director (AD) - Partner Engagement and Marcomms plays a pivotal role in driving TTSH's strategic partnership with key partners such as corporates, insurers, external healthcare providers with a strong focus on General Practitioners (GPs) and oversees marketing communications initiatives for the Corporate Partnership Development team. This position requires a dynamic leader who can collaborate across multiple stakeholders to develop and implement engagement and outreach strategies that support TTSH's long-term growth objectives. Key Responsibilities STRATEGY DEVELOPMENT AND IMPLEMENTATION Supporting the Director and Deputy Director, the AD is expected to develop and co-drive engagement and business strategies in line with long-term growth objectives. The AD is expected to work with stakeholders to strengthen TTSH’s branding and drive business-focused initiatives, ensuring growth and long-term sustainability of the organisation. PARTNER ENGAGEMENT AND OUTREACH The AD will leverage various engagement channels and platforms to cultivate and strengthen strategic relationships with key partners such as external healthcare partners and GPs. This includes conducting active in-person outreach, networking and relationship-building efforts to strengthen trust, understand needs and drive collaboration. He or she will serve as the primary liaison for partner feedback and suggestions, ensuring insights are captured and translated into actionable improvements. The AD will collaborate closely with HQ counterparts to identify, prioritise and engage partners in under-served regions, expanding reach and enhancing inclusivity across the network. Partnering internal stakeholders, the AD will define differentiated value propositions and translate these into clear, compelling messaging. He or she will proactively position and promote relevant existing and/or new services to partners, ensuring alignment with needs and organisational priorities. MARKETING AND COMMUNICATIONS The AD will focus on driving awareness and visibility of TTSH and its services by conceptualising and delivering marketing collaterals that articulate TTSH’s identity, capabilities and differentiated value propositions to partners. He or she will ensure alignment, consistency and effectiveness of messaging across channels and touchpoints. The AD will also lead the planning and delivery of high-impact events, including partner engagements, public forums and external events to enhance visibility and deepen partner relationships. He or she will ensure events are purpose-driven, professionally executed and aligned with organisational priorities. DATA-DRIVEN Process and Referral OPTIMISATION The AD will ensure appropriate business and operational performance metrics tracking and collation, as well as measure the impact of engagement and outreach initiatives; he or she will work with HQ to analyse referral patterns, identify gaps and drive improvements in partner engagement. Separately utilising partner feedback, data and performance metrics, the AD will work closely with the partner concierge unit to improve partner experience and ensure a seamless, efficient and differentiated concierge experience. SERVICE INNOVATION AND DEVELOPMENT Through the course of partner engagement, the AD should ideally identify unmet clinical and patient needs through partners and stakeholder engagement, data analysis and market research. He or she would work closely with internal stakeholders to focus on developing differentiated service offerings that drive patient value and enhance TTSH’s competitive positioning. The AD should also lead the team to embark on periodic market scanning and research. Required Qualifications Bachelor's degree in Marketing, Communications, Business Administration, Healthcare Management, or related field Minimum 8-10 years of progressive experience in product development, service innovation, or healthcare management Proven track record in identifying market opportunities and scaling innovative solutions Strong experience in cross-functional collaboration and stakeholder management Demonstrated ability in data analysis and performance optimisation Excellent written and verbal communication skills Experience in healthcare service design and patient experience improvement Confident in engaging clinicians and GPs Apply now » Find similar jobs: TTSH, Corporate Functions Main Home Page Terms of Use Personal Data Protection Act (PDPA) Opens in a new tab. Institution All Job Function All Job ID All Employment Type All Clear Search Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Create Alert × Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Apply now » Head, Business Development, National University 6G Artificial Intelligence Centre (NXAC) (Contract) Job ID: 10242 Job Function: Administration Institution: National University Health System About NXAC NXAC is a newly established research and translation centre under the National University Health System (NUHS. The Centre brings together clinicians, engineers, and research scientists to advance three converging frontiers — Advanced Connectivity, Spatial AI, Embodied AI — and to translate them into deployable solutions. NXAC has been designed from the outset for translation, with an operating model that bridges academic research and commercial deployment so that discoveries can move rapidly from laboratory to clinic to market, both within NUHS and through partner health systems regionally and internationally. NXAC is seeking an accomplished and entrepreneurial Head for Business Development to lead the Centre’s translational strategy (both internal and external), build and convert a portfolio of high-value partnerships, and establish the revenue and translation pathways that will underpin NXAC’s long-term sustainability and impact. This is a senior, founding-team role for a commercially astute leader who can translate deep-technology research into compelling propositions for partners, industry, investors, and health systems. The successful candidate will shape how a pioneering healthcare deep-technology centre takes its work to market, and will play a central part in positioning NXAC as a global reference centre for industry collaboration in spatial AI, connectivity, and adaptive robotics for healthcare. Key Responsibilities Business development strategy and pipeline Develop and execute the Centre’s business development strategy across its research domains, aligned with NUHS and NXAC’s mission, governance, and sustainability objectives. Assist the Director of NXAC to maintain project continuity and stakeholder engagement within NUHS Build, qualify, and manage a structured pipeline of industry, investor, and institutional opportunities, maintaining rigorous pipeline discipline and reporting. Define and refine the Centre’s commercial offerings, including sponsored and collaborative research, consultancy, testbed and facility access, intellectual property licensing, and training. Able to hold strategic level conversations with NXAC partners and maintain relationships within the ecosystem Lead the business development team to accomplish the objectives of NXAC Career development and strategic development of the business development team in NXAC Partnerships and industry engagement Originate, negotiate, and close strategic partnerships, master research agreements, and commercial engagements with corporate partners across the telecommunications, medical technology, robotics, and cloud and artificial intelligence sectors. Cultivate and steward senior relationships with industry, government agencies, investors, and partner institutions in Singapore and internationally. Support an industry advisory board to guide the Centre’s commercial direction and deepen its ecosystem. Represent the NXAC Director at high level engagements when required Represent the NXAC Director for conferences and meetings both local and international when required. Commercialisation and translation Work closely with researchers, clinicians, and the technology transfer function to identify commercially promising outputs and shape their route to market. Support the protection and management of intellectual property and contribute to the establishment of spinouts, joint ventures, and licensing arrangements through the Centre’s commercial structure. Develop evaluation and pilot agreements with health systems and partners that demonstrate real-world impact and create reference deployments. Possess essential concepts on how these projects and ideas can be translated from bench research into clinical and operational implementation in the healthcare setting. They should be proficient in principles and concepts of advanced connectivity, spatial AI, and embodied AI Funding and grants Identify and pursue co-funded and industry-leveraged grant opportunities with national and international funding bodies, partnering with principal investigators on commercially oriented proposals. Strengthen the Centre’s funding diversification and reduce reliance on any single source of income. Stakeholder management and governance Prepare and present business development plans, pipeline reviews, and performance reports to the Executive Director, Board, and key stakeholders. Ensure that all engagements are conducted in accordance with the Centre’s governance, legal, financial, and ethical requirements, working closely with institutional legal, finance, and compliance teams. Requirements Significant (at least 8 years) senior business development, commercial, or partnerships experience, with a demonstrable track record of originating and closing complex, high-value, multi-stakeholder agreements. Substantial experience in one or more relevant sectors, such as healthcare technology, medical devices, deep technology, telecommunications, robotics, or artificial intelligence. A sound understanding of intellectual property, technology transfer, and the commercialisation of research, including licensing, spin-offs, and joint ventures. Excellent commercial judgement, negotiation, and deal-structuring capabilities, combined with strong financial literacy. Outstanding communication, influencing, and relationship-management skills, with the executive presence to engage credibly with senior industry, clinical, academic, and government leaders. The drive, adaptability, and resourcefulness to thrive in an early-stage, founding-team environment. An established network across the healthcare, deep-technology, telecommunications, or investment communities in Singapore and the wider region. Familiarity with requirements from academia, clinical practice, and industry perspectives. Ideally someone who has had years of experience interfacing with researchers, clinicians, and industry partners. Familiarity with public research funding and grant mechanisms. Experience with international business development and cross-border collaboration. Key Competencies Strategic and commercial acumen Relationship building and stakeholder influence Negotiation and deal-making Results orientation and pipeline discipline Entrepreneurial initiative and comfort with ambiguity Collaboration and the ability to work effectively with scientific and clinical teams Integrity and sound governance judgement Apply now » Find similar jobs: Administration Corporate Site View All Jobs Contact Us Opens in a new tab. The role will play a crucial role spearheading clinical trial activity in NUHS, enhancing not only the cluster’s standing but Singapore reputation as a premier site for clinical trials in alignment with the national strategy. Institution All Job Function All Job ID All Employment Type All Clear Search Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Create Alert × Select how often (in days) to receive an alert: Apply now » Director, Business Operations and Go-to-Market Job ID: 9175 Job Function: Administration Institution: National University Health System Job Title: Director, Business Operations and Go-to-Market (BizOps & GTM) Reports to: Chief Executive Officer (CEO) Role Overview We are seeking a high-capacity operator and strategic leader to join our leadership team as the Director of Business Operations & Go-to-Market. Reporting directly to the CEO, this hybrid role integrates company-level business operations, commercial execution, and health-system AI adoption leadership—ensuring that EndeavourX’s products achieve real-world clinical and operational impact across NUHS and external clients. This is a pivotal role for a fast-growing AI spinout. You will: • Drive operational excellence across multiple functions. • Lead cross-functional GTM strategy and execution. • Oversee AI workflow integration, adoption, and benefits realisation with health-system partners. • Act as an organisational force multiplier, building structure, cadence, and accountability as the company scales. The role will suit a strategic thinker with strong stakeholder management, a builder’s mindset, and exceptional ability to execute in a dynamic startup–enterprise hybrid environment. As the company grows, this role offers a pathway toward expanded senior leadership responsibilities. Key Responsibilities 1. Business Operations (BizOps) Strategic & Cross-Functional Operations • Partner with CEO/COO to integrate day-to-day operations across legal, finance, HR, product, clinical, data science, and customer-facing teams. • Establish and lead the BizOps function, including OKR setting, quarterly business reviews, operating rhythms, and cross-functional execution cadences. • Develop and standardise company-wide processes to support internal scaling (hiring, onboarding, budgeting, resource planning), and external scaling (product deployments, customer engagements). Organisational Structure & Leadership • Build operating models to align clinical, technical, and commercial teams for rapid AI use-case rollout. • Maintain executive-ready reporting for the CEO and COO, including Board materials, management accounts summaries, performance dashboards, and KPI packs. 2. Go-to-Market (GTM) Strategy & Execution GTM Strategy & Commercial Engine Building • Lead strategic initiatives and special projects from the CEO/COO, including major accounts, partnership structuring, and pricing strategy. • Develop and refine GTM strategy across segments (public healthcare, private hospitals, regional markets), aligning commercial pipeline with product and clinical priorities. • Define customer segmentation, ICPs, commercial channels, and expansion roadmaps. Execution & External Engagement • Own execution of GTM plans, including negotiations, contracting, procurement cycles, and stakeholder management. • Partner closely with Sales & Customer Success teams to accelerate pipeline progression, customer onboarding, and product usage. 3. Sales & Customer Success Oversight (As Required) (Especially during VP Sales & CS maternity leave in 1H 2026) • Provide interim leadership of Sales & CS, ensuring continuity of day-to-day sales operations, reporting, pipeline hygiene, and customer engagement. • Mentor junior team members to maintain delivery reliability during the transition period. 4. AI Workflow Integration, Adoption & Scaling This Director will co-lead, with the CEO/COO, the operationalisation of AI use cases across clinical and operational settings. Workflow Integration & Change Management • Engage clinical, nursing, operations, and allied health teams to map current vs future workflows for AI-enabled processes. • Lead workflow redesign efforts to integrate AI tools into patient-care and administrative pathways. • Coordinate closely with project technical leads and PMOs on technology development, integration, and Epic/downstream system alignment. • Ensure implementations meet clinical governance standards and regulatory expectations. Adoption, Implementation & Scaling • Lead institutional rollout of AI use cases: pilots, onboarding, adoption cycles, and scale-up. • Develop implementation playbooks, SOPs, and site-specific operational guides. • Align adoption activities with enterprise-level programmes within health systems, ensuring EndeavourX products land firmly in operational practice. ⸻ 5. Benefits Realisation & KPIs • Support development of benefits cases (throughput gains, staff time saved, clinical efficiency, safety outcomes). • Define and track adoption metrics, usage analytics, benefit realisation, and operational KPIs for AI deployments. • Work with health system PMOs and EndeavourX leadership to feed results into portfolio reporting, quarterly business reviews, and Board updates. • Use metrics to continually refine commercial positioning and customer success narratives. 6. Stakeholder Management • Serve as the connective tissue across clinical, operational, IT, innovation, and leadership stakeholders in NUHS and external clients. • Represent EndeavourX in working groups, customer governance forums, and transformation programmes. • Provide structured updates to PMOs or executive steering committees as needed. • Build trusted senior-level relationships to drive alignment and unblock issues swiftly. Qualifications & Experience • 7–10 years of experience in business operations, GTM, strategy, project management, or healthcare transformation roles in health tech, SaaS, or regulated industries. • Proven experience in startups, scale-ups, or corporate spinouts. • Track record in leading cross-functional initiatives and large-scale operational rollouts. • Excellent communicator with strong senior-stakeholder management instincts. • Experience with healthcare systems, public sector environments, or regulated technology is advantageous. • Familiarity with AI-enabled workflow redesign, digital transformation, or implementation science is a strong plus. jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, Supply Chain and Economic Resilience Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 5 Jun 2026. Closing on 30 Sep 2026. With the shifts in the global order and deepening geopolitical tensions, we live in a world which is less predictable and secure. In recent years, global supply chains are increasingly being scrutinised for their ability to withstand shocks. Countries are reconfiguring trade networks and supply chains in the name of resilience and security. As a small and open economy with limited natural resources, Singapore needs to be prepared and reacts nimbly in the face of such changes . The Supply Chain and Economic Resilience Division (SCERD) is responsible for ensuring economic resilience and the resilience of Singapore’s critical supply chains. SCERD also oversees building and institutionalising resilience, operational preparedness and crisis management capabilities in the Ministry of Trade and Industry. In this role, you will: • Develop national and sectoral strategies to secure the resilience of Singapore’s supply chains for essential and critical goods and services, and to mitigate and withstand impact from external shocks; • Analyse and synthesize findings on critical sector supply chains, challenges and needs; • Build the division’s collaboration network with key stakeholders across the Government, industry and academia; • Strengthen supply chain intelligence and analysis capabilities. Requirements • Excellent writing, communication, interpersonal and presentation skills; • Able to work independently as well as in teams, and across multiple workstreams; • Aptitude for policy design, systems and analytical thinking, in order to deal with complex problems and recommend actionable solutions; • Possess a driven and self-starter attitude as well as resourcefulness in solving problems; • Good stakeholder management skills dealing both with internal and external stakeholders; and • Data analysis skills including data visualisation and the ability to extract insights from complex datasets.

jobs.title: Deputy Director, Liquidity & Asset-Liability Mgmt Risk Specialist (Contract)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 20 Jul 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. Deputy Director, Liquidity & Asset-Liability Management Risk Specialist (1-Year Contract) The Financial Risk Specialist Division (Banking Department II) is recruiting a specialist to support MAS’ supervision of financial institutions’ liquidity and asset-liability management risks. This position entails evaluating financial institutions’ approaches towards liquidity risk and asset-liability management, including their methodologies and models. You are expected to conduct market surveillance, research the latest risk management techniques, and develop supervisory policies / guidelines to financial institutions in Singapore. Relevant experience in liquidity and asset-liability risk management functions with a financial institution, or relevant degree in Quantitative Finance. Postgraduate degree in those fields or candidates with professional qualifications like CFA, PRM or FRM preferred. Understanding of liquidity risk management processes and interest rate risk management. Highly analytical, quantitative and possess good working knowledge of data analytical tools, with the ability to evaluate financial institutions’ methodologies and models, and to leverage data and analytics to derive risk insights Good and concise writing skills with the ability to translate technical reports into supervisory risk implications. Ability to drill down to details rigorously, as well as identify broad trends and issues proactively Natural curiosity and energetic approach to problem solving and able to bring in fresh perspectives Strong team player, and willingness to go the extra mile when necessary All applicants will be notified on whether they are shortlisted or not within 4 weeks of the closing date of this job posting.

jobs.title: Assistant Director/Senior Manager (Longitudinal Studies), SIR/RA
jobs.inst: Ministry Of Social And Family Development
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 9 Jul 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. The Strategy, International & Research (SIR) Directorate oversees and drives MSF’s strategic planning, transformation, international engagement, as well as research and analytics efforts to achieve MSF’s strategic outcomes. The Research and Analytics Division drives MSF’s research and analytics efforts, to strengthen the nexus between policy, practice, operations, research and analytics to support MSF outcomes, policies and programmes. Our work entails coordinating the social research agenda, conducting research and data analytics studies, and building research expertise for MSF. As an Assistant Director/Senior Manager (Longitudinal Studies), you will play a key role in advancing MSF’s longitudinal research agenda, leveraging multi-wave survey and administrative data to generate insights on social mobility, family outcomes and long-term trajectories, and translating these into policy and programme improvements. • Contribute to shaping MSF’s research agenda on social mobility outcomes, with a focus on longitudinal evidence building • Oversee the design, implementation and enhancement of MSF’s flagship longitudinal panel survey, including questionnaire design, fieldwork and sampling strategy, data quality and knowledge management. • Conduct and lead longitudinal data analysis to study trajectories and transitions of families over time, and translate analytical findings into policy-relevant insights • Partner internal divisions, Whole-of-Government and external partners to co-develop research studies and translate findings into policy implications and programme improvements • Strong in quantitative methods and application, and proficient in at least one statistical/ data analysis software (e.g., R, Python, SPSS, STATA) • Strong analytical thinking and communication skills • Strong project management and stakeholder management skills • Experience with longitudinal/panel datasets and analysis would be an advantage • Familiarity with social policies and social mobility issues would be an advantage

jobs.title: Assistant Director, Service Planning, Customer eXperience Department
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 19 Jun 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. As an Assistant Director in the Customer eXperience Department (CXD), you will play a central role in shaping and advancing MOM's service excellence agenda. You will ensure that MOM's service vision, strategies, and plans keep pace with customers’ evolving needs and deliver meaningful impact in a resource sustainable manner. • Service Strategy and Transformation You will lead efforts to keep MOM's service vision and delivery strategies relevant and forward-looking, and translate service goals into actionable plans. This includes devising service differentiation strategies, rallying leadership and divisional support to drive implementation, and overseeing CXD's annual work plan formulation. • Service Performance Management You will review, design and monitor key service metrics to track progress in achieving key service outcomes, work with divisions to formulate effective action plans and provide regular performance analysis and updates to departments and senior management. • Customer Survey and Qualitative Studies You will lead efforts to design and oversee implementation of surveys and qualitative studies to better understand customer needs and their service journeys with MOM. You should have strong analytical and critical/systems thinking skills to translate research findings and data into actionable insights and recommendations. Strong leadership skills with the ability to coach and develop teams are essential, and you should be resilient and comfortable navigating ambiguity in a dynamic environment.

jobs.title: Assistant Director, Finance Management, ACE Group
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 16 Jun 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). We are looking for a meticulous and service-oriented finance professional to join the Finance Management function in ACE Group, Ministry of Manpower (MOM). The officer will lead revenue collection, accounts receivable management and revenue contracting for ACE Group’s Onboard Centres and Recreation Centres, including the drafting and administration of tenancy agreements. The role also provides oversight of arrears management and compliance with government finance requirements, and drives process improvements through SOP development, automation and technology-enabled solutions. • Lead and oversee revenue collection and revenue contracting for Onboard Centres and Recreation Centres, including the drafting and administration of tenancy agreements, arrears monitoring and follow-up on outstanding payments. • Provide oversight of reconciliation checks, transaction reviews and regular reporting on revenue collection and arrears. • Recommend appropriate actions for outstanding payments, late payment interest, write-offs, waivers and customer appeals. • Engage internal and external stakeholders to resolve billing, fee and payment-related issues, and maintain high service standards. • Strengthen governance and compliance with prevailing government policies, MOM requirements and internal guidelines. • Drive process improvements through SOP development, process reviews, automation and technology-enabled solutions. • Tertiary qualification in Accounting, Business Administration, Finance or a related discipline is preferred. • Relevant experience in finance, revenue management, accounts receivable, contract administration or related functions, preferably with exposure to public sector finance, governance and compliance requirements. • Strong analytical, problem-solving and judgement skills, with the ability to assess arrears, appeals, waivers, write-offs and related financial matters. • Good stakeholder management and communication skills, with the confidence to engage internal and external parties on billing, payment, tenancy and compliance matters. • Meticulous, organised and able to manage multiple priorities, deadlines and operational issues independently. • Proficient in written communication, presentation and IT skills, with an interest in process improvement, automation and technology-enabled solutions.

jobs.title: Deputy/Assistant Director, Infrastructure Finance
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 30 Jun 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. Asia's infrastructure financing needs are large and growing, driven by urbanisation, the energy transition, and digitalisation. Meeting these needs requires mobilising significant private capital from institutional investors and infrastructure asset managers seeking long-term opportunities, alongside concessional funding from multilateral development banks and development finance institutions (MDB/DFIs). You will be part of a team that develops and executes strategies to: i. Deepen Singapore’s position as a hub for infrastructure asset management, project finance, and capital markets activity serving Asia; ii. Strengthen engagement with institutional investors, infrastructure asset managers and MDB/DFIs to anchor capabilities and originate new activity in Singapore; iii. Catalyse blended finance structures and expand the use of risk mitigation instruments to de-risk infrastructure projects and crowd in private capital; and iv. Drive product innovation to address market gaps and unlock new investment opportunities. Monitor global infrastructure financing trends, identify gaps in the ecosystem and develop initiatives to grow Singapore’s role as a regional hub; Design and develop blended finance and risk-sharing structures (e.g. guarantees) to improve project bankability and crowd in private capital; Engage infrastructure asset managers, banks, MDB/DFIs, and project developers to co-create initiatives and anchor capabilities in Singapore; Support FAST-P, a Singapore-led blended finance platform, working with global public, private and philanthropic partners to mobilise capital for Asia’s green and transition infrastructure; and Promote Singapore as a leading infrastructure financing hub to global partners and represent MAS in international forums to shape regional market and policy developments. Strong ability to synthesise complex market, financial and policy developments into clear strategies and actionable recommendations Strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to operate across policy, commercial and developmental contexts, as well as to engage senior counterparts across financial institutions, MDB/DFIs, government and international organisations Comfortable working in ambiguity and driving initiatives from concept to execution Prior experience in project finance, asset management, or a multilateral institution is an advantage You will be working in a fast-paced environment that would require the ability to manage multiple priorities and needs of stakeholders, as well as the agility to respond to changes and developments. As part of the shortlisting process for this role, you may be required to complete a medical declaration and/or undergo further assessment. All applicants will be notified on whether they are shortlisted or not within 4 weeks of the closing date of this job posting.

jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, Innovation, Research and Development Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 3 Jul 2026. Closing on 30 Sep 2026. As Singapore’s constraints in manpower, land, energy and carbon continue to tighten, delivering economic impact with our R&D strategies becomes more important than ever. Having deep technology advantage will enable us to double down on our strengths to strive for global leadership in key growth sectors such as advanced manufacturing and pursue emerging growth areas such as space technology that can push the frontier of our economy. The end goal is to enable our enterprises to remain competitive and create good jobs for our people. As we embark on the new Research, Innovation and Enterprise (RIE) 2030 tranche, MTI’s Innovation, Research and Development Division shapes this phase of Singapore’s development as an innovation-driven economy, by driving new policies and strategies to enhance research and innovation among the business and research communities. You will be part of the team that works on: • A*STAR deskhead. Formulate and oversee the implementation of R&D and corporate strategies for A*STAR. This includes working with A*STAR and relevant stakeholders to maximise value capture, ensuring that A*STAR is sufficiently resourced for the future, and measuring, tracking and communicating the success of A*STAR’s initiatives. • National Space Agency of Singapore (NSAS) deskhead. Formulate and oversee the implementation of Singapore’s space strategy as well as NSAS’ corporate workplans. This includes working with NSAS and relevant stakeholders to ensure NSAS is sufficiently resourced to achieve their mandate, and measuring, tracking and communicating the success of NSAS’ initiatives. • Innovation & enterprise. Identify, develop and implement emerging deep tech sectors where Singapore has a right to play. Monitor and review overall innovation & enterprise landscape in Singapore. Track implementation and review progress of initiatives funded through RIE2030. Working with agencies in reviewing and refining policies to strengthen Singapore’s position as an innovation and IP hub. • Manufacturing, Trade and Connectivity Domain. Monitor and review progress and achievements of initiatives funded through the domain, work with economic agencies to develop sectoral R&D plans, evaluate R&D programmes and resourcing requirements, and apply appropriate policy levers to align the plans and activities of research performers with desired policy outcomes. • International relations. Further MTI’s science, technology and innovation agenda internationally through bilateral/multilateral engagements. This includes engaging in international science and technology platforms (e.g. G20, ASEAN), to find areas of concrete collaboration and broaden global thought leadership, while being sensitive to geopolitical considerations. • Experience or interest in policy formulation, scheme. design or project management. A background in, or familiarity with Science & Technology, will be an advantage. • Excellent analytical skills. • Excellent writing, presentation and communication skills. • Excellent interpersonal and stakeholder management skills.
