jobs.title: Deputy Director (Chief Information Security Office & Governance)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 24 Jun 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). You are responsible for leading and coordinating strategic initiatives to enhance the operational readiness and efficiency of MINDEF/SAF in the realm of cybersecurity. - Provide leadership for the Ministry and its agencies within the cybersecurity domain through the development of appropriate cybersecurity strategies and action plans - Establish policies, standards and procedures in line with Whole-of-Government (WOG) cybersecurity directions - Conduct cybersecurity risk assessment and risk acceptance across the organisation, from stakeholders to end-users and Infocomm Technology (ICT) professionals - Review, endorse and align information security risk management and mitigation plans - Align IT needs with the strategic cybersecurity direction of the whole-of-government - Implement change management processes to keep up with evolving cyber threat landscape Challenge(s) - Balancing MINDEF/SAF internal policy requirements with whole-of-government policy direction - Integrating multi-agency governance, risk and compliance cybersecurity plans and align with WOG - Education in Information Security, Computer Science or Electrical/Electronic/Computer Engineering-related fields - At least 15 years of experience in related areas such as cybersecurity governance, risk management and compliance - Strong critical thinking, analytical, problem-solving, interpersonal skills - Good understanding of both cybersecurity and operations processes - Possession of cybersecurity certifications (i.e., CISSP, CISA and CRISC) is advantageous Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Exit Management)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 26 Jun 2026. Closing on 26 Jul 2026. You lead a team and serve as the Subject Matter Expert (SME) on exit management matters for military regulars and defence executive personnel. You will be responsible for the end-to-end management of exit processes including release, resignation, end-of-term, discharge, second career transition, and employment matters for personnel across all services. You also drive continuous improvement of HR processes and systems to enhance service quality and operational efficiency. - Lead daily operations and ensure smooth execution of all exit management functions - Serve as the primary SME providing specialist advice on exit management matters to Department Heads, Service Personnel Departments, and SAF units - Manage and review system processes related to retirement, resignation, end-of-term, discharge, and career transition programmes - Prepare and submit monthly retirement and release cases for approval - Oversee the accurate maintenance of exit administration data for management reporting and KPI monitoring - Facilitate timely disbursement of release benefits and ensure exit liabilities and overpayments are properly transmitted to payment agencies - Serve as the primary point of contact for exit-related audits, including drafting responses to audit observations - Assess, motivate, and develop staff to achieve optimal performance and organisational excellence Challenge(s) - Managing a wide spectrum of HR administrative responsibilities while balancing process reviews and IT system enhancements, ensuring daily operations continue seamlessly - Navigating a high volume of exit cases with discernment and prioritising high-impact cases to meet service quality targets - Driving mindset transformation and cultivating a customer-centric culture across a diverse team while managing the expectations of a broad range of stakeholders - Exercising tact and diplomacy when communicating with Senior Commanders, Department Heads, and counterparts across Services and agencies - Keeping abreast of policy developments to respond effectively to novel situations and ensure compliance across all exit management functions - Education in Business Administration and Human Resource is advantageous - At least 6 years of experience in HR management - Proven leadership ability with experience managing teams and driving organisational performance - Strong stakeholder management and interpersonal skills, with the ability to engage effectively across all levels - Meticulous and process-oriented, with a keen eye for compliance and service quality - Familiarity with HR systems and experience working with IT and system stakeholders is advantageous Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (IT Infrastructure) - DIS
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 26 Jun 2026. Closing on 26 Jul 2026. You are responsible for the management of information technology (IT) infrastructure projects and strengthening the infrastructure architecture to enhance availability, scalability, security and maintenance. - Provide dedicated focus for the development and delivery of IT infrastructure and other related projects to enhance connectivity, UX, UI and productivity - Strengthen the IT infrastructure architecture to enhance availability, scalability, cross-compatibility, maintenance and security - Develop IT infrastructure analytic tools to support decision-making for optimising the management of OSIT infrastructure - Manage and work with various project teams to ensure delivery and roll-out of projects within the approved budget and schedule Challenge(s) - Working with different IT Subject Matter Experts (SMEs) across intra and inter-ministry level - Education in IT or related disciplines - At least 6 years of prior IT project-management experience - Ability to work independently and communicate across multiple agencies within MINDEF/SAF as well as across Whole-of-Government Ministries and Statutory Boards - Ability to work well in a fast-paced environment Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Deputy Director, Operational Excellence (2 year contract)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 26 Jun 2026. Closing in 4 day(s). The Health Promotion Board’s vision is to make Singapore a nation of healthier people. Come be a part of this journey if you’re passionate about creating boundary-pushing work that drives behavioral change. You will be a part of HPB’s Operational Excellence team, which strives to transform the way the organisation works — driving meaningful improvements across business processes, programme audit management, programme effective and evaluation frameworks, and inventory management to build a more efficient, accountable, and future-ready organisation. Reporting to the Director (Risk Management, Operational Excellence and Compliance), you will be at the forefront of driving meaningful, organisation-wide transformation — shaping how the organisation designs its processes, governs its programmes, manages its contracts, and evaluates its outcomes. This is a rare opportunity to build a culture of accountability and continuous improvement that enables the organisation to deliver quality services sustainably and at scale. As Deputy Director (Operational Excellence), you will work closely with Senior Management and divisional leaders to lead end-to-end assessments of business processes, bringing thought leadership and best practices to redesign workflows, establish meaningful performance indicators, and embed continuous improvement mechanisms that deliver measurable gains in service quality and operational efficiency. You will spearhead the development of a centralised programme audit management capability — collaborating with stakeholders across divisions to design robust governance frameworks, standardised methodologies, and innovative digital solutions that elevate audit rigour and consistency organisation-wide. You will be a key advisor to Senior Management and internal stakeholders on Terms of Reference and contract management practices — establishing clear accountability structures, performance tracking systems, and governance protocols that strengthen cross-functional delivery and protect organisational interests. You will shape how the organisation measures success by championing a rigorous, evidence-based approach to programme evaluation — co-designing standardised methodologies and holistic performance measures that empower divisions to assess outcomes consistently and make well-informed decisions. As a trusted change leader, you will guide Senior Management and divisions through implementation across all workstreams — building lasting organisational capability through training and knowledge transfer, and ensuring that every improvement, including in areas such as inventory management, is practical, sustainable, and delivers real impact where it matters most. An ideal candidate will be a dynamic and self-motivated individual with: Education: Bachelor’s degree in business administration, Industrial Engineering, or a related field. PMP certification and Lean Six Sigma Black Belt certification are highly desirable. Professional Experience: Minimum 10 years of hands-on experience in business transformation, process reengineering, project management, and operational excellence. Experience in service sectors, particularly healthcare or public service, is advantageous. Track Record: Demonstrable success in applying operational excellence and project management methodologies to deliver measurable improvements in efficiency, cost, and service quality within complex matrix organisations. Proven track record of influencing and engaging stakeholders at all levels of an organisation. Project Management: Strong skills in overseeing complex, multi-stakeholder initiatives in fast-paced environments. Proficiency in various project management methodologies (e.g., Agile, Waterfall, Hybrid). The candidate should also possess: Collaborative and consultative approach - ability to partner with stakeholders while respecting divisional ownership Strong analytical mindset - ability to interpret complex data

jobs.title: Manager/Assistant Director (Compensation & Benefits)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 25 Jun 2026. Closing in 6 day(s). The Human Resource Team in MND works closely with its Family of Statutory Boards (of close to 10,000 in staff strength) to support MND Family-wide objectives and strategic priorities through a range of HR initiatives, across several spectrums of HR, such as talent management, key learning focus areas, and other HR policies. Within MND, it supports the manpower needs of the organisation, as well as the growth and development of officers. As Manager/Assistant Director, Compensation & Benefits, you will be responsible to review the compensation and benefits frameworks (e.g. salary structures) within the MND Family, to ensure that we remain competitive in attracting and retaining core professions to support our mission. Part of the work requires you to review compensation and benefits proposals submitted by the Statutory Boards and formulate recommendations to Senior Management, balancing considerations at the broader level. You will also be required to manage and work with the different stakeholders, i.e., PSD and SBs, to manage salary reviews, if any, including the annual bonus payment cycles. • Candidates with at least 3 – 5 years of work experience in HR, with at least 1 year of relevant experience in the Total Rewards domain • Possess strong stakeholder management skills, with the ability to work and engage stakeholders from different agencies independently, as well as explain complex compensation concepts in clear and simple terms to management. • Strong project management and critical thinking skills • Analytical and detail-oriented, able to handle sensitive data with confidentiality

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Contract Management) - Kranji Camp
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 30 Jun 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. You are responsible for the end-to-end contract management of training-related contracts within the Training Integrated Workforce Branch (TIB). You will drive contract renewals, manage contractor performance, and work with stakeholders to establish new contracts for emerging training requirements. - Review contracting approaches, pricing mechanisms and Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) prior to contract renewal, consult users on service changes, and prepare the Contract Review Report (CRR) - Staff all training-related contracting papers to seek approval for contract renewal establishment from relevant outsourcing forums - Prepare for tenders, complete tender evaluation reports, and ensure all relevant tender documents are endorsed at the appropriate levels - Conduct reviews of contractor-provided services, gather user inputs on contractor performance and perform trend analysis to identify potential gaps in KPIs and service delivery - Manage changes to the Scope of Work (SOW) and KPIs, ensure all changes are deliberated, approved in a timely manner - Monitor and verify all KPIs to ensure timely payment to contractors, identify breaches or non-compliance, and ensure contractors adhere to stipulated KPIs - Manage, mitigate and resolve ground issues arising between contractors and users to ensure service continuity Challenge(s) - Maintaining neutrality while facilitating discussions among diverse stakeholders with competing priorities, to arrive at solutions that best meet all parties' needs - Establishing reasonable and balanced KPIs that deliver fair outcomes for both contractors and the organisation - Education in any discipline - At least 6 years of relevant experience in contract management or the handling of tenders - Strong understanding of contract management principles, procurement frameworks, and performance monitoring - Good stakeholder management, communication and negotiation skills - Ability to analyse KPI trends, identify service gaps, and recommend improvements - Ability to manage relationships across users, contractors and oversight bodies Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Training & Doctrine) - Kranji
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 30 Jun 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. You are responsible for maintaining and developing Combat Service Support (CSS) doctrine at the Division and above-level, serving as the custodian of the CSS Knowledge Tree to ensure all doctrine content remains current, relevant, and well-integrated across services and Army Divisions. - Translate new CSS operating concepts into CSS doctrines for Division and above-level CSS operations, ensuring promulgation and adoption across services and Army Divisions - Direct and co-ordinate the review of existing CSS doctrines with relevant Subject Matter Experts (SMEs), drawing on gaps identified during major exercises and training - Ensure the relevancy and integrity of the CSS Knowledge Tree - Leverage technology, including Artificial Intelligence (AI), to streamline and document doctrinal processes, knowledge management, and the development of Army CSS doctrine Challenge(s) - Navigating the complexity of translating evolving CSS operating concepts into clear, actionable doctrine that is relevant and applicable across diverse services and Army Divisions - Education in any field - At least 6 years of prior experience in a Doctrine, Training, or Operations appointment, particularly in areas of Logistics operations, is preferred - Proficiency in staff writing, research, and presentation skills - Strong critical thinking with the ability to communicate with influence Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (OSIT Logistics Operations)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 30 Jun 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. You are responsible for leading the Operations Support IT (OSIT) Logistics Operations Section in developing and implementing comprehensive logistics plans for OSIT operations. You will oversee inventory management, client deployment across MINDEF/SAF units, and the effective management of IT Concierge infrastructure. - Lead the development and implementation of integrated logistics plans for OSIT operations, driving process redesign and enhancing efficiency through stakeholder engagement, demand forecasting, and inventory management and accountability - Oversee end-to-end planning, coordination, and execution of client deployments, including post-deployment support and strategy refinement based on feedback - Manage the full lifecycle of IT Concierge infrastructure and OSIT equipment, including installation, maintenance, decommissioning, and compliance with security and documentation standards - Implement efficient procurement procedures and resolve discrepancies to ensure timely supplier payments - Provide strategic leadership, foster accountability, and lead cross-functional initiatives to improve processes and service delivery Challenge(s) - Managing high-volume, time-sensitive logistics across multiple concurrent OSIT refresh operations while effectively coordinating diverse stakeholders and balancing competing priorities under resource constraints - Maintaining a careful balance between governance and compliance and operational urgency, ensuring that critical requirements are met efficiently without compromising on standards or accountability - Education in Logistics Management, Supply Chain Management, Operations and Logistics, or a related discipline is preferred - At least 6 years of working experience in logistics-related areas - Proficient in managing complex, multi-stakeholder projects with competing priorities and timelines - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with keen attention to detail and a commitment to excellence - Strong leadership and decision-making ability - Good interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build trust and influence stakeholders at all levels. - Skilled in negotiation, stakeholder engagement, and dispute resolution. - Effective steward of resources Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director (Industry Ecosystem), Sector Policy and Planning
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 30 Jun 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. You will work closely with our statutory boards and across Government to design and implement policies and strategies to enhance Singapore’s industry ecosystem, attract new investments, and ensure that the fruits of economic growth benefits all Singaporeans. Specifically, you will: • Design and implement policies and strategies to enhance our economic competitiveness and value proposition to investors • Review and develop a suite of incentive products (grants, tax incentives, loans, equity, and hybrid instruments) that are aligned with Singapore’s economic strategies, generate strong outcomes and offer taxpayers value-for-money • Assess tax incentive applications/amendments that require Ministry approval • Review the performance of individual sectors, identify opportunities for restructuring, and foster the growth of nascent sectors • Evaluate business plans comprehensively and critically, including to ensure alignment with our economic strategies • Formulate Singapore’s policy responses to emerging issues such as on economic security and supply chain resilience • Monitor and develop our response to international economic and tax developments, such as the Base Erosion and Profit Shifting (BEPS 2.0) project • Be the custodian for the Economic Expansion Incentives (Relief from Income Tax) Act • Work with the Ministry’s Political Office Holders and Senior Management, partners from across Government (especially the Economic Development Board and Enterprise Singapore), and industry stakeholders. To excel in this role, you should be comfortable with: • Translating knowledge and insights into practical frameworks, strategies and policy recommendations • Working in a fast-paced environment and adapting to organisational needs and priorities • Assessing various viewpoints and proposing recommendations with clear articulation of benefits and trade-offs, employing data, insights, and credible external perspectives in the process

jobs.title: Deputy Director, AI R&D (LLM & Multimodal AI), Q Team CoE
jobs.inst: Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 10 Jul 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). The Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX) is a statutory board under the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) which aims to pioneer innovation solutions and develop world class science and technology capabilities to transform and empower the Home Team in delivering safety and security for Singapore. The HTX AI Products team is committed to empowering our Home Team Department (HTD)'s flagship projects through the seamless integration of AI technologies. From identifying opportunities to delivery, launch, and continuous enhancement, HTX AI Products aim to push boundaries and setting new We are seeking a visionary and highly skilled Deputy Director to lead and manage a multidisciplinary team focused on the design, development, deployment, and governance of Large Language Model (LLM) and Large Multimodal Model (LMM) solutions. The ideal candidate will combine deep technical expertise with strong leadership, strategic thinking, and organizational management capabilities. This role will be pivotal in shaping HTX AI strategy, ensuring operational excellence, and driving innovation in real-world applications. Leadership & Strategy: Define and execute the strategic roadmap for LLM/LMM research, development, and deployment in alignment with organizational goals. Serve as a trusted advisor to senior leadership on AI/LLM/LMM trends, risks, and opportunities. Foster a culture of innovation, ethical AI use, and continuous improvement across the team. Team Management: Lead, mentor, and grow a high-performing team of AI researchers, machine learning engineers, MLOps specialists, and product managers. Oversee cross-functional collaboration with stakeholders in data engineering, product, compliance, and security. Ensure clear objectives, measurable performance metrics, and career development pathways for team members. Technical Oversight: Direct the training, fine-tuning, evaluation, and deployment of cutting-edge LLMs. Guide the design of robust MLOps pipelines for scalable and reliable deployment. Oversee research on prompt engineering, retrieval-augmented generation (RAG), multilingual LLMs, and domain adaptation. Ensure responsible AI practices, including fairness, bias mitigation, interpretability, and compliance with regulations. Program & Project Management: Manage project portfolios, budgets, and timelines, ensuring delivery of high-quality outcomes. Implement governance frameworks for model monitoring, security, and risk management. Drive partnerships with academia, industry, and open-source communities to accelerate progress. Tertiary education preferably an advanced degree (PhD or Master’s) in Computer Science, Machine Learning, AI, or related fields. 10+ years of progressive experience in AI/ML, with at least 5 years in team leadership or senior management roles. Proven track record in large-scale ML/LLM development, deployment, and lifecycle management. Strong understanding of AI, AI platforms, MLOps practices, and modern data infrastructure. Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills. Preferred: Experience with fine-tuning foundation models, RAG pipelines, and multilingual/multi-domain adaptation. Prior experience in public sector, regulated industries, or mission-critical environments. Demonstrated ability to manage cross-border or multi-site AI teams. Publications or patents in LLMs, NLP, or generative AI. ***All new hires are appointed on a two-year contract in the first instance and will be assessed and considered for permanent tenure over time, based on performance. As part of the shortlisting process for this role, you may be required to complete a medical declaration and/or undergo further assessment. All applicants will be updated on the status of their applications within 4 weeks upon closing of the advertisement.

jobs.title: Senior Executive/Assistant Director, Strategic Planning and Policy
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 23 Jun 2026. Closing in 4 day(s). Shaping Singapore’s Future as a Global Hub for Intangible Assets (IA) and Intellectual Property (IP) As a member of the Strategic Planning and Policy team, you will play a key role in shaping Singapore’s national plans to build the country as a global hub for intangible assets (IA) and intellectual property (IP). You will contribute directly to the development and implementation of IP-related policies and initiatives that support creators, innovators, and enterprises in unlocking the value of their IA and IP for business growth. In this role, you will also collaborate with local and international stakeholders, including government agencies, IP practitioners and enterprises, to build a vibrant IA/IP ecosystem. Key Responsibilities: 1. Shape organisational direction and outcomes Work with senior management and departments to support corporate planning functions, including through data and economic analyses. 2. Stakeholder management Develop and maintain relationships with key stakeholders, including other public agencies. Support the preparation of strategic updates to internal and external stakeholders. 3. Policy formation and reviews Support policy formulation and reviews through data analysis, industry consultations and horizon scanning. Help shape initiatives to help enterprises use their intangible assets and IP for growth. 4. Operational support Support the department in a variety of tasks, including secretariat functions, data visualisation, event organisation and preparation of reports. What We’re Looking For: • Interest in innovation, IP and public policy. • Strong communication skills, with the ability to engage stakeholders at all levels. • Strong analytical and critical thinking ability. • Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively across teams and agencies. • Experience in corporate planning, consulting or analytical roles would be advantageous.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Service and Digital Transformation), FLG/OPT
jobs.inst: Ministry Of Social And Family Development
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 24 Jun 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. Families are the bedrock of our society. The goal of Family Life Group (FLG) is to build strong and resilient families, supporting them through all life stages. As the central division for FLG, the Operations Planning & Transformation (OPT) division is responsible for ops strategy, corporate planning, data and digitalisation, transformation as well as organisational development strategies for FLG. You will be leading a team to drive digital and technology transformation plans across Family Life Group, to improve business outcomes such as user experience, service delivery and programme efficacy. Our aim is to strengthen access to FLG services and programmes and deliver MSF’s strategic outcomes of encouraging marriage and parenthood. The role involves the following: • Conducting systems-level review and planning of FLG’s digital, AI and technology capabilities to optimise FLG services and programmes that involves both Ministry and Whole-of-Government (WOG) stakeholders. • Identifying technological gaps and opportunities to improve service delivery and programme implementation to better serve families through all stages of life. • Evaluating and addressing cross-cutting service gaps and enhance customer experience. • Spearheading FLG’s digital and technology priorities and plans. • Identify and develop technology use cases that can transform core businesses/service delivery in FLG. This include working with key stakeholders to discover, design, prototype (e.g. proof-of-concepts) to scale up. To meet the challenges of this role, you should possess: At least 8 years of experience in digital transformation, such as digitalisation of operations, leveraging digital tech to improve business outcomes, creating digital products or platforms or encouraging innovation and experimentation in digital-tech or AI. KEY TRAITS - Strong analytical and critical thinking, writing and communication skills. - Ability to analyse issues from a strategic and systems perspective, while having a good grasp of details and processes. - Strong stakeholder engagement and management skills. - Experience in leading teams in transformation related work. - Strong problem-solving skills with proven ability to deliver impact in greenfield environments - Strong interest in digital technology and emerging technologies (eg. AI) will be a plus

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Mental Healthcare Policy)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 24 Jun 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). You are responsible for the staffing of mental health policies and capability development, including mental health plans and strategies, and the operationalisation of wartime mental health capabilities. You support the development, review, and refinement of mental health policies and strategies to future-proof MINDEF/SAF's mental health capabilities and deepen the organisation's understanding of national and SAF mental health trends. - Draft parliamentary replies and Committee of Supply speeches, including preparing supplementary information - Draft mental healthcare directives to manage the mental health of pre-enlistees, servicemen and regulars - Study best practices and policies from Whole-of-Government (WOG) stakeholders such as the National Mental Health Office, Ministry of Home Affairs, Ministry of Education, and Institute of Mental Health - Draft communication lines for external media on an ad-hoc basis - Support the development and implementation of MINDEF/SAF's mental health strategy, including needs analysis and study of best practices - Support the registration and regulation of psychologists, and develop social work capability including referral workflows and linkages to social service agencies - Conduct post-implementation reviews, including planning, gap analyses, and reporting - Draft wartime operational concepts including core requirements, organisational structure, battle cycles, manpower deployment and interventions - Assist in testing and refining wartime roles and linkages through exercise planning and coordination - Conduct periodic reviews of existing mental health policies, strategies, and plans, and vet staff officers' work Challenge(s) - Balancing multiple considerations including the evolving national mental health landscape, sustainability of resources, operational effectiveness, and the principles of equity and universality, requiring a strong understanding of organisational requirements and regular engagement with stakeholders - Navigating conflicting demands from a wide range of stakeholders including manpower officers, ground commanders, and mental health practitioners, each with different priorities, and reconciling these through effective stakeholder management to gain buy-in - Education in Psychology, Counselling, Social Work, or a related field - At least 9 years of relevant experience - Competencies in healthcare strategy and organisational planning, healthcare leadership and management, professional competency in mental health or related fields, and data analytics Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Security Systems & Operations)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 24 Jun 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). You play a pivotal role in managing advanced aviation security systems [Republic of Singapore Air Force (RSAF) F-35 Security Systems] and overseeing all F-35 security-related development and operations, working in close partnership with the US Government (USG) and their F-35 program security office. - Develop security plans and operating procedures to safeguard the F-35 classified facilities - Engage F-35 partner nations and attend overseas security conferences on F-35 security developments - Coordinate and facilitate security inspections by the USG Challenge(s) - Keeping abreast of security threats and risks, including the emergence of new technology, that is applicable to the existing security systems - At least 6 years of working experience - Strong command in English language for effective communication with foreign partners and staff writing requirements - Ability to travel occasionally for meetings, seminars, and deployments - Ability to lead a small team of middle managers to conduct security operations Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Readiness & Collaboration)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 24 Jun 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). You are responsible for overseeing the planning and execution of all contract management activities for designated commands and departments in the Digital and Intelligence Service (DIS), working with various stakeholders to advance industry support by incorporating operational needs into contracts. - Lead the evaluation, selection, and onboarding of vendors and service providers - Lead the identification, assessment, and mitigation of contractual and operational risks - Lead, mentor, and develop a high-performing team of contract management professionals - Lead the team in identifying critical capabilities and capacities that cannot be fulfilled organically, and develop contractual outcomes to address these gaps Challenge(s) - Managing complex, large-scale, multi-party contracts with varying terms, conditions, and performance metrics across diverse sectors - Managing conflicting priorities and expectations among internal departments, senior leadership, and external vendors - At least 6 years of experience in contract management related areas such as procurement, logistics, contract establishment, or contractor management - Proficiency in managing complex, multi-stakeholder projects - Strong analytical and problem-solving skills, with keen attention to detail and a commitment to excellence - Strong leadership and decision-making ability - Good interpersonal and communication skills, with the ability to build trust and influence stakeholders at all levels - Skilled in negotiation, stakeholder engagement, and dispute resolution - Competency in financial analysis and budgeting Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Audience Analytics)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 24 Jun 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). You drive the design, development, and implementation of audience profiles and frameworks to enhance MINDEF/SAF's strategy, policy, and planning across public communications, internal communications, and community engagement. - Develop strategic recommendations for the engagement of MINDEF/SAF's audiences - Coordinate the conduct of social science studies across different segments of MINDEF/SAF's audiences Challenge(s) - Keeping abreast of the latest research and practices in psychology, and ensuring that MINDEF/SAF's capabilities evolve alongside the changing environment - At least 6 years of experience in designing and conducting research studies with an analysis component - Basic knowledge in social science analysis and experience using analysis software such as SPSS; experience with analysis or programming languages such as R or Python is advantageous - Strong critical thinking skills, with the ability to think quickly and innovatively to solve problems and develop solutions - Good organisational acumen and a keen appreciation of defence, security, and the changing information environment, with the ability to apply psychology to meet organisational needs - Strong presentation and communication skills to present findings and recommendations in a meaningful and accessible way Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Connectivity Governance & Engagement)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 24 Jun 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). You drive the enhancement of the Singapore Armed Forces (SAF)'s warfighting network capabilities through the design and upkeep of the SAF's network connectivity architectures. You will also serve as the technical policy lead for the SAF in collaborating bilaterally and multilaterally with partner armed forces and defence establishments on communications and connectivity efforts. - Work with various stakeholders to consolidate and fuse connectivity requirements - Establish and maintain connectivity-related policy documents, ensuring their relevance and feasibility - Develop new and revise connectivity-related policies in response to evolving operational needs - Engage extensively with stakeholders to gather effective feedback for policy enhancement Challenge(s) - Exercising defence relations acumen in engagements with foreign armed forces and defence establishments - Understanding and managing the dynamics of cross-functional teams with varying priorities - Developing a strong understanding of the operational connectivity needs, systems and projects - Education in Computer Science, Engineering or an equivalent discipline - At least 6 years of experience in bilateral or multilateral defence relations - Strong acumen in communications and connectivity solutions - Keen interest in communications and connectivity work within a defence relations domain Only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

jobs.title: Senior Assistant Director/Assistant Director/Manager (Strategic Planning)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 17 Jul 2026. Closing on 09 Aug 2026. With increasing pace of change and uncertainty in the operating environment, the Strategic Planning Division plays a critical role in stewarding Singapore’s limited land resources to meet the needs and aspirations of Singaporeans. This includes planning for key national priorities such as housing and economic development, contributing to innovative approaches in creating new land and space, while ensuring that land use remains sustainable, flexible, adaptable and resilient for the future. The officer will be part of a dynamic and fast-paced environment, with significant opportunities to learn and grow while working on complex challenges to improve Singapore’s future. The officer will have the opportunity to engage in policy and planning issues across different timeframes (e.g. safeguarding land for long term vs addressing immediate needs), land use domains (e.g., residential, commercial, community, military uses) and geographical regions in Singapore. The officer will also gain a deeper understanding of how to carefully balance competing demands and make calibrated trade-offs among different considerations (e.g., fiscal, social, economic, environmental), interest groups such as nature and heritage groups, and local communities. The role will also allow the officer to work with a wide range of Government and non-Government stakeholders and sharpen their relationship management, stakeholder engagement and public communication skills. 1. Deep Critical Thinking - Distil critical issues and sense-make from a wide range of information, arriving at effective policy solutions 2. Systems Thinking - Connect issues with larger system and identify inter-dependencies across issues/ domains/ agencies 3. Internal Stakeholder Management - Drive alignment on policy positions and approaches among key internal stakeholders 4. Co-create and Co-deliver with External Stakeholders - Mobilise citizens and stakeholder to co-create and co-deliver solutions to policy issues 5. Clear and Concise Writing - Crystalise core issues via clear and concise writing.

jobs.title: Deputy/Assistant Director, AML Supervision (1-Year Contract)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 27 May 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. This role is focused on developing a knowledge management and analytics platform that enhances information discovery, and drives data-driven insights on supervisory effectiveness. Design, develop and manage an AI-enabled knowledge management hub that consolidates institutional knowledge, improve information discovery and support staff queries through chatbot and intelligences search functions Support the automation of supervisory effectiveness data collection from line supervisory departments, transformation of collected data into meaningful dashboards, reports and visual insights to support monitoring of risk-based supervision effectiveness Design the overall framework for creating, organising, maintaining and retrieving institutional knowledge, including policies and procedures, FAQs, guidance notes, past cases and decision records Work with TG to build a platform that supports intelligent search, chatbot-based queries, document summarisation and recommendation of relevant materials Able to structure and maintain living documents with clear taxonomy and version control Knowledge of how AI systems ingest and retrieve structured content You will be working in a fast-paced environment that would require the ability to manage multiple priorities and needs of stakeholders, as well as the agility to respond to changes and developments. As part of the shortlisting process for this role, you may be required to complete a medical declaration and/or undergo further assessment. This is a 1-year contract. All applicants will be notified on whether they are shortlisted or not within 4 weeks of the closing date of this job posting.

jobs.title: Associate/Assistant Director, Transformation Division (1-Year Contract)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 27 May 2026. Closing in 2 day(s). You will support MAS’ organisation-wide transformation efforts by working closely with stakeholders to track initiatives, synthesise insights, and support implementation and follow-through. This role offers exposure to a range of transformation work, including governance, reporting, and engagement. Your key responsibilities will include: Collecting and consolidating information on ongoing transformation initiatives to support organisation-level reporting and alignment with Whole-of-Government priorities. Cleaning and analysing transformation data collected to derive meaningful insights. Preparing materials for management updates, including organising and presenting data and analysis for reporting and discussion. Building relationships and coordinating with stakeholders (e.g. product teams, policy owners and implementation departments) to understand needs and gather ground feedback. Supporting governance and policy alignment work, including analysing implications, consolidating inputs, and supporting development of recommendations. Supporting change management (e.g. organising and executing activities on the ground, tracking adoption rates, and gathering user feedback). Supporting staff communications, engagement and training (e.g. developing EDMs, presentations, training resources). jobs.jd: Posting Date: 2 Jun 2026. Closing on 31 Dec 2026. You will lead a team of Learning & Development Partners. You are expected to engage and collaborate closely with our internal stakeholders to drive performance, growth and capability development. A key aspect of your role will be partnering with Divisions to conduct comprehensive training needs analysis, identifying skills gaps and learning requirements to develop targeted solutions that address their specific needs. These responsibilities include developing, implementing, and reviewing competency frameworks and training roadmaps, as well as designing and implementing L&D strategies, plans, and policies. You will collaborate with external stakeholders (MINDEF/SAF, Whole-of-Government (WoG) agencies, and commercial entities) to source, manage, and evaluate local and overseas training courses to ensure alignment with MSD’s L&D objectives and evolving training needs. You will also design, deliver and evaluate in-house course curricula, including managing training-related administrative processes and associated training resources effectively. As a Learning and Development (L&D) Partner, you will contribute across the full spectrum of learning capability development. Your key responsibilities include: • Develop, implement, and review L&D policies, competency frameworks, training plans, and roadmaps in close collaboration with internal stakeholders to support their long-term capability development. • Manage and review local and overseas training programmes to ensure they remain relevant, comply with governance standards, align with MSD’s organisational strategy and adapt to the changing workforce needs. • Coordinate and manage training operations, from budget planning and training providers management to participant enrolment and stakeholder engagement, while ensuring smooth logistical execution. • Design, deliver, and evaluate in-house training and curricula using varied instructional methods suited for adult learners across different skill levels. • Use feedback and evaluation data to assess training effectiveness and enhance both programme quality and outcomes. • Source, curate and collaborate with partners (e.g., MINDEF/SAF, Whole-of-Government, commercial providers) on their course delivery, training resources, and evaluation efforts. • Provide regular progress updates, key findings, and strategic recommendations to Management to inform L&D decisions and goals. You should possess a tertiary qualification, preferably in a relevant discipline, along with at least 3 years of related work experience. Professional certifications in Human Resources (HR), Learning and Development (L&D) or Organisation Development (OD) will be an advantage. Strong team player, effective written and verbal communication, and stakeholder management skills are essential. You should also have a good understanding of user-centric design and change management principles, as well as strong analytical and organisational abilities. The ability to think and take a systematic approach to implementing L&D policies and executing learning interventions in a timely manner is critical. Additionally, strong planning, coordination, and people management skills are important for executing training plans and managing courses effectively while optimising training resources. The ability to work both independently and collaboratively in a fast-paced, dynamic environment is key to success in this role.

In this role, you will help develop policies and strategies to advance and safeguard Singapore’s economic and security interests in the international arena. You will also be MTI’s counterparty to other Government agencies and key committees that cover the range of geo-political and security spheres. As a TIER officer in the Trade Strategy and Security Division, you will be part of a dynamic team working on fast-moving pieces to help Singapore navigate changes in our geo-economic environment. You will have the opportunity to hone your strategic thinking skillset, policy acumen, and international orientation perspectives in both the economic and security domains, by: • Assessing the impact and implications of geo-political developments on Singapore’s economic and security interests; • Developing strategies, policies, options and responses to expand Singapore’s economic heft or relevance as well as strengthen Singapore’s economic resilience; • Preparing and executing communications and engagement strategies with key public and private sector stakeholders; • Organising and representing MTI at various high-level inter-agency platforms related to economic, security, and geo-political portfolios; and • Collaborating and networking with various internal and external stakeholders. jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, International Trade (GES)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 15 May 2026. Closing on 31 Oct 2026. The Trade and International Economic Relations (TIER) cluster drives the formulation of Singapore's foreign economic policy and advances Singapore's economic interests in the international economic arena. Climate change is one of the defining issues of our time. It is a complex global challenge that calls for concerted international action and collaboration between governments, industry and individuals. As a low-lying city state, Singapore is particularly vulnerable to the effects of climate change and has a strong interest in shaping effective global climate action. The Green Economy and Sustainability (GES) team sits within MTI’s International Trade Division, which is charged with formulating Singapore’s foreign economic policy and advancing Singapore’s economic interests in the internationally. The GES team leads Singapore’s international economic engagement on green trade issues. We also work closely with agencies, such as MSE, MOF, NEA and PMO(NCCS), to formulate and coordinate strategies to chart the path forward for Singapore’s transition to a low-carbon economy. As a TIER officer in the International Trade (GES) Division in the Ministry of Trade and Industry, you will be part of a high-performing team operating at the forefront of international economic diplomacy and climate policy, shaping the rules and frameworks that will define the global green economy. You will work on one or more of the following work streams: a. Negotiation of Article 6.2 bilateral/plurilateral agreements. Develop and implement strategies and policies to strengthen bilateral cooperation with like-minded countries on carbon credits aligned with Article 6 of the Paris Agreement. Officer will negotiate binding bilateral and plurilateral agreements with foreign governments, drive technical cooperation initiatives, and build robust project development pipelines — directly contributing to Singapore's ability to meet its Nationally Determined Contribution (NDC). b. Global carbon market rules and standards setting. Monitor and analyse evolving global carbon market developments and develop strategies to actively shape the international rules and standards that govern high-integrity carbon markets, positioning Singapore as a credible and influential voice in this space. c. Green trade policy development. Synthesise geopolitical, trade, and climate trends to craft Singapore's green trade policy agenda, safeguarding the country's long-term economic competitiveness through the low-carbon transition. The officer’s work will directly inform how Singapore positions itself in an increasingly climate-conscious global trading environment. d. Green trade instruments negotiation. Negotiate international instruments across multilateral, plurilateral, and bilateral fronts to advance Singapore's green trade interests. Officer will work with Ministries across the government to tackle complex, cross-cutting issues spanning environmental goods, services and investments, trade-related climate measures (TrCMs), environmental attribute certificates (EACs), and sectoral areas such as sustainable aviation fuel (SAF) and green buildings. e. Multilateral negotiations. Represent Singapore at the highest levels of international climate diplomacy, including at the UNFCCC Conference of the Parties (COP). You will advocate for Singapore's green economy and low-carbon interests, building coalitions and influencing outcomes that have real-world consequences for global climate action. As the climate space is fast evolving, an officer may be deployed across different workstreams, based on the officer’s development and MTI’s organisational needs. • Experience in cross-cultural negotiations and international relations, particularly in the public sector; and/or • Good knowledge in carbon credits/markets and Paris Agreement; and/or • Good knowledge of green trade related issues including trade-related climate measures, environmental attribut

jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, International Trade Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 15 May 2026. The Trade and International Economic Relations (TIER) cluster drives the formulation of Singapore's foreign economic policy and advances Singapore's economic interests in the international economic arena. MTI’s International Trade Division (ITD) is charged with protecting and advancing Singapore’s economic interests by: * Supporting free trade and open markets; * Upholding and strengthening the rules-based multilateral trading system as embodied in the World Trade Organisation (WTO); * Deepening economic integration with like-minded countries through free trade agreements (FTA) and bilateral investment treaties (BIT); * Driving initiatives in regional economic fora such as the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC); and * Advancing our companies’ interests abroad rigorously, including by lowering barriers for Singapore exports Responsibilities As an ITD officer, you will be assigned to one of four specialised teams covering trade in goods, digital economy and services, investment, or multilateral trade policy. You will deepen your knowledge, skills and expertise on a myriad of international trade issues and apply them in the following areas: * Developing and implementing Singapore’s foreign economic policy; * Representing Singapore in bilateral/multilateral trade and investment negotiations; * Participating in international meetings and conferences; * Providing advice to the Singapore Government on trade policy matters; * Collaborating with external stakeholders, such as Singapore-based companies, business chambers and the general public; * Ensuring Singapore’s full and timely implementation of international trade obligations; and * Analysing international developments and their potential implications on Singapore Requirements * Trained in Economics, Law, Political Science, International Relations, Accounting, Business or related disciplines is preferred, but not essential * Keen interest in international economic affairs and negotiation; * Strong analytical and conceptual abilities, with an eye for detail; * Excellent written and oral communication skills; * Ability to work well in teams and in a fast-paced and dynamic environment; and * Willingness to travel frequently

jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, ASEAN Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 15 May 2026. The Trade and International Economic Relations (TIER) cluster drives the formulation of Singapore's foreign economic policy and advances Singapore's economic interests in the international economic arena. ASEAN – Where Proximity, Policies, Partners, and People Intersect Position Overview: Trade Negotiator and Economic Policy Officer Proximity: As we prepare for the future economy, the ASEAN region offers exciting growth opportunities. To enable our businesses and jobholders to seize these opportunities, MTI’s ASEAN Division advances Singapore’s economic interests and deepens regional economic integration at the ASEAN platform. Specifically, we seek to boost flows of goods, services, investments, data, people and ideas to strengthen the broader regional economic ecosystem. Policies: Our team negotiates/reviews regional Agreements at the ASEAN platform to lower tariffs, address non-tariff barriers, improve trade facilitation, grow services flows, enhance the digital ecosystem, and address sustainability challenges amongst others, to improve the ease of doing business and harness the potential of emerging opportunities. Beyond Free Trade Agreements (FTAs), we also develop new initiatives and forms of cooperation to expand economic space through engagement, capacity building, and collaboration. Partners: We nurture and work with partners to drive and champion deepening economic integration through the ASEAN Economic Community (AEC), a key trading partner and investment destination for Singapore. Beyond the 10 ASEAN Member States, the AEC provides opportunities for Singapore-based businesses to take advantage of ASEAN’s Agreements and initiatives with various external partners to facilitate their exports, investments and business activities. ASEAN’s suite of partners includes Australia, Canada, China, the European Union, India, Hong Kong, Japan, Korea, New Zealand, and the United States, amongst others. To ensure businesses can benefit, we also engage stakeholders such as business councils and research institutions to increase awareness and understanding. People: Our team invests in building human capital. We have a unique opportunity to represent Singapore at Free Trade Agreement negotiations, analyse key developments and their potential implications, and take ownership for driving meaningful outcomes. Trade officers in MTI’s ASEAN Division develop technical competencies in either the domain of goods or the domain of digital economy, services, and investment. They gain experience in negotiations, public policy writing, and strategy development, including developing ideas to advance integration under the AEC and how Singapore should engage ASEAN on the economic track amidst a challenging global economic environment. As trade diplomats, we also sharpen skills such as coalition building and relationship management with counterparts in the region and beyond. • Keen interest in international economic affairs and negotiations; • Strong analytical and conceptual abilities, with an eye for detail; • Excellent written and oral communication skills; • Self-starter, versatile and adaptable to different environments; • Ability to work well in teams and engage people; • Ability to work in a fast-paced and dynamic environment; and • Willingness to travel frequently

jobs.title: Deputy/Assistant Director, Investment Schemes & Disclosures
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 17 Jul 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. The candidate will join a team responsible for: Regulation of sustainability disclosures by securities issuers and efforts to build up the broader sustainability reporting and assurance ecosystem, including that for ESG Rating and Data Providers and ESG funds; and Regulation of offers of units in collective investment schemes (CIS), including real estate investment trusts (REITs), exchange-traded funds (ETFs) and unit trusts. Key areas of work include: Sustainability disclosure implementation and assurance – for listed companies and retail investment products, and contributing to global, regional and domestic efforts in standard-setting and development of best practices. Other sustainability-related issues in the securities regulation space – these include guidelines, industry codes or frameworks, for instance the Code of Conduct for ESG Rating and Data Providers. Collective investment schemes (CIS) regulation – administration of rules governing fund offers under the Securities and Futures Act and the Code on Collective Investment Schemes. The role will require engagements with domestic and international standards-setting bodies and regulators to meet market needs for globally consistent and comparable sustainability disclosures and understand their regulatory approaches as they evolve, as well as to support effective implementation of MAS’ CIS regulatory regime. Deep technical knowledge and hands-on experience with corporate financial reporting and/or sustainability reporting, including integration of sustainability-related risks and opportunities into financial decision-making and reporting and sustainability disclosure standards or frameworks (e.g. IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards, Climate Bonds Initiative and GRI Sustainability Reporting Standards). Accounting degree with accounting / audit work experience preferred. Good understanding of sustainability-related matters with nexus to sustainability reporting and securities regulation (e.g. sustainability issues and value creation in the real economy, industry-specific materiality considerations, ESG reporting metrics and taxonomies). Strong strategic thinking, data analysis and problem-solving capabilities. Strong interpersonal skills, ability to positively influence internal and external stakeholders and contribute to internal capacity building. Strong project management skills and ability to handle organisation-wide projects involving multiple stakeholders. Excellent verbal and written communication skills. jobs.title: Director (Assurance)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 4 Jun 2026. Closing on 09 Jun 2027. As Director (Assurance), you will serve as a trusted advisor and strategic partner to public agency leadership, leading high-performing internal audit teams delivering Shared IA Services to Ministries and Statutory Boards. This is not simply an audit leadership role — you will be a change agent, driving the evolution of IA practices across the public sector through digital innovation, agile methodologies, and a broadened risk lens that encompasses technology, cybersecurity, and sustainability. You will shape the future of the public sector IA community by building capabilities, professionalising practices, and fostering a culture of continuous improvement and intellectual curiosity. Strategic Audit Leadership You will direct the planning, management, and execution of risk-based audit engagements across agencies under AGD's portfolio of clients, ensuring timely delivery and consistently high audit quality. Moving beyond traditional compliance-focused auditing, you will develop and maintain a forward-looking audit universe that reflects emerging risks and align audit priorities with the broader governance and risk landscape of the public sector. You will also champion agile auditing methodologies, designing shorter and more iterative audit cycles that deliver faster, more actionable insights to stakeholders. Digital Transformation You will lead the integration of data analytics, robotic process automation (RPA), and AI-assisted audit tools into the audit methodology to enable continuous monitoring and deeper audit insights. You will oversee the adoption of audit management platforms to improve workflow efficiency and documentation standards. Staying ahead of developments in generative AI, machine learning, and other emerging technologies, you will assess their applicability to the IA function and build the team's capacity to leverage these tools effectively. Experience with data analytics tools such as Power BI, or Python in an audit context will be particularly valued. Emerging Risk and Advisory As a strategic advisor, you will engage credibly with C-suite and senior public sector leadership, translating complex audit findings into clear, business-relevant insights that drive meaningful governance improvements. You will provide assurance and advisory perspectives on a broadened risk landscape, including IT general controls, cybersecurity risk, digital business processes, third-party and supply chain risk, and increasingly, sustainability and ESG governance frameworks. You will work closely with agency leadership to anticipate risks before they materialise, positioning IA as a forward-looking function rather than a retrospective one. Community Building and Capability Development You will work with IA heads across the public sector to build a vibrant community of practice, driving the development of shared frameworks, toolkits, and upskilling programmes that raise the overall standard of public sector IA. You will implement capability development roadmaps that equip IA practitioners with both technical audit competencies and digital skills — including data literacy, AI fluency, and agile ways of working. You will represent AGD in cross-agency forums and professional bodies to advance the ambition of public sector IA. People Leadership and Culture You will manage, mentor, and develop a team of IA professionals, building a pipeline of future audit leaders who are technically strong, digitally capable, and strategically minded. You will foster a team culture that values psychological safety, diversity of thought, intellectual curiosity, and a genuine commitment to public service excellence. Attracting and retaining talent in a competitive market will be an important part of your mandate. • A strong background in accountancy or a related discipline is essential, along with at least 10 years of progressive audit experience with a meaningful portion in a leadership capacity. • CIA certi

jobs.jd: Posting Date: 18 Jun 2026. Closing on 31 Dec 2026. The Trade and International Economic Relations (TIER) cluster drives the formulation of Singapore's foreign economic policy and safeguards Singapore's economic interests in the international economic arena. The South Asia, Middle East and Africa Division (SAMEA) is a division within TIER that develops and implements policies aimed at growing Singapore's economic relations with over 70 countries in these regions. The job scope includes leading initiatives such as bilateral platforms, pursuing economic agreements, and staffing of Ministerial visits. As part of SAMEA Division, the India Directorate works on strengthening and broadening Singapore’s economic engagement with India, which is one of the world’s largest and fastest growing economies, a key trade partner of Singapore, and a significant market for our companies. jobs.title: Head/Deputy Head/Assistant Head, RIE Plans
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 13 Jul 2026. Closing on 26 Jul 2026. The National Research Foundation (NRF) is a department within the Prime Minister's Office, and we set the national direction for Research, Innovation and Enterprise (RIE) by developing policies, plans and strategies; funding strategic initiatives that strengthen our scientific and technological capabilities, grow our RIE talent base, and achieve economic and national impact; and fostering RIE collaboration in Singapore and internationally. A positive Research Security (RS) environment gives assurance that Singapore institutions can be trusted partners on projects with many different parties, while ensuring that open collaboration and sharing on non-sensitive research and development can occur unimpeded and contribute to knowledge in the public domain. The officer’s responsibility is to develop and strengthen plans and policy for RS in the RIE ecosystem. The incumbent will: • Develop plans and policies to build an RIE ecosystem that can be trusted by multiple different parties to manage sensitive, private and proprietary data and knowhow; • Interface with relevant agencies and international counterparts to communicate Singapore’s plans and policies, as well as learn from the practices of other countries and institutions; • Communicate plans and policies to RIE ecosystem stakeholders, including Institutes of Higher Learning, Research Institutes and companies, to ensure they understand the importance and abide by prevailing plans and policies, as well as to take in their feedback on how to practice good RS; • Collect and analyse data from international and local RIE ecosystems to benchmark and make recommendations on new plans and policies; • Manage RS-related issues for sensitive and important programmes and proposals; and • Perform any functional work necessary for the effective running of NRF (e.g. secretariat and accompanying officer duties and participate in workgroups and committees). Background in science and engineering related disciplines preferred; • Experience in a research environment, or research administration preferred; • Good awareness of current affairs; • Strong analytical abilities with a broad sense of perspective; • Excellent command of written and spoken English; • Excellent inter-personal skills; and • Team-player, meticulous, resourceful, able to work in a fast-paced environment and to perform under pressure.

jobs.title: (Senior) Assistant Director/Manager, Infrastructure Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 13 Jul 2026. Closing on 27 Jul 2026. The Infrastructure Division at the Ministry of National Development (MND) is responsible for shaping policies to enable Singapore to develop world-class infrastructure and create a vibrant, resilient, liveable and sustainable Living Environment. You will work on issues that sit at the intersection of infrastructure, community needs and public policy — translating ground feedback and strategic priorities into practical upgrading initiatives that enhance estate accessibility, connectivity, safety and quality of life. You will also have the opportunity to work on key policy issues to transform the Built Environment Sector. For instance, CORENET X is a major transformation of Singapore’s building regulatory approval process, moving the industry towards a more coordinated, digital and integrated way of making regulatory submissions. This role offers the opportunity to shape policies and programmes that directly improve the living environment in Singapore’s private residential estates and to transform the Built Environment Sector. This includes programmes such as the Enhancement for Active Seniors (Private Housing) and the Estate Upgrading Programme, and policies relating to CORENET X. You will engage agencies and stakeholders, address both policy and operational issues, and help ensure that programmes are delivered effectively and responsibly. In doing so, you will gain exposure to end-to-end policymaking — from policy review and programme design to governance, communications and delivery — while contributing to visible outcomes that benefit Singapore and Singaporeans. Through your work, you will have the opportunity to translate strategic intent and objectives into policy moves and programmes, and work closely with the Building and Construction Authority (BCA) and other stakeholders. The role is also well-suited for officers who enjoy policy work with a strong implementation focus. • A team player with strong interpersonal, collaboration, and stakeholder management skills. • Organised, with excellent written and communications skills. • Basic data processing and visualisation skills (via MS Excel). • Experience in policymaking and/or inter-agency work would be advantageous.

jobs.title: Senior Exec / Asst Director Maintenance Enforcement Officer (11-month contract)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 15 Jul 2026. Closing on 29 Jul 2026. The Maintenance Enforcement Division (“MED”) is a new division under the Insolvency and Public Trustee’s Office (“IPTO”). The MED enhances access to justice by improving the process for enforcing maintenance arrears. A summary of the maintenance enforcement process and the role of the MEO can be found in this video (https://med.mlaw.gov.sg/video-role-of-maintenanance-enforcement-officers/). Your responsibilities as a Maintenance Enforcement Officer (MEO) in the MED will include the following: a) Managing your own cases relating to the enforcement of maintenance orders. You will conduct (1) fact-finding and (2) conciliation, and (3) write reports regarding your findings. In conciliation, which replaces the existing mediation process, MEOs will play a more active role in seeking information and recommending solutions to the parties. You will be required to understand agreements reached between parties and draft the corresponding Consent Order. You will also need to appear in court via video-conferencing to assist parties in recording the Consent Order. You may also be required to appear in court if the judge requires your attendance to address any queries regarding the report. b) Beyond your casework, you will also have the opportunity to contribute to projects relating to, amongst others, enhancing operational workflows, developing IT systems and testing GenAI products, and evaluating the maintenance enforcement process. Key competencies a) [Strong interpersonal and communication skills] Strong communication abilities and emotional resilience to manage hostile or emotional situations, de-escalate conflicts, and facilitate constructive dialogue between parties with fundamental disagreements, particularly drawing from family law work, social work or counselling experience. b) [Investigating and Gathering Evidence] Ensure all evidence related to the case are appropriately obtained, documented, processed, and stored in accordance with agency policy and procedures to support case resolution. Able to assess financial information and formulate practical settlement proposals based on parties’ financial circumstances and needs (including understanding of common financial documents such as bank, CPF, and IRAS statements as well as payslips) c) [Case management] Independently manage multiple case files progression, as well as related financial matters while ensuring compliance with SOP and timelines. d) [Leveraging on technology] Keep up to date on technological trends relevant to my work in enforcement, and adopt relevant technology-enabled solutions to address current and future enforcement challenges. e) [Application of law] Demonstrate understanding of and apply the fundamental legal principles, laws, legislation and government regulation to the relevant areas of work. f) [Litigation support] Prepare high quality and accurate written correspondence as well as court papers such as the MEO Report or follow-up reports to be submitted to the Court within required timeline. g) [Data Analytics] Proficiency in data analysis with strong Microsoft Excel skills, including advanced functions, pivot tables, and data visualisation, experience in interpreting datasets and translating findings into actionable insights. Personal traits Candidates should: a) Possess strong verbal and written communication skills b) Be able to do front-line work with members of public c) Have a keen interest in the development of ops-policies and operational work d) Be meticulous and capable of collecting and evaluating complex or multifaceted information e) Prior experience in mediation, legal drafting, handling casework that involves family disputes, family law practice, finance, accounting will be an advantage, but is not a pre-requisite for the role. f) Ability to speak local languages/dialects will be an advantage.

jobs.title: Manager / Assistant Director, Corporate Strategy, Research and Strategy Manageme
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 17 Jul 2026. Closing on 26 Jul 2026. Want a front-row seat to how ministry-level strategy is shaped – and a chance to influence it? What the role is: Join MND’s Corporate Strategy team and work with senior leadership to tackle complex, cross-cutting challenges that shape Singapore’s built environment – from housing and land use to urban development, infrastructure, municipal services, technology and resilience. From supporting medium- to long-term strategic planning to navigating emerging risks, you will help translate issues and trade-offs into clear insights for senior decision-making, while building strong foundations in strategy, problem-solving, and stakeholder engagement early in your career. Join the team that shapes how the MND Family thinks about its future. As part of the Corporate Strategy team, you will help MND Senior Management make sense of complex, cross-cutting or emerging issues, connect perspectives across the MND and its Statutory Boards (i.e. MND Family), and align stakeholders around strategic priorities. This is a unique opportunity to gain exposure to how strategic issues are framed, discussed and progressed at the senior leadership level, across a wide range of challenges spanning housing, land use, urban development, municipal services, technological change, risk and resilience, while building strong foundations in strategy, stakeholder management, and enterprise risk governance. - Shape strategic priorities and decision-making Support the development and refinement of the MND Family’s medium- to long-term priorities (5–10 years) by structuring issues, synthesising perspectives, and translating strategic considerations into clear papers and discussions. - Drive cross-cutting strategy projects Lead and structure blue sky initiatives that cut across divisions and agencies - bringing together different perspectives to tackle current and emerging system-level challenges, such as public service experience in an AI-driven society. - Shape senior leadership platforms Help design and drive key forums (e.g. senior management retreats and strategic discussions), ensuring that conversations are focused on the most critical issues and lead to clear outcomes. - Strengthen organisational resilience Identify emerging risks and vulnerabilities, and work with stakeholders to develop strategies that enhance the MND Family’s ability to respond to a rapidly changing environment. - Track what matters Develop and refine ways to monitor progress on key priorities, ensuring leadership has a clear, honest view of where things stand - and where course corrections are needed. Why this role - A panoramic view of how Singapore’s built environment is shaped across housing, land use, infrastructure, urban development and municipal services - Exposure to how strategic issues are framed, discussed and progressed at senior leadership platforms across the MND Family - Rare early-career opportunity to experience how large, diverse organisations are managed from the inside - Build a whole-of-system perspective and develop range - Accelerated learning and development in your career - Strong analytical and critical thinking - Clear and structured communication - Ability to engage and influence stakeholders - Ownership and initiative - Interest in strategy, public policy and organisational management Experience: Open to both strong fresh graduates and early-career officers, with relevant internship or strategy experience.
