jobs.title: Senior Assistant Director (Program Management Team)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 3 Jul 2026. Closing on 31 Aug 2026. We are seeking a highly experienced and strategic candidate to join our Programme Management Team as a lead, overseeing the design development and implementation of critical high-security building facilities. This pivotal role demands a proven track record in leading complex, multi-faceted construction programmes, not just individual project. The successful candidate will serve as the primary technical liaison between our organisation and external design consultants, ensuring all design elements meet stringent government security requirements, operational excellence and industry best practices. You will play a crucial role in delivering world-class, resilient infrastructure vital to our organisation's mission-critical operations. (A) Design Coordination and Management • Coordinate and lead external design consultants, including architects, structural engineers, mechanical and electrical engineers, and security specialists, to develop comprehensive, integrated building designs. • Lead cross-functional teams in translating business requirements into detailed technical specifications and design briefs for consultant engagement, ensuring alignment with programme objectives. • Manage the end-to-end design review process, chair technical coordination meetings, and proactively resolve conflicts between different engineering disciplines to ensure integrated, optimised and secure solutions. (B) Security and Compliance Oversight • Establish, enforce and audit stringent security design parameters across all disciplines, encompassing physical security measures and robust cybersecurity considerations within building management systems and operational technology. • Specify, review and validate sophisticated access control systems, comprehensive surveillance infrastructure, advanced intrusion detection systems, and secure perimeter design, ensuring layered defence. • Ensure all designs rigorously incorporate appropriate compartmentalisation, secure zones, and controlled access pathways, maintaining full compliance with relevant international and local standards and certification scheme such as TIA-942, Uptime Institute Tier classifications, ISO 27001, BCA-IMDA Green Mark for Data Centre (Platinum), BCA Green Mark for Healthier Workplaces (Platinum) and local building codes. (C) Quality Assurance and Technical Review • Conduct rigorous review and validation of all design submissions to ensure 100% compliance with security requirements, operational needs, and industry standards. This includes comprehensive technical review of architectural, structural, mechanical, and electrical drawings, specifications, and documentation, with a keen eye for detail and potential vulnerabilities. • Oversee Workplace Safety and Health (WSH) implementation by project contractors, ensuring all critical systems meet stringent performance requirements and safety standards. (D) Project Lifecycle Management • Oversee the entire project lifecycle from conceptual design through construction, commissioning and handover, ensuring timely delivery within approved budgets and to the highest quality standards. • Manage and validate comprehensive testing and commissioning activities such as Factory Acceptance Tests (FAT), Site Acceptance Tests (SAT), and Integrated Systems Testing (IST) for critical mechanical and electrical systems such as Uninterrupted Power Supply (UPS), generators, chillers, and cooling towers, ensuring resilience and reliability. • Provide regular, accurate, and insightful progress reports and expenditure tracking to senior management, highlight key risks and mitigation strategies. • Possess a degree in Engineering (Electrical, Mechanical, Civil, or related discipline) with a minimum of 7 years of demonstrated experience in building design or construction projects, with a significant portion in high-security or critical infrastructure environments. • Professional engineering certification or chartership i

jobs.title: Senior Assistant Director, Finance Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 7 Jul 2026. Closing on 06 Aug 2026. We are seeking a skilled and motivated individual to join our team as a Finance Business Partner. In this role, you will play a pivotal role in providing advice and support to our customers on budgetary and financial matters to ensure that programmes are managed effectively and financial resources used optimally to achieve the desired outcomes. • Supports NSAS Senior Management as a strategic business partner on key financial matters, including budget availability and utilization and putting in place an effective governance framework with robust internal controls • Puts in place systems and processes to ensure sufficient funding for NSAS in the short to medium term. Consolidates funding requirements from NSAS Divisions, analyzing funding requests and putting them to Senior Management for approval, before submission to MTI-HQ • Reviewing the status of financial incentives awarded to companies and assessing the achievement of incentives conditions with on-site visit and documents review • Ensuring service delivery in accordance with pre-defined service level agreements, from planning, fieldwork and reporting • Identifying gaps, leading and shaping policy framework from issues identified • Promoting organizational awareness on incentive governance and risk management • Putting in place a grants management system to allow the NSAS to seamlessly integrate grant award and payment, based on the WOG AGD financial system • Putting in place an accounting system to properly record and account for NSAS’s annual spending based on the WOG financial system, adopting the new Chart of Accounts and financial structure for compliance with Government financial procedures and regulations • Oversee and manage an effective and efficient system for payment processing of invoices to ensure that financial liabilities are settled with accurate payments made accurately and on a timely basis, and in accordance with the Government rules and regulations. • Oversee the staff claim processing and ensure that the valid claims are promptly processed in accordance with the prevailing guidelines and payments are made to officers on timely basis. • Managed outsourced vendors and ensure that feedback on errors and performance issues are addressed promptly. • Process, analyse and prepare financial operations related reports for regular management reporting and annual/ad hoc returns to the central agencies. • Oversee and manage the financial year-end closing process and liaise with AGO and the external/internal auditors with respect to the audit process and queries, if any. You should possess: • Relevant working experience of at least 6 years, in business partnering, finance and/or budgeting; • Prior experience and knowledge on process re-engineering and driving finance transformation would be an advantage; • Ability to work independently and a good team player with excellent interpersonal, communication and stakeholder management, as well as strong analytical skills; • Committed, self-driven and thrive in a fast-paced environment

PwDs are welcome to apply.

jobs.jd: Posting Date: 7 Jul 2026. Closing on 31 Aug 2026. The Strategy and Women’s Development Division (SWD) is the national focal point on gender matters in Singapore, advancing women’s development through policy, partnerships, communications, and international engagement. You will lead a dynamic and close-knit team of officers to drive key initiatives to build a society where men and women stand as equals and pursue their aspirations to the fullest. You will be involved in leading the implementation of the White Paper on Singapore Women’s Development and facilitate greater women’s representation in leadership roles. You will work closely with public sector agencies, women’s organisations and industry partners. You will also manage issues related to SWD’s engagement with regional and international counterparts and fora pertaining to women’s development, including monitoring the progress and implementation of Singapore’s obligations to the United Nations Convention on the Elimination of All Forms of Discrimination against Women, and represent Singapore at regional and international meetings. • Relevant qualification • At least 4-5 years of working experience preferably in areas related to policy-making and stakeholder engagement • Strong conceptualisation and analysis skills • Systematic, meticulous and good project management skills • Excellent written and communication skills, including in public communications • Good team player with well-rounded interpersonal skills to work across teams and engage diverse stakeholders (public and private sector) • Able to work independently and thrive in a fast-paced environment Only shortlisted candidates will be notified within 4 weeks from the closing date of this position.

jobs.title: [SSD] Divisional Director, Surveillance and Standards Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 17 Jul 2026. Closing on 01 Aug 2026. Job Description • Lead and direct the Financial Reporting and Standards Department and the Inspection Department in carrying out ACRA’s role as the regulator of public accountants, and in promoting high professional standards in the public accountancy sector through key regulatory programmes, including the Practice Monitoring Programme and Financial Reporting Surveillance Programme, to uphold high-quality financial reporting in Singapore. • The role requires extensive accounting and auditing experience, strong technical knowledge, leadership and management capabilities and experience in leading change. The job scope includes working closely with local and international accounting stakeholders, providing strategic and technical accounting advice to the Accounting Standards Committee in discharging its duties under the Accounting Standards Act, and making recommendations to the Public Accountants Oversight Committee on the outcomes and findings arising from inspections of public accountants and public accounting entities. Key Responsibilities Leadership • Provide strategic advice and direction to SSD’s l management teams and staff, as well as the secretariat teams supporting international engagements with bodies such as the International Forum of Independent Audit Regulators (IFIAR), International Accounting Standards Board (IASB) and ASEAN Audit Regulators Group (AARG). • Build capabilities by deepening the technical and professional skillsets of staff, strengthening leadership bench strength, and developing a sustainable talent pipeline to support current and future organisation priorities. • Lead transformation initiatives across the audit and accounting regulatory areas by driving process improvements, adoption of AI and data-enabled approaches to strengthen regulatory effectiveness, enhance operational efficiency and support the sector’s continued development. • As a member of ACRA’s Senior Leadership Team, provide strategic direction and enterprise-level leadership across ACRA, contributing to organisation-wide priorities and outcomes beyond the division’s own functional areas. Strategy and Planning • Strategise and direct the division’s environmental scanning efforts to ensure that ACRA remains abreast of developments in accounting and auditing standards and regulations, as well as changes in the international economic and business environment. • Lead engagement with the public accountancy sector and key stakeholders involved in financial statement preparation, including company directors and management, international audit regulators and accountancy professional bodies, to gather insights and address emerging accounting-related issues. Policy and Operational Excellence • Provide strategic direction for the periodic review and updating of legislation and policies to ensure they remain relevant, robust and responsive to evolving regulatory needs. • Liaise with MOF and other government agencies to advance ACRA’s mission and strategic objectives. • Participate in committee meetings of the Practice Monitoring Sub-Committee (PMSC), Public Accountants Oversight Committee (PAOC), Accounting Standards Committee (ASC) and other relevant committees to provide strategic insights and direction for the development of policies, standards and regulatory programmes. • Build relationships and networks with key stakeholders in the international regulatory community to strengthen international cooperation and ensure alignment with ACRA’s interests and objectives.

jobs.title: Assistant Director, Data Engineering & Architecture (1 year contract)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 2 Jul 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. The Health Promotion Board’s vision is to make Singapore a nation of healthier people. Come be a part of this journey if you’re passionate about creating boundary-pushing work that drives behavioral change.[TV2.1] You will be a part of HPB’s Chief Data Officer's Office (CDOO), Data Engineering and Architecture team, which is building the National Lifestyle Repository (NLR) — a first-of-its-kind platform that integrates lifestyle and preventive health data from across Singapore's health and community ecosystem into a 360-degree citizen-centric view. This work sits at the heart of HPB's Precision Public Health strategy, positioning HPB as the authoritative data custodian for lifestyle data in Singapore and empowering partners and grassroots leaders to deliver more personalised and impactful health programmes at scale. As Assistant Director leading a team of data engineers, you will provide strategic leadership and technical direction across the full lifecycle of the NLR — from architecture and governance to data partnerships, distilling business needs and engineering delivery. You will define and own the overarching technical vision, architecture roadmap, and data governance frameworks, including data exchange protocols and interoperability frameworks, that guide the team's pipeline development, ecosystem support, and governance operationalisation workstreams. You will contribute to HPB's data portability strategy, drawing reference from international approaches and contextualising them to Singapore's health and community landscape. You will lead high-level data partnerships with key agencies including PA, SportSG, AIC/AAC, NParks, LTA, MOH policy and technical teams, and regional health clusters, securing data sharing agreements and consent frameworks that enable trusted, scalable data flows across the ecosystem. You will translate high-level policy and organisational requirements into clear strategic technical directions for the team, ensuring outputs remain aligned with evolving business and programme needs. You will provide strategic oversight and technical guidance across the team's workstreams, resolving escalated issues, ensuring coherence between business requirements, pipeline implementation and governance standards, and driving improvements across the 5Vs of data (Volume, Velocity, Variety, Veracity, and Value) at both team and organisational level. You will research, prototype, and champion the adoption of new technologies and engineering approaches to advance CDOO's data engineering capabilities and future-proof the NLR, and surface and resolve cross-cutting strategic issues that require senior-level decision-making. You will lead, mentor, and develop a team of data engineers, setting clear workstream priorities, guiding them to distil business requirements, managing dependencies and milestones, and engaging senior stakeholders to shape future use cases for Health Promoting Town and Precision Public Health initiatives. An ideal candidate will be a technically strong and strategically minded leader, a dynamic and self-motivated individual with at least 8 years of working experience , including minimally 2 years in supervisory roles, in data engineering or data architecture, including a proven track record in designing and managing large-scale, scalable data platforms across on-premises and cloud-based environments (e.g. AWS, Azure). The candidate should also possess: Demonstrated experience leading data engineering teams and providing oversight across pipeline development and data governance workstreams in complex, multi-stakeholder environments. Strong foundation in solution architecture design, infrastructure planning, data quality management, and data governance strategy, with the ability to translate high-level policy and business requirements into clear technical directions for the team. Proficiency in coding and programming (e.g. Python, R), with the ability to e

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Industrial Security Research)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 1 Jul 2026. Closing on 31 Dec 2026. You will lead a team responsible for safeguarding MINDEF/SAF from potential security risks and compromises. You and your team will be responsible for identifying and managing risks that could compromise MINDEF/SAF. This means developing security frameworks, conducting risk assessments and providing risk mitigation measures to stakeholders within the defence ecosystem. You will also lead efforts in building security awareness through the development of guidance materials, training efforts and working with different entities to embed good security practices into everyday workflows. We are looking for someone who combines strong analytical thinking with the ability to lead a team in a complex organisation. You should have tertiary education, preferably with at least five years of relevant experience in security risk management, intelligence analysis or a related field. Experience in managing a team is essential. Strong written and verbal communication skills are prerequisites as you will need to translate security concepts for audiences who are not security specialists.

jobs.title: Director (Sector Funding), ECDA/SF
jobs.inst: Ministry Of Social And Family Development
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 1 Jul 2026. Closing on 28 Jul 2026. The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) is the regulatory and developmental agency for the early childhood sector in Singapore, overseeing key aspects of children’s development below the age of 7, in both kindergartens and childcare centres. The Sector Funding division in ECDA administers supply-side funding schemes for government supported and non-government supported preschool operators to increase families’ access to quality and affordable early childhood care and education for their children. It also administers the childminding pilot to provide additional care options for families. You will be leading the Sector Funding Division, comprising 3 teams, to formulate, implement and refine supply-side funding for the preschool sector, comprising approx. 300 operators and 1,800 centres. Specifically, your Division administers the following schemes: 1) The Anchor Operator Scheme 2) The Partner Operator Scheme 3) Schemes for non-Government funded centres (e.g. the Preschool Opportunity Fund and any new grants as and when decided) You will also be overseeing the pilot and subsequent scale-up of the Childminding service, which provides care services for families at premises or at childminders’ homes. As Director/SF, you will be responsible for the development, implementation and review of operations policy related to supply-side funding for the early childhood sector, to ensure attainment of schemes’ objectives. You will also oversee grant administration and processes, to ensure accurate and timely disbursement of funds to operators, with proper governance. The total grant amount disbursed under the different schemes is about $2 billion annually. With the growth of the sector, your Division will play the main role in steering the sector towards greater accessibility, affordability and quality of preschools. As a member of ECDA’s Senior Management Team, Director/SF is expected to contribute to the wider work of ECDA, such as in strategic planning, policy discussions, organizational development, and resource use and optimization. The key responsibilities of this role include the following: • Drive the formulation and review of funding models and scheme parameters to ensure attainment of scheme objectives. This includes determining the best approach to funding, drafting and administration of funding agreements, and crafting and monitoring of key performance indicators. • Ensure proper end-to-end grant administration, from determining the application approach, to processing of applications, to contract management, grant disbursement, and conducting external audits. • Develop and review grant control frameworks and grant management procedures and policies to enhance scheme administration and ensure proper governance. • Develop and maintain strong partnerships with operators to engender shared goals and smoothen grant implementation. • Drive the corporate functioning and development of the Division, including determining and securing sufficient budget and its proper management, and the recruitment, optimal deployment and training of manpower. • Drive transformation initiatives such as policy and process innovation, systems and technological development and capability building in the Division. Competencies Required: • Background in any discipline. Knowledge/training in Business Management, Finance, Accountancy or Economics would be an added advantage. • Strong analytical, problem solving, and systems-thinking skills. • Meticulous and comfortable with numbers. • Able to handle and understand legal language. • Attention to detail while not losing the big picture. • Excellent written and communication skills, and able to provide guidance to teams in these skills when needed. • Effective interpersonal skills to work across divisions within ECDA, as well as with public and private sector stakeholders. • Relevant work experience in Ops policy, grants administration would be an advantage. •

jobs.title: Head, Cyber Development & Research, AI Security & Research, xCyber
jobs.inst: Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 14 Jul 2026. Closing on 29 Jul 2026. The Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX) is a statutory board under the Ministry of Home Affairs (MHA) which aims to pioneer innovation solutions and develop world class science and technology capabilities to transform and empower the Home Team in delivering safety and security for Singapore. The jobholder will be a member of xCybersecurity, providing security-by-design leadership to all departments in the Home Team and HTX. xCybersecurity promotes the adoption of defence-in-depth and security best practices and help enable the transformation of Home Team’s cybersecurity capabilities. Through our work, we enhance the resilience of Home Team information-communication systems and networks against sophisticated threats to confidentiality, integrity, and availability. The role requires an experienced leader to spearhead the organization’s cybersecurity and AI security research initiatives. The jobholder will be responsible to develop and implement R&D plans in alignment with HTX’s cybersecurity strategy, directing research into emerging threats, and fostering collaboration with industry and academic partners to strengthen resilience against evolving cyber risks. As Head, Cyber Development & Research, you will be required to: • Develop and drive the organization’s overarching cybersecurity and AI security R&D strategy and framework. • Lead the creation of a cybersecurity R&D roadmap • Manage and coordinate cyber research portfolio of projects, driving innovation and translating research into practical security solutions. • Direct research projects to detect emerging threats, vulnerabilities, and trends in cybersecurity. • Translate research into innovative security solutions, including prototypes and automated tools. • Collaborate with developers, architects, and stakeholders to integrate new security solutions into systems. • Oversee the implementation of security processes and best practices across AI-enabled cyber projects. • Facilitate workshops, seminars, and knowledge-sharing platforms to promote continuous learning. • Build strong partnerships with industry, academia, and internal teams to enhance collaboration and expertise. • A bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Information Technology, or related Engineering fields • At least 8 years of experience in a relevant field, with a minimum of 5 years in a supervisory role. • Professional cybersecurity qualifications such as CISSP, GCIH, or relevant AI security/emerging technology credentials (preferred). • Strong ability to multi-task and perform under extreme pressure with tight deadlines. • Meticulous planning skills and attention to detail. • Ability to work independently as well as collaboratively within a team. • Resilience in managing a reasonably high level of stress while maintaining effectiveness. • Demonstrated drive, level-headedness, and adaptability in dynamic environments. • Excellent interpersonal and communication skills for engaging with internal and external stakeholders. jobs.title: Head, MLOPs, AI Platform, xCloud
jobs.inst: Home Team Science and Technology Agency (HTX)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 10 Jul 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). HTX is the world’s first Science and Technology agency that integrates a diverse range of scientific and engineering capabilities to innovate and deliver transformative and operationally ready solutions for homeland security. As a statutory board of the Ministry of Home Affairs and integral to the Home Team, HTX works at the forefront of science and technology to empower Singapore’s frontline of security. Our shared mission is to amplify, augment and accelerate the Home Team’s advantage and secure Singapore as the safest place on planet earth. You will be part of the AI Platform Team — a group of passionate developers and engineers who believe that DevSecOps is a culture and that embracing AI is the future. The team is charged with transforming the way HTX delivers AI-infused software, leveraging the latest technologies to enable agile software development, zero-trust security, continuous DevSecOps, and artificial intelligence. As Head, MLOps, you will lead a team of engineers in designing, developing, and maintaining secure end-to-end DevSecOps solutions and processes. This is a hands-on technical leadership role sitting at the intersection of systems engineering, applied ML, and enterprise governance — pivotal in ensuring the security, scalability, and operational efficiency of software development and engineering workflows across HTX. You will lead the design, architecture, and delivery of HTX's enterprise-scale AI platform, spanning the inference stack that serves in-house and open-source LLMs, and the agentic AI platform that enables officers across Home Team Departments to build, govern, and automate their own workflows. Through platform engineering, you will enhance developer experience and productivity while safeguarding organisational assets. You will own the architectural direction of the AI platform, influence senior leadership on AI strategy, and build the team and partnerships needed to deliver at scale across on-prem, intranet, and air-gapped environments. AI Platform Architecture & Delivery: Define and evolve the reference architecture for an enterprise agentic AI platform covering agent harnesses, sandboxing strategy, MCP connector gateways, durable execution, skills libraries, approval workflows, and structured audit trails. Shift the organisation from bespoke per-workflow builds to a platform where officers can describe what they need in natural language and have governed, versioned, repeatable workflows generated and executed. Inference Infrastructure: Own the roadmap and production operation of the LLM inference stack, covering vLLM/SGLang/TGI-class serving, GPU utilisation, quantisation, speculative decoding, KV-cache optimisation, and related throughput and latency work. Ensure the stack runs reliably across on-prem GPU clusters and air-gapped environments. Senior Leadership Engagement: Shape and communicate the AI platform strategy to chief executives and senior leadership across the Home Team. This includes building the business case, securing funding, and translating rapidly evolving technical trends (agentic harnesses, MCP adoption, managed agent services) into decisions the organisation can act on. Team Building & Mentorship: Grow the engineering team through hiring, onboarding, and structured internship programmes. Define reading lists, career paths, and technical standards. Mentor engineers across inference, platform, and agentic workflow areas. ● 8+ years of experience in ML platform, MLOps, or large-scale systems engineering roles, with demonstrated technical leadership delivering production AI/ML platforms ● Strong hands-on experience with LLM inference optimisation (vLLM, SGLang, TGI, TensorRT-LLM), including speculative decoding, quantisation, and KV-cache strategies ● Working knowledge of modern agent harnesses(Opencode, Hermes) and durable orchestration patterns, MCP and MCP gateways, and evaluation frameworks for agentic systems ● Understandin

Provide data policy and advisory support. Implement data provisioning and reconciliation standards. They should be comfortable engaging senior stakeholders, translating complex business needs into data solutions, leading multidisciplinary initiatives, and building confidence in data-driven ways of working across the organisati

jobs.title: Manager/Assistant Director
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 10 Jul 2026. Closing in 3 day(s). The Singapore Food Agency’s mission is to ensure and secure a supply of safe food. The Authorisations Division grants licences, permits and other authorisations to enable the supply of safe food in Singapore. Specifically, the Food Business Department of the Authorisations Divisions is responsible for granting licences, permits and other authorisations for food businesses, which comprise food production (farms), non-retail, and retail food businesses. The successful candidate will be part of the Food Business Department and will be responsible for developing frameworks to drive the operationalisation of Part 4 of the Food Safety and Security Act (FSSA) concerning the authorisation of food businesses. Support the development of materials for stakeholder engagement and any engagement plans as required. Support the work relating to authorisation frameworks for related and relevant FSSA subsidiary legislation At least 2 years of working experience in policy / operational policy development and regulatory frameworks, with a proven track record of leading multi-stakeholder policy or regulatory projects. Ability to coordinate across cross-functional teams, synthesise inputs and deliberations and translate them into coherent and detailed frameworks and concepts of operation Meticulous, detail-oriented and strong analytical thinking skills, with the ability to translate broad legislative or policy intent into detailed, operationally implementable regulatory frameworks and requirements Strong project management skills, with the ability to manage multiple complex workstreams concurrently, drive progress, and deliver quality outputs within committed timelines. Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage, influence and collaborate effectively with a diverse range of internal and external stakeholders. Familiarity with licensing and authorisation frameworks, or regulatory compliance in the food industry would also be an advantage. This leadership role requires sharp process thinking, strong execution capabilities, and sound governance judgment, with the ability to translate complex organisational priorities into comprehensive, user-centric workflows and processes. This includes providing guidance on complex, urgent or red flag cases, and reviewing outgoing communications such as website content, advisories and FAQs from a customer-centric lens to ensure clarity and reduce unnecessary service demand. You will ensure compliance and support audit execution across the division by overseeing first and second line of defence check findings from the Compliance & Audit team and Customer Experience Department (CXD), and reviewing quality check results to uphold service standards. Process Improvement & Capability Building You will lead and drive service and administrative improvement initiatives, including training workshops and brown bag sessions to strengthen officers' capabilities in service delivery and administrative governance. You will also collaborate with operations units to streamline workflows and improve efficiency through data-driven insights. We are looking for a resourceful and organised leader with strong operational acumen, a keen eye for service quality, and the governance judgment to keep programmes on track. You should be comfortable working across functions, managing competing priorities, and driving both strategic and operational initiatives. You should also bring: • At least 5 years of experience in operations, administration, service quality or related fields, with a track record of operational excellence. • Strong process thinking, with the ability to translate complex issues into systematic workflows and clear processes. • Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to engage effectively across diverse teams and levels of seniority. • Strong interpersonal and stakeholder management skills, with a collaborative and solutions-oriented working style. • Good analytical skills and digital literacy, with the ability to interpret data and leverage digital tools to drive process improvements.

jobs.title: Assistant Director, Innovation Trials & Adoption Lead (2-year contract)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 8 Jul 2026. Closing on 06 Aug 2026. The Innovation Office helps the Health Promotion Board identify, explore and shape innovation opportunities that can transform and strengthen how we engage citizens and improve population health. Reporting to the Deputy Director, Innovation Office, the Innovation Trials & Adoption Lead drives the experimentation and validation aspects of IO's mandate. The role translates promising opportunities into a curated portfolio of proofs of concept, pilots and trials at different stages of maturity, generating evidence to support decision-making and future adoption. The role provides strategic stewardship for early-stage opportunities where ownership, value or use may still be emerging. It helps HPB clarify whether an opportunity should proceed, stop, pivot, transition to a business unit, scale further, or be translated into useful insights for wider organisational learning. Design and lead practical experiments that test an opportunity's value, feasibility and readiness for adoption, working with stakeholders to define clear success criteria, learning objectives and decision points. Manage a portfolio of trials across different stages - prioritising, sequencing and advancing them in line with strategic value and organisational readiness - while coordinating internal teams, vendors and external partners to keep trials progressing in a timely and compliant manner. Shape and steward early-stage innovation opportunities to clarify their strategic value, identify future business ownership and translate learnings into insights that other teams can build on. Evaluate trials against objectives, risks, feasibility and potential impact, and develop clear recommendations on whether opportunities should proceed, stop, pivot, transition to a business unit or scale. Surface evidence-based findings and recommendations to senior management, and ensure that learnings from all trials - including those that do not proceed - are captured and shared to inform future innovation efforts. Identify suitable long-term owners for concluded trials and work with them to transfer ownership and insights effectively. Develop a practical and replicable handover process and transition plan that can be applied consistently across all concluding trials, and support business units in adopting or adapting these insights for future opportunities. Build stakeholder understanding, interest and support for innovation opportunities, bringing together relevant parties to shape, test and refine ideas through experimentation. Document key lessons, observations and evidence from each trial in a format suited for decision-making, handover or future reference, and package trial outputs so they can be adopted, adapted or referenced by business units and future innovation teams. Present findings and recommendations clearly to senior management, and work closely with other IO leads to advance opportunities through the innovation pipeline. At least 8–10 years of relevant experience in innovation, transformation, strategy, product development, pilot delivery or related fields. Strong ability to work effectively in ambiguous and evolving environments. Experience shaping, leading or supporting pilots, proofs of concept and innovation programmes. Experience managing a portfolio of work, leads or opportunities, with the ability to track progress, prioritise effort and keep multiple moving parts organised. Experience coordinating vendors or external partners to support delivery of pilots, trials or related workstreams. Familiarity with public-sector procurement processes, with the ability to engage vendors in a timely and compliant way. Strong analytical, stakeholder engagement, sensemaking and decision-support skills. Familiarity with digital technologies, AI, health technology or public-sector innovation would be an advantage. Successful candidates will be offered a 2-year contract in the first instance

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Strategic Planning), ECDA/CD

jobs.jd: Posting Date: 9 Jul 2026. Closing in 3 day(s). The Early Childhood Development Agency (ECDA) is the regulatory and developmental agency for the early childhood sector in Singapore, jointly overseen by the Ministry of Education (MOE) and the Ministry of Social and Family Development (MSF). We oversee children's development below the age of seven across kindergartens and childcare centres, spanning quality standards, professional development, programme affordability, and early intervention services. You will be part of the Ops Policy and Planning team within the Professional Pillar, which leads and drives quality across Early Childhood (EC) and Early Intervention (EI) professionals and programmes, anchored on four outcomes: quality professionals, quality programmes, quality preschools, and better outcomes for every child. Strategic Planning and Roadmap • Drive and coordinate the Pillar's multi-year strategic planning agenda, collaborating with senior leaders, internal teams, and external partners to ensure alignment with ECDA's mandate and national early childhood and early intervention priorities. • Monitor and synthesise policy shifts, research developments, and sector trends locally and internationally to support evidence-based planning and keep the Pillar's strategies current and forward-looking. • Conduct environment scanning and foresight work and perform data analytics to identify emerging trends, insights, and threats that could shape the Pillar's policies and programmes. Planning Frameworks and Performance • Design and maintain planning frameworks, KPIs, and outcome metrics for quality education, tracking progress against strategic goals and surfacing gaps or misalignments for timely attention by senior leadership. • Support annual resource planning and reporting cycles to ensure accountability and alignment with Whole-of-Government (WoG) objectives. • Coordinate the tracking of key performance indicators and support enterprise risk management across the Pillar. Cross-Pillar Coordination and Initiative Development • Serve as a strategic bridge across the Pillar's teams to ensure coherence across workstreams — for example, ensuring that quality improvement initiatives for preschools are matched with corresponding professional development pathways for EC and EI practitioners. • Lead cross-team efforts to conceptualise and design new and cross-cutting initiatives, and coordinate their development and implementation across the Pillar. • Stay closely plugged in to policy developments across MSF and work with partners across the public sector and ground agencies to translate broader policy directions into actionable plans for the Pillar. • A degree in any discipline, with at least 8 years of relevant working experience, with a proven track record of leading teams and managing complex, multi-stakeholder projects. • Domain knowledge in at least one of the following areas would be advantageous: workforce or professional development, child or educational research, early childhood or early intervention, programme development or quality assurance. • Strong people leadership skills, with the ability to motivate, develop, and guide a team while fostering a collaborative and high-performing work culture. • Excellent writing, communication and presentation skills, with the ability to distil complex information into clear and actionable insights for senior leadership and diverse audiences. • Strong analytical, systems thinking, and organisational skills, with the ability to see how different workstreams connect and influence one another. • A results-oriented, highly self-motivated and resilient individual who thrives in a fast-paced and dynamic environment. • Able to manage multiple priorities and tight deadlines, and work effectively both independently and in a team or cross-team setting.

plans. • Excellent writing, communication and presentation skills • Strong planning, organisational, and interpersonal skills • Be a results-orientated, highly self-motivated and resilient person • jobs.title: Market Director, Communities & Networks, Singapore Global Network
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 8 Feb 2026. Closing on 30 Aug 2026. About EDB The Singapore Economic Development Board (EDB), a government agency under the Ministry of Trade and Industry, is responsible for strategies that enhance Singapore’s position as a global centre for business, innovation, and talent. We undertake investment promotion and industry development, and work with international businesses, both foreign and local, by providing information, connection to partners and access to government incentives for their investments. Our mission is to create sustainable economic growth, with vibrant business and good job opportunities for Singapore. For more information on EDB, please visit www.edb.gov.sg About SGN The Singapore Global Network (SGN) is a division under EDB and focuses on building a strong ecosystem of overseas family, friends and fans of Singapore. This includes growing a network of individuals whose skills, affinity and influence could strengthen Singapore’s business and talent ecosystem, along with enhancing international mindshare of Singapore as a vibrant global hub in Asia. The Communities & Networks team is responsible for driving region-specific efforts to grow and engage SGN’s global networks. The global team comprises officers who develop and execute strategies either from the Singapore office or one of SGN’s overseas offices. For more information on SGN, please visit www.singaporeglobalnetwork.gov.sg You will work closely with colleagues in EDB and across government, as well as in-market partners ranging from industry to community organisations to develop and execute market development strategies for our activities. Your responsibilities include but are not limited to: Planning and executing strategies to grow, engage, and foster community within SGN’s network of members in your assigned region through a range of online and offline initiatives. This includes designing and delivering both one-to-one and one-to-many engagements. Identifying, developing and maintaining a network of corporate / community partners who can achieve SGN’s business outcomes. Collaborating with other EDB divisions and Singapore Government agencies to develop and execute projects to harness SGN’s network. Monitoring ground sentiments, identifying opportunities and preparing insights to inform SGN’s strategies and support the national agenda. A university degree in any discipline with at least 5 years of working experience in marketing, international relations or community building. Relevant overseas working experiences will be a bonus. Strong interest in current affairs and knowledge of latest developments in Singapore and trending global issues. Enthusiastic about building and nurturing relationships, and managing stakeholders. Energetic, sociable, empathetic and comfortable engaging a cross-cultural audience. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, in both written and spoken English. Additional language skills (e.g. Mandarin, Bahasa Melayu, etc.) would be a bonus. Exceptional organisational and project management skills, able to manage multiple complex projects concurrently. Highly adaptable, able to work independently and in cross-functional teams. Open to being based overseas for a work posting. Detail-oriented with accuracy of delivery. Ability to travel overseas for work. Singaporean.

jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, Energy Markets and Infrastructure Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 2 Jul 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. MTI’s Energy Markets and Infrastructure Division plays a key role in ensuring Singapore’s energy security and creating an energy system that is resilient, cost-competitive, and ready to transition to a low carbon future. Energy security is an existential issue for Singapore. We import almost all our energy supply and are exposed to global energy market movements. The energy crisis after COVID and the war in Ukraine have upended the global energy ecosystem. The climate crisis compels all countries to accelerate their pace of decarbonisation, even as they struggle with energy costs and unpredictable supply chain shocks. Amidst this backdrop, Singapore needs a stable, reliable, and cost competitive energy system that keeps the lights on and safeguards our economic competitiveness and dynamism. MTI’s Energy and Carbon (E&C) Cluster leads initiatives to reduce emissions and drive the transition to a low-carbon economy, while ensuring energy security and cost-competitiveness, and enabling Singapore to sustain economic growth. Officers in this cluster develop energy and carbon markets, plan energy infrastructure, build Singapore’s capabilities to harness low-carbon energy sources, and manage emission trajectories and carbon budgets. You will collaborate with stakeholders across Government, industry, and international partners to support sustainable economic transformation and green growth through strategic planning, policy analysis, and partnerships. The Energy Markets and Infrastructure Division within the E&C Cluster takes the lead in: • Planning for Singapore’s energy transition and resilience, including energy demand and supply and the corresponding impact on infrastructure requirements, system costs and electricity prices. • Strengthening Singapore’s gas and electricity markets and our national grid infrastructure • Developing horizontal enablers for the energy sector e.g. manpower and skills development, energy public engagement strategy. Your responsibilities include one or more of the following workstreams: • Formulate robust strategies and policies to regulate and develop the gas and electricity sectors. This involves working with key stakeholders to develop and implement regulatory and market structures, to strike a balance among our objectives of economic competitiveness, energy security and environmental sustainability. • Formulate and review strategies and policies to enhance our energy security by diversifying Singapore’s energy sources, including those for electricity and gas imports. • Formulate and review policies and strategies to develop supporting enablers for the electricity and gas sectors, including infrastructure and financing. • Develop and implement communications and engagement plans to support electricity and gas market changes. • Monitor developments in global energy markets and analyse implications for domestic energy policies. We are looking for driven and capable individuals who thrive in a fast-paced and dynamic environment, have a keen interest in energy markets and policies, are adept at working with multiple stakeholders within and outside the public sector, and are equipped with the following qualities and competencies: • Strong analytical skills • 0 to 6 years of relevant experience in policy-related work, sustainability and energy-related fields • Ability to pick up new content areas quickly, and assimilate knowledge from a wide variety of sources to provide policy recommendations • Comfortable working with a wide range of stakeholders, both within and beyond the public service, which requires strong interpersonal, organisational and communication skills • Ability to work well both independently and as part of a team • Be driven, meticulous and resourceful

jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, Energy Partnerships Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 2 Jul 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. By joining the Energy Partnerships Division at MTI, you will become part of a critical and urgent mission: to support Singapore’s energy transition by securing low-carbon electricity imports from our region and building an interconnected regional grid. Low-carbon electricity imports are one of Singapore’s ‘Four Switches’, alongside natural gas, solar, and low-carbon solutions such as hydrogen, ammonia, and carbon capture and storage. The climate crisis requires us to quickly transition to low-carbon energy sources to avoid the devastating impact of rising temperatures. Decarbonising our power sector, which contributes 40% of current emissions, is key to realising Singapore’s commitment to achieving net zero emissions by 2050. Even as we take steps to secure a low-carbon energy supply, we will also need to safeguard our energy security and affordability. Navigating the energy trilemma successfully will be one of the defining policy challenges of our generation and hold far-reaching implications for our economy and society. In 2027, Singapore will take on the ASEAN Chairmanship. For the energy sectoral track, this will be an important opportunity to advance regional cooperation and drive progress on energy connectivity through the ASEAN Power Grid (APG). MTI’s Energy and Carbon (E&C) Cluster leads initiatives to reduce emissions and drive the transition to a low-carbon economy, while ensuring energy security and cost-competitiveness, and enabling Singapore to sustain economic growth. Officers in this cluster develop energy and carbon markets, plan energy infrastructure, build Singapore’s capabilities to harness low-carbon energy sources, and manage emission trajectories and carbon budgets. You will collaborate with stakeholders across Government, industry, and international partners to support sustainable economic transformation and green growth through strategic planning, policy analysis, and partnerships. The Energy Partnerships Division within the E&C cluster formulates Singapore’s energy policies and strategies, together with the Energy Market Authority, other Government agencies, academics, and private sector stakeholders. The Energy Partnerships Division takes the lead in: • Driving Singapore’s efforts to import and deploy renewable energy from the region to realise target to import around 6GW of low-carbon electricity by 2035 to decarbonise our power system. • Advancing energy collaboration with like-minded countries, international organisations, and think tanks to support Singapore and the region’s energy transition and energy resilience, and advocate for the APG. Your responsibilities include one or more of the following workstreams: • Drive G2G engagements, including through visits, working groups, dialogues, and instruments of collaboration such as agreements and memoranda of understanding, to advance the realisation of cross-border electricity trading projects. • Develop policies and strategies to advance the financing of cross-border power transmission infrastructure; the manufacturing, deployment and protection of subsea power cables; and the recognition of cross-border Renewable Energy Certificates to support the implementation of cross-border electricity imports projects. • Develop and strengthen partnerships with international thought leaders such as the International Energy Agency (IEA), International Renewable Energy Agency (IRENA), Multilateral Development Banks, as well as local partners such as the Energy Studies Institute and Asia Competitiveness Institute. • Foster collaborations with like-minded partners at: (1) Local events such as Singapore International Energy Week (SIEW) (2) Multilateral international platforms such as ASEAN Ministers on Energy Meeting (AMEM), G20, and Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC). We are looking for a self-motivated officer who possesses a keen desire to contribute to Singapore’s decarbonisation and energy transition jour

jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, Low-Carbon Energy Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 9 Jul 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. Energy security is an existential issue for Singapore. With the growing climate imperative, a global energy transition shifting to low-carbon fuels is underway. At the same time, geopolitical pressures are causing significant stresses in energy markets, leading to risks of supply disruptions and significant price volatility. The energy transition will be especially challenging for Singapore, given our scarce natural resources and limited renewable energy options. One of Singapore’s biggest challenges over the next few decades is to secure a low-carbon energy supply that is reliable and cost-competitive. The future of Singapore’s energy landscape depends on our ability to harness four switches – natural gas, solar, electricity imports, and low-carbon solutions such as hydrogen, ammonia and carbon capture and storage. MTI’s Energy and Carbon (E&C) Cluster leads initiatives to reduce emissions and drive the transition to a low-carbon economy, while ensuring energy security and cost-competitiveness, and enabling Singapore to sustain economic growth. Officers in this cluster develop energy and carbon markets, plan energy infrastructure, build Singapore’s capabilities to harness low-carbon energy sources, and manage emission trajectories and carbon budgets. You will collaborate with stakeholders across Government, industry, and international partners to support sustainable economic transformation and green growth through strategic planning, policy analysis, and partnerships. The Low-Carbon Energy division within the E&C Cluster takes the lead in: • Developing, implementing and monitoring various alternative energy sources for Singapore, including solar, hydrogen in its various forms, bioenergy, geothermal energy, as well as other promising low-carbon technologies. • Devising new policies to ensure that new energy solutions can be productively deployed and scaled sufficiently to help meet our 2050 net zero goals. • Overseeing issues relating to energy, grid, storage and carbon transformation Research & Development in Singapore. This could also entail the development of new climate technology pilot funding scheme, working directly with global climate tech solution providers, academics, and investors. Your responsibilities may include one or more of the following workstreams: • Develop strategies and policies to support the implementation of the Singapore National Hydrogen Strategy (published in October 2022), with a focus on: - Development of commercial industry projects to develop hydrogen supply chains in Singapore - Long-term infrastructure and land planning - Competitive monitoring - Advancement of international collaboration to leverage like-minded partners’ efforts to advance regional hydrogen supply chains and interoperable standards and regulations - Facilitate collaborations across key stakeholders, involving Government agencies, industry players, academia and international counterparts as appropriate • Develop and implement R&D strategies in areas that are critical to Singapore’s energy interests and supports the development of Singapore enterprise • Engage with new energy and decarbonization-focused companies and funds to track, monitor and make relevant recommendations with regards to the latest developments in the climate technology realm • Develop and implement communications and engagement plans with regard to Singapore’s alternative energy policies • Monitor developments in global energy landscape and analyse implications for domestic energy policies to enable these developments We are looking for matured, driven, self-aware, coachable and capable individuals who possess a strong desire to contribute and directly enable Singapore’s energy transition, thrive in a fast-moving environment across organisational boundaries and geographies, have a strong interest in alternative energy technologies and policies, and are equipped with the following qualities and competencies: •

jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, Carbon Markets Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 2 Jul 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. Climate change is one of the defining issues of our time. It is a complex global challenge that calls for concerted international action and collaboration between governments, industry and individuals. Singapore, as a low-lying city state, is vulnerable to the effects of climate change. Hence, we have to work together with the international community to address climate change and build a climate resilient future. At MTI, we seek to pursue sustainable development by balancing economic needs with environmental protection and social inclusion. The transition to a low-carbon and green economy will be especially challenging for Singapore, given our scarce natural resources and open economy. MTI’s Energy and Carbon (E&C) Cluster leads initiatives to reduce emissions and drive the transition to a low-carbon economy, while ensuring energy security and cost-competitiveness, and enabling Singapore to sustain economic growth. Officers in this cluster develop energy and carbon markets, plan energy infrastructure, build Singapore’s capabilities to harness low-carbon energy sources, and manage emission trajectories and carbon budgets. You will collaborate with stakeholders across Government, industry, and international partners to support sustainable economic transformation and green growth through strategic planning, policy analysis, and partnerships. The Carbon Markets Division within the E&C cluster drives the national development of non-energy mitigation measures and policies, such as carbon capture and storage (CCS) and high-quality carbon credits. We also work closely with agencies such as NCCS, MSE, and MOF, to formulate and coordinate strategies to ensure that Singapore is prepared for the impact of climate change - both domestically and internationally - while seizing new opportunities to create good jobs for Singaporeans. The team plays a critical role in charting the path forward for Singapore’s low-carbon transition. You will be part of a dynamic team responsible for formulating Singapore’s carbon mitigation strategies and policies, with a focus on carbon credits. You will examine carbon mitigation issues from multiple perspectives, assess the trade-offs, and work with other government agencies and relevant stakeholders. As the climate space is fast evolving, an officer may be deployed across different workstreams, based on the officer’s development and MTI’s organisational needs. Your responsibilities include one or more of the following work streams: • Develop and implement plans relating to carbon credits to meet our climate targets, while ensuring economic competitiveness. These include designing a robust international sourcing strategy to meet our climate targets; identifying and negotiating diverse new sources of high-quality Article 6-compliant carbon credits; collaborating with the private sector, international and multilateral organisations, and like-minded countries to secure access to Article 6-compliant carbon credits; catalysing and incentivising the development of new carbon credit project types; as well as growing the pipeline of high-integrity carbon credits projects for Singapore. • Develop and implement communications and engagement plans in collaboration with MTI corporate communication and engagement officers to support the implementation of the above climate strategies and policies, in close coordination with other agencies in the Singapore Government. We are looking for driven, capable and committed officers who possess a strong desire to contribute to Singapore’s carbon transition, thrive in a fast-paced and dynamic environment, are adept at working with multiple stakeholders and across organisational boundaries, and are equipped with the following qualities and competencies: • Experience in policy review and formulation • 0 to 9 years of relevant experience in climate change/sustainability areas in the public or private sector and/or non-government organis

jobs.title: Senior/Assistant Director, Carbon Strategy Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 2 Jul 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. Would you like to play a role in charting the path forward for Singapore’s low-carbon transition? This is the key responsibility of the Carbon Strategy Division within MTI’s Energy and Carbon cluster. Making the transition to a low-carbon economy in a secure and cost-competitive manner is critical for Singapore. With finite resources and an open economy, Singapore faces unique trade-offs and challenges in making this transition, requiring innovation in technology and policy to seize new opportunities in a carbon-constrained world. MTI’s Energy and Carbon (E&C) Cluster leads initiatives to reduce emissions and drive the transition to a low-carbon economy, while ensuring energy security and cost-competitiveness, and enabling Singapore to sustain economic growth. Officers in this cluster develop energy and carbon markets, plan energy infrastructure, build Singapore’s capabilities to harness low-carbon energy sources, and manage emission trajectories and carbon budgets. You will collaborate with stakeholders across Government, industry, and international partners to support sustainable economic transformation and green growth through strategic planning, policy analysis, and partnerships. The Carbon Strategy Division within the E&C Cluster takes the lead in cross-cutting policymaking to decarbonise our economy and meet our Net Zero targets, while maintaining our economic competitiveness. We are looking for driven and capable officers who (i) thrive in a fast-moving environment, (ii) have a keen interest in carbon, energy and economic transition policy, and (iii) are adept at working with multiple stakeholders within and outside the public sector. In particular, the officer will be responsible for one or more of the following workstreams: (a) Developing strategies for national mitigation measures, leveraging carbon tax implementation and the deployment of the Carbon Mitigation Programme Budget; (b) Leading the development of industry decarbonisation policies, through the development of effective incentive schemes and other levers; (c) Overseeing R&D efforts into longer-term industry decarbonisation pathways. The candidate should be equipped with the following competencies: • Possess strong analytical skills, and be able to provide policy recommendations by drawing on a wide variety of sources; • Have a keen interest in the development and deployment of policies and technologies that can support Singapore’s Net Zero ambitions; • Be very comfortable working with a wide range of stakeholders, both within and beyond the public service, which requires strong interpersonal, organisational and communication skills; • Possess the ability to work well both independently and as part of a team; • Be driven, meticulous and resourceful; • Candidates with relevant experience in policy-related work (particularly in the public sector), and sustainability and energy-related fields will be assessed favourably.

jobs.jd: Posting Date: 12 May 2026. Closing on 31 Aug 2026. You will be part of a team that oversees and stewards Singapore’s competition and consumer protection policies, which aim to foster a fair and competitive economic environment for businesses and consumers in Singapore. Promoting market competition and safeguarding consumer interests are key to upholding business and consumer confidence in the economy. With the emergence of new technologies and business models, changing consumer preferences and global economic shifts, our policies must remain relevant and effective so that Singapore continues to be a stable and attractive destination for business and investment, and consumers enjoy many choices and shop with peace of mind. By strengthening the twin pillars of competition and consumer protection, you will contribute to MTI’s objective to foster a vibrant, innovative and competitive economy. (b) Working closely with the Competition and Consumer Commission of Singapore (CCCS) and Consumers Association of Singapore (CASE) to review specific cases, including assessments of strategic mergers and business collaborations. (c) Performing deskhead functions for CCCS and CASE, including strategic reviews and driving organisational change. (d) Working with industry stakeholders to co-develop guidelines to raise business standards, in diverse sectors such as e-commerce, entertainment and retail. (e) Working with other MTI divisions, economic and regulatory agencies to address cross-cutting issues, such as cost-of-living and how competition can support economic/ industry development objectives. jobs.title: Senior Associate/Assistant Director (Finance)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 13 Jul 2026. Closing on 13 Aug 2026. The Accountant-General’s Department (AGD) plays an important role in making Singapore a better place through Finance. At AGD, we aim to be a future-ready leader in transforming Public Sector finance by putting finance at the heart of our decision-making. We do this through building a strong foundation in providing robust financial systems and processes that serve key stakeholders including our pensioners, vendors and the public sector. Our team of accounting professionals work at AGD and are deployed to the public agencies to deliver a wide range of financial and accounting-related services to the Public Service. Together we play a critical role in helping the Government achieve its fiscal and economic objectives through sound stewardship and governance. Prime Minister’s Office (PMO) Finance Group was formed with a strong One Public Service mindset to provide finance services across PMO agencies under its care, managing the respective agencies’ budgets to ensure financial accountability, fiscal sustainability, and the effective & efficient use of public resources. PMO Finance Group supports officers in meaningful finance work and are empowered to make improvements to processes and workflows, to formulate internal policies and best practices. You will be an AGD officer forward deployed to PMO Finance Group. Your responsibilities include: • Ensuring optimal provision, allocation and management of the agency’s budget to achieve Government and agency’s strategic objectives in a fiscally sustainable and value-for-money manner; • Reviewing and formulating of internal finance policies and procedures for efficient and effective financial administration and strong internal controls; • Ensuring all budgetary and financial matters are performed in line with all finance regulations and guidelines; • Supporting the Internal Approving Authority and Tender Boards by reviewing funding proposals and tender board submissions to ensure that projects undertaken are operationally and financially viable Functional/Technical Competencies • Tertiary qualifications in Accountancy / Business / Finance / related disciplines • CA Singapore certification is preferred • Minimum 3 years of related working experience in similar positions is preferred • Ability to proactively develop and manage relationships with various key stakeholders • Ability to put forth sound proposals and recommendations for management decision-making Generic Competencies • Possess strong analytical, critical thinking, numerical and problem-solving skills • Good time management skills and able to multi-task and prioritize key deliverables • Meticulous and a good team player who is able to work well independently • Strong communication and interpersonal skills You will be notified whether you are shortlisted within 4 weeks of the closing date of this job posting.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Strategic Planning)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 10 Jul 2026. Closing in 4 day(s). Singapore is internationally renowned for our transport infrastructure and for being a global aviation and maritime hub. At the Ministry of Transport (MOT), we spearhead developments in our air, land and sea transport sectors to advance Singapore’s economic competitiveness and Singaporeans’ quality of life. We seek dynamic, analytical and committed individuals to join our able MOT team in realising our vision of a global interconnected transport hub. If you share our vision and possess the qualities we seek, do join us! You will be part of the Strategic Planning team within MOT’s Strategic Planning & Organisational Transformation Division. The job will require you to: a) Plan, coordinate and implement the MOT Family strategic planning agenda by collaborating with senior leaders, MOT’s Divisions and our Statutory Boards as well as other government agencies to align with the Whole-of-Government (WoG) objectives and outcomes. b) Plan, review and coordinate the annual strategic planning, resource planning and reporting cycle to ensure accountability, drive alignment and achievement of WoG and MOT Family strategic objectives and priorities. c) Analyse data and information to identify patterns, trends, outliers, insights and threats to better support evidence-based decisions and policy development, as well as undertake foresight-related environment scanning and develop a portfolio of up-to-date foresight capabilities and products. d) Coordinate and track the development and implementation of policies and programmes by business units to ensure that resources, capabilities and structures enable us to deliver well. e) Oversee Ministry Family Parliamentary Matters with the objective of enhancing parliamentary engagement and coordination, support coherence in policy positions and narratives, and strengthen institutional capability across the MOT on Parliamentary matters. You should be a Singapore citizen with at least 4 years of relevant working experience in a policy/corporate/strategic planning role. You should be results-oriented, highly self-motivated, resilient and possess the ability to connect effectively with diverse stakeholders both at the individual and group level. You should also be a good team player, possessing excellent analytical, communications and organisational skills, with the ability to distil complex information into actionable insights. Meticulous attention to details is necessary as well as the ability to multi-task and work well under tight deadlines. Job holders would have the opportunity to work in different or multiple sections within the Division, taking into consideration the officers’ competencies and development needs, as well as the needs of the Division.

As an Assistant Director in the Research Team, you will lead a team to work closely with MSF and ECDA Divisions, other government departments, relevant research agencies/institutes and preschools to inform evidence based early childhood policies, programmes and practices. Research initiatives include, but are not limited, to the evaluation of innovative pilots and programmes, and implementing measures of holistic child outcomes • Provide secretariat support for inter-agency meetings relating to research. • Undertake any other additional tasks and duties as assigned. You should be a highly motivated self-starter who values teamwork and excellence. An ideal candidate should possess the following: Education Qualifications - A relevant tertiary qualification e.g., social sciences, education, psychology, economics, sociology, public policy or field with strong research methodology components. Relevant Experience - Experience leading and managing a team. - At least 4-5 years of experience in research, evaluation of social programmes or related areas preferred. - Demonstrated experience in stakeholder engagement across diverse institutional contexts (academic, government, practitioner communities). - Experience in early childhood development, education policy, or social policy research would be advantageous. - Proficiency in Microsoft Office (PowerPoint, Excel, etc). Skillsets in data management and familiarity with statistical analysis software such as SPSS, R, STATA would be advantageous. jobs.jd: Posting Date: 10 Jul 2026. Closing in 5 day(s). [What the role is] 1. Translate data and research into actionable and meaningful insights that would guide both internal teams and external industry stakeholders, with the objective of driving data-driven decision making across the organisation. 3. Liaise and coordinate with information agencies across Whole-of-Government (WOG). 4. Fulfilment of analytics deliverables and where applicable, data partnerships with external stakeholders. Lead, manage and drive data cleaning, exploration, ingestion, compilation and analysis to derive insights and support business outcomes related to STB’s key tourism performance measures including Visitor Arrivals, Tourism Receipts, Hotels Stats. 2. Direct and coordinate data collection and analytics strategies, analysis of data collected from both quantitative and qualitative methods to come up with evidence-based insights that will help drive business outcomes. This includes performing data wrangling and transformation to prepare raw data into analysis-ready formats, manage data ingestion, applying statistical/analytical methods to extract insights from data, utilising data visualisation tools to communicate findings effectively. 3. Deliver tourism performance insights to stakeholders. 4. Monitor and lead a team on regular reporting, publications and data requests from stakeholders and maintain key databases from which analysis is drawn from. 5. Oversee all aspects of project management, ranging from contract and vendor management to end-to-end data operations, including fulfilling data custodian role of ensuring good quality data, developing and/or enhancing existing datasets and dashboards. 6. Collaborate with cross-functional teams, such as product managers, data scientists/engineers and business stakeholders, to integrate and deploy models and dashboards into analytics platforms. 7. Provide data advisory support to internal (within STB) and external (WOG, industry) stakeholders as relevant. This may include the assessment of economic contributions from major tourism projects. 8. Collaborate with internal and external stakeholders to gather intelligence on emerging issues and provide actionable insights. 9. Provide effective leadership and guidance to the analytics team in the areas of operational data analytics including data manipulation to extract insights, synthesis of results and ensuring data accuracy and alignment with organisational outcomes and communicating the findings to stakeholders. 10. Grow a high-performance analytics team. [What we are looking for] a) Trained in Data Analytics, Applied Mathematics, Statistics, Economics or a related quantitative discipline b) A minimum of 8 years of relevant experience in data analytics, interpretation and visualisation, with at least 3 years’ experience in leading teams. c) Strong foundational knowledge of data analytics methodologies and analytics skills, with a good eye for detail and possess an aptitude/experience in solving analytical problems using quantitative approaches. d) Good mastery of market research methodologies. e) Advantageous if you have experience in research applications in a client-side context. f) Working knowledge in statistical modelling and forecasting. g) Proven track record and hands on experience in integrating and synthesising research and data across multiple sources to derive meaningful conclusions. h) Proven track record in engaging different groups of stakeholders (within and outside of the organisation). i) Fluency in data manipulation to extract insights, strategic direction and narrative, forecasting and project future visitor demand. j) Strong business orientation with the ability to connect understanding from data to key business outcomes. k
