The Rehabilitation and Protection Group (RPG) undertakes statutory functions in the rehabilitation of adult and youth offenders, and the protection of vulnerable children and families. Our mission is to empower individuals and families to break cycles of abuse, neglect and offending. As part of the RPG family, the Prevention and Operations Policy Division (POP) is responsible for the overall prevention efforts, strategic planning and operations policy. The Tech, Data and Innovation (TDI) branch within POP is responsible for data management and analyses, ops-tech and digital transformation, and innovation for the whole of RPG. You will be part of the Data Operations and Management (DOM) section that is dedicated to managing and leveraging data, systems and technology to drive operational excellence and strategic decision-making for the RPG, with focus on Protective Services (PSV). As a Senior / Assistant Director, you will lead the DOM team, reporting to the Deputy Director to oversee critical data operations and management functions that support RPG's mission. Responsibilities: • Strategic Leadership and Team Management: Lead and supervise a team of data analysts and officers, providing mentorship and guidance to build data competencies within the team. You will be responsible for ensuring the team delivers high-quality data outputs that meet the needs of RPG operations, service planning, and strategic decision-making. • Data Operations Excellence: Oversee end-to-end data management processes for key PSV datasets, including child protection, adult protection, adoption operations, and services overseen by PSV. You will ensure data accuracy, completeness and timeliness across data collection and reporting processes, whilst maintaining compliance with MSF's data governance policies and practices. • Stakeholder Engagement and Project Management: Manage data requests, prioritization trade-offs and sequencing of data initiatives for PSV. You will engage pillar directors and work closely with pillar teams to understand business context and data requirements, serving as a trusted partner in translating operational needs into data solutions. • Analytical Rigour and Decision Support: Perform sophisticated data analysis to identify trends, correlations and anomalies in RPG service data. Provide analytical support for management queries, policy questions and operational planning needs to enable data-driven decision-making within RPG. You will be expected to present complex data insights in clear, accessible formats for diverse audiences, from frontline staff to senior management. • Data Quality and Governance: You will take on the role as Domain Data Steward, to spearhead data quality initiatives by implementing data completeness checks, developing data entry principles, and working with pillar Service Planning teams to create data dictionaries for key reported data. You will lead the team in tracking data quality metrics and addressing data management issues across the data lifecycle. • Innovation and Continuous Improvement: Drive the development of self-service capabilities through data repositories, Case management system live dashboards, and PowerBI dashboards to reduce repetitive data requests and empower teams with real-time insights. You will identify opportunities to streamline data processes, enhance efficiency and improve the overall data maturity of RPG. • Any other duties assigned by the Reporting Officer and Director. Technical/Functional Competencies: • Bachelor’s degree in a relevant field, such as Social Sciences and Data Science. • Minimum of 10 years of experience in data management and analysis and minimum of 5 years’ experience in leading a team. • Expertise in processing data using Microsoft Excel and any other data management software. • Certifications and experience in using data visualization tools such as PowerBI and/or Tableau. • Ability to code and process data u

jobs.title: Market Director, Communities & Networks, Singapore Global Network (Americas)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 5 May 2026. About EDB The Singapore Economic Development Board (EDB), a government agency under the Ministry of Trade and Industry, is responsible for strategies that enhance Singapore’s position as a global centre for business, innovation, and talent. We undertake investment promotion and industry development, and work with international businesses, both foreign and local, by providing information, connection to partners and access to government incentives for their investments. Our mission is to create sustainable economic growth, with vibrant business and good job opportunities for Singapore. For more information on EDB, please visit www.edb.gov.sg About SGN The Singapore Global Network (SGN) is a division under EDB and focuses on building a strong ecosystem of overseas family, friends and fans of Singapore. This includes growing a network of individuals whose skills, affinity and influence could strengthen Singapore’s business and talent ecosystem, along with enhancing international mindshare of Singapore as a vibrant global hub in Asia. The Communities & Networks team is responsible for driving region specific efforts to grow and engage SGN’s global networks. The global team comprises officers who develop and execute strategies either from the Singapore office or one of SGN’s overseas offices. For more information on SGN, please visit www.singaporeglobalnetwork.gov.sg We’re looking for a Market Director in Americas to grow and activate SGN’s network across the region. In this role, you will work closely with colleagues in EDB and across government, as well as in-market partners ranging from industry to community organisations to develop and execute market development strategies for our activities. This role includes a probationary period to assess performance and suitability, after which the role will be based in the Americas. Your responsibilities include but are not limited to: Planning and executing digital and in-person strategies to grow, engage, and foster community within SGN’s network of members in Americas. This includes designing and delivering both one-to-one and one-to-many engagements. Driving market strategy by identifying opportunities and analysing ground sentiments in the Americas, translating insights into actionable strategies that support SGN’s objectives and broader national priorities. Identifying, developing and maintaining a network of corporate / community partners who can achieve SGN’s business outcomes. Collaborating with other EDB divisions and Singapore Government agencies to connect global networks with economic and national priorities. A university degree in any discipline with at least 5 years of working experience in marketing, international relations or community building. Relevant overseas working experiences will be a bonus. Strong interest in current affairs and knowledge of latest developments in Singapore and trending global issues. Proven ability to build, manage, and sustain relationships with diverse stakeholders. Energetic, sociable, empathetic and comfortable engaging a cross-cultural audience. Excellent interpersonal and communication skills, in both written and spoken English. Additional language skills (e.g. Mandarin, Bahasa Melayu, etc.) would be a bonus. Exceptional organisational and project management skills, able to manage multiple complex projects concurrently. Highly adaptable, able to work independently and in cross-functional teams. Detail-oriented, with a high level of accuracy and quality in deliverables. Ability to travel overseas for work. Prior professional experience working or based in the United States, with a strong understanding of the U.S. landscape, culture, and stakeholder environment would be a bonus. Singaporean.

jobs.title: Deputy Director (Investigation), ECDA/RL
jobs.inst: Ministry Of Social And Family Development
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 11 Jun 2026. Closing in 3 day(s). The Regulation & Licensing Division (RL) in the Early Childhood Development Agency ensures quality standards of care and education in preschools (e.g. child care centres, kindergartens), through the enforcement of regulatory requirements under the Early Childhood Development Centres (ECDC) Act (2017) and Regulations (2018) on preschools. This includes the licensing of centres, investigations into reports of incidents in preschools, and handling public feedback on preschool services. The Deputy Director (Investigation) leads and oversees three teams of officers in the Investigation Branch, who conduct investigations into incidents occurring in preschool settings. This could include cases of alleged child mismanagement, and other breaches of the ECDC Act, Regulations, and Code of Practice. Key Responsibilities: • Assess whether allegations should be investigated, assign cases across investigation officers. Guide and supervise officers in investigation work, on recommendations for interventions/penalties, and on updates to Senior Management and Political Office Holders. • Calibrate the appropriate course of regulatory actions to take against non-compliant centres. • Work with officers on appeals by operators against the regulatory actions taken and by parents who are unhappy with the outcome of feedback/investigations, by re-looking into the case, grounds of appeal and ensuring fair and defensible decisions are in made. • Liaise with operators, educators, parents, government agencies, and members of public for sensitive cases, ensuring that replies are informative, clear and empathic. Provide support to officers in managing challenging operators and parents, and input to media enquiries/media releases on cases. • Develop and implement investigation protocols and procedures, including measures to improve efficiency in the Investigation Branch. • Lead workgroups to improve ways to improve child safety in preschools (e.g. mandatory CCTV policy). • A good degree; preferably, in early childhood, education or social sciences. • At least 10 years of working experience leading officers/team; preferably with experience in investigative and/or operational work. • Knowledge of early childhood education practices and regulations, or able to build up a good understanding of them through on-the-job training. • Tenacious and resilient. Able to take negative reactions from unhappy stakeholders, able to work fast and hard over prolonged periods, where necessary. • A good team-player. • Able to be firm and fair, while being service-oriented, and to communicate clearly and engage effectively. • Nurturing and focused on people development.

jobs.title: Associate/Assistant Director, Asset Mgt & Funds (Funds Team) (1-Yr Contract)

The team monitors global trends and developments in the asset management industry and funds ecosystem to tap areas of growth in the sector. For this role, you will be responsible for the administration of MAS’ grants and tax incentives for funds, while also contributing to the implementation and refinement of the portal system currently under development. You will be in frequent contact with industry players to review tax incentive and grant applications, evaluate KPIs and handle exceptions. You are expected to have good written communication and analytical skills. Prior experience in supporting the development of new technology systems or experience within the asset management industry is not required but will be an advantage. Knowledge in MS Word and MS Excel Good verbal and written communication skills Good interpersonal skills and strong team player Careful and meticulous worker Independent worker who is motivated to drive projects and tasks to successful completion You will be working in a fast-paced environment that will require the ability to manage multiple priorities and needs of stakeholders, as well as the agility to respond to changes and developments You will be working in a fast-paced environment that would require the ability to manage multiple priorities and needs of stakeholders, as well as the agility to respond to changes and developments. jobs.title: Senior Assistant Director, Digital Systems & Capabilities, Registries Cluster
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 11 Jun 2026. Closing on 02 Aug 2026. Senior Assistant Director, Digital Capabilities and Systems, Registries of Patents, Designs and Plant Varieties (3-years contract). There is possibility to be emplaced on a permanent position. You will play a key role in shaping and governing Singapore’s national IP registry digital platform, ensuring it is reliable, efficient, user-centric and future-ready. As Systems Lead (Deputy System Owner), you will own and drive the roadmap, delivery and continuous improvement of systems that support end-to-end IP registration processes. You will enable and support the digital transformation of Registry operations through effective system design, delivery and optimisation. The platform supports a high volume of IP transactions and is critical to Singapore’s innovation ecosystem, serving businesses, agents and international stakeholders. Great ideas do not discriminate, and anyone can have them. Given the right protection and support, a great idea can become a power brand, a transformative technology, an innovative process, or design, or even a song or story that moves one’s soul. The Intellectual Property Office of Singapore works with creators, innovators and innovative companies to catalyse the ideas of today and transform ideas into the assets of tomorrow. We leverage our deep technical, legal and business expertise to help innovative enterprises grow using their intangible assets, including IP. The Registries of Patents, Designs and Plant Varieties (PDPVP) supports IPOS’ vision by building and maintaining a top-ranked IP regime in the world. Key Responsibilities: System Ownership and Platform Development • As a deputy system owner, you will oversee the governance, delivery, stability and continuous enhancement of the Registry digital platform for IP filing, IP search, IP portfolio management and IP application processing. • Partner closely with IT department and vendors to lead and drive system delivery, and ensure solutions meet performance, scalability, integration and compliance requirements. Business Process Enablement • Support Registry operations in process optimisation by identifying and implementing system-enabled improvements. • Provide system-driven insights and solutions to address operational pain points, in partnership with process owners. Team Leadership and Capability Building • Lead and manage a team of system administrators and business process improvement specialists. • Build and lead a high-performing systems team. • Set clear direction to strengthen system ownership capabilities within the team and drive a culture of accountability, continuous improvements and user-focused delivery. Stakeholder Engagement • Collaborate with a wide range of internal and external stakeholders to identify opportunities and operational needs, co-create solutions and manage relationships that span technical, operational and policy dimensions. Change Management and Adoption • Lead change management initiatives to ensure new processes and digital tools are successfully adopted across all user groups – Registry operations and customers. • Monitor system usage and feedback to continuously improve user experience and effectiveness. Write to us if you have/are: • 7 to 10 years of experience in digital transformation leadership with proven track record of leading successful large-scale system implementations. • 3 to 5 years of leadership and supervisory experience in managing team of system administrators and business process improvement specialists, with demonstrated change management capabilities. • Experience as a system owner or deputy system owner for enterprise or transactional digital platform • Basic understanding of system architecture and IT governance. • Strong analytical and problem-solving capabilities with excellent written and verbal communication skills. • (Optional) Background in intellectual property, especially in Patents and Designs, will be advantageous. • (Optional) Agi

jobs.title: Senior Assistant Director, Talent Acquisition & Talent Management, HRD
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 29 Apr 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. The Senior Assistant Director, Talent Acquisition & Talent Management oversees MOM’s talent function by leading two teams – Talent Acquisition and Talent Management. In this role, you will ensure we hire the right people and enable our employees to grow and develop in their careers. You will manage day-to-day operations across both teams while working with senior leadership to support the organisation's hiring and talent development needs. The position involves coordinating recruitment activities at eth upstream and subsequently employee development programmes to create a congruent and progressive approach to managing our workforce. You will report directly to the Deputy Director of Human Resources and work regularly with department heads across the organisation. Talent Acquisition • Oversee the Talent Acquisition team across all recruitment activities, including working with departments to understand staffing requirements, managing interview processes, sourcing candidates who possess the right competencies and align with organisational values, and advancing our employer brand to attract quality applicants. • Design good candidate stakeholder experiences throughout the recruitment journey, from initial engagement through to successful onboarding of new hires. • Drive continuous efforts to improve efficiency and effectiveness by optimising and re-imagining processes, whilst ensuring compliance with proper hiring procedures and employment regulations. • Participate in outreach activities and promote MOM as an employer of choice. Talent Management • Oversee the Talent Management team across all talent management matters, including talent identification, engagement, development, and deployment. • Collaborate closely with senior management to develop robust succession planning for key roles, establish clear career progression pathways as well as devise a structured approach to developing and deploying talents. • Establish comprehensive talent management metrics to measure programme effectiveness, track outcomes, and identify areas for continuous improvement. • Partner with the IT department to develop a talent management system to streamline manual tracking processes, and to bridge the gap between talent management business needs and technical requirements. • At least 5 years of HR experience in the public service, preferably in human capital domain areas such as talent acquisition, talent management and development. • People management experience or demonstrated deep interest and passion for developing talent, with ability to engage diverse stakeholders of different seniority effectively. • Resourceful self-starter with a learner mindset and the resilience to drive meaningful organisational change. • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills with the ability to synthesise information from multiple sources and draw sound, evidence-based inferences.

jobs.title: Assistant Director (Tech Strategy)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 7 Jul 2026. Closing in 4 day(s). Singapore is internationally renowned for our transport infrastructure and for being a global aviation and maritime hub. At the Ministry of Transport (MOT), we spearhead developments in our air, land and sea transport sectors to advance Singapore’s economic competitiveness and Singaporeans’ quality of life. We seek dynamic, analytical and committed individuals to join our able MOT team in realising our vision of a global interconnected transport hub. If you share our vision and possess the qualities we seek, do join us! As Assistant Director of Technology Strategy (AD-Tech Strat) in the Technology and Data Policy Department, you will be supporting the Deputy Director to advance Singapore’s transport and connectivity agenda through science, technology, and data innovation. You will contribute to policy development, strategic planning, and cross-agency coordination, while managing programmes that address emerging technological trends, and opportunities and risks. Key responsibilities include: 1. Policy Development & Strategic Planning • Formulate science and technology (S&T) and data policies aligned with national transport and connectivity goals. • Draft components of the MOT Family S&T Frameworks, programmes and plans, including supporting MOT's R&D agenda. • Plan and conduct workshops using futures thinking and horizon scanning methodologies to identify emerging issues, opportunities and risks. 2. Technology Evaluation & Risk Analysis • Monitor and assess emerging technologies (e.g., AI, cybersecurity, digital infrastructure) and their relevance to the transport sector. • Contribute to risk assessments and help identify regulatory gaps or strategic vulnerabilities concerning new scientific and technological advancements. 3. Stakeholder Engagement & Coordination • Liaise with other MOT HQ divisions, and Statutory Boards (SBs) to engage external partners like research institutions and industry ecosystem players to gather inputs and align policy directions. • Coordinate working groups and inter-agency meetings to support collaborative initiatives. 4. Capability Building & Transformation • Assist in implementing capability development programmes to enhance S&T planning and portfolio management, and data literacy across the MOT family. • Support transformation efforts in the SBs harness S&T and data effectively. Qualifications • Tertiary qualification in engineering, science and technology, public policy or other relevant fields. • Postgraduate qualification is an advantage Experience & Technical Skills • 3 years’ experience in public policy, S&T strategy, or data governance • Strong analytical and writing skills, with the ability to synthesize complex information. • Familiarity with futures thinking, innovation ecosystems, and public sector transformation. • Knowledge in public infrastructure, transport and engineering sector. Core Competencies • Strong communication (written & verbal), stakeholder management, and cross agency coordination skills. • Ability to manage multi stakeholder programmes with tight deadlines. • Sound judgement, attention to detail, and high standards of governance.

jobs.title: Senior/ Assistant Director, South Asia, Middle East and Africa Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 12 Jan 2026. Closing on 31 Dec 2026. The Trade and International Economics Division (TIER) drives the formulation of Singapore's foreign economic policy and safeguards Singapore's economic interests in the international economic arena. The South Asia, Middle East and Africa Division (SAMEA) is a division within TIER that develops and implements policies aimed at growing Singapore's economic relations with over 70 countries in these regions. The job scope includes driving initiatives such as bilateral platforms, pursuing economic agreements, and staffing of Ministerial visits. • Formulate policies to grow two-way trade, investment and people flows with India • Negotiate and implement trade agreements and MOUs with India, such as the India Singapore Comprehensive Economic Cooperation Agreement, MOUs in Semiconductor, and Science & Technology • Develop and implement strategies that increase collaboration with India in new areas to create downstream opportunities for Singapore companies • Promote Singapore’s economic engagement of India through supporting regular visits, meetings and cooperation between Singapore and India • A related field of study in disciplines such as, but not limited to, Economics, Political Science, International Relations • An interest in economic development, international relations and the emerging markets, particularly in South Asia, and/or Middle East • Enjoy a fast-paced, challenging environment • Good writing, analytical and communication skills • Independent, energetic and resourceful

jobs.title: Assistant Director/Manager (Analytics)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 16 Jul 2026. Closing on 01 Aug 2026. The Municipal Services Office (MSO) was set up in Oct 2014 to improve the coordination, delivery and quality of municipal services by Government and Town Councils. These services include the upkeep and improvement of common areas in public and private estates, and the upgrading or provision of facilities and infrastructure. The next bound of municipal service delivery involves: i. ramping up of forward intelligence capabilities to enable foresight planning and support the development of pre-emptive operations; ii. deploying data focused tools (e.g. dashboards and regular data reports) to increase the productivity of officers across the municipal sector and to support them in making data-driven policy decisions; iii. humanising the digitalisation of government services (e.g. OneService Chatbot) for an optimised, resident-centric experience. You will be part of the Analytics Team within the Planning Group in MSO, and your duties include: • Wrangling the data using advanced quantitative methods/techniques to discover insights and identify potential areas to deep dive into. This includes mining large volumes of structured / unstructured data to discover interesting and relevant insights, patterns, or relationships aimed at developing effective policy solutions. • Oversee the Municipal Services Dashboard (MSDB), a series of dashboards used by various municipal stakeholders. This includes, but is not limited to, management of contracts, budget and user accounts, as well as working with the appointed vendor on the maintenance and operation of the MSDB. • Assist in product-managing the OneService Chatbot, a recently launched AI Chatbot on WhatsApp and Telegram for residents to provide municipal feedback. Have the chance to work alongside different GovTech teams to roll out new Chatbot features as well as implementing publicity campaigns to achieve widespread adoption of the Chatbot. • Oversee and maintain a repository of municipal feedback data (more than 1.3 million new data per year!) with the appointed vendor. Value-add to the team’s collective output with strong knowledge management, particularly in the upkeep of metadata and ETL / analytical scripts. • [Data Management & Analysis - Scripting] Able to write scripts for data preparation and analysis. • [Data Management & Analysis – Exploration Analysis] Able to explore and analyse work on datasets using modern analysis tools and modern programming methodologies ie Python, R and/or SQL etc. • [Policy & Planning - Data-driven Policy Analysis] Able to utilise data analytics (directly/indirectly) to support policy analysis and simulate the impact of different policy options. • [Thinking Clearly and Making Sound Judgement & Policy & Planning - Deep Critical Thinking] – Able to distil critical issues, sense-make from a wide range of information, understand current realities and make sound, timely decision on plan of action. • At least 2 years of experience in data analysis and machine learning (e.g. use of decision trees, logistic regressions, neural networks) • Familiarity with data warehousing • Candidates without the relevant work experience but possess the required qualities are welcome to apply.

Specifically, you will supervise the team to ensure the following key deliverables: • Implementation of a modernised helpdesk system with Gen AI functionalities • Leveraging on new technologies (e.g. LLM, Gen AI, etc.) to promote self-help for the helpdesk system • Conduct data analysis (using IT tools) on past helpdesk queries and statistics to gain insights into users’ behaviour and events and identify areas of improvement to operations • Documentation of processes and creation of knowledge base articles to support financial processes and accounting-related service requests to improve efficiency • Exploring and adopting of emerging tools, including pilot testing and deployment • Monitoring and evaluating on the effectiveness of implementation of new technologies/tools • Assisting on work on any other ad-hoc projects or tasks assigned • Tertiary qualifications in Finance, Business, IT or Systems related field with a minimum of 9 years of experience • CA Singapore certification is preferred • Keen interest in Ops-Tech; • Prior experience in machine learning and/or Gen AI is a plus; • Good understanding of Finance and Procurement processes as well as system implementation; • Good critical thinking, problem-solving and analytical skills; • Time management skills to multi-task and prioritize key deliverable ; • Meticulous and a good team player who can work well independently and with various stakeholders; • Good communication and presentation skills You will be notified whether you have been shortlisted within 4 weeks from the closing date of this job posting.

jobs.title: Programme Head (Digital Engagement & Adoption)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 17 Feb 2026. Closing on 17 Sep 2026. IMDA’s vision is to build a dynamic digital economy, and a cohesive digital society, driven by an exceptional Infocomm and media ecosystem. In line with Singapore’s social compact, IMDA seeks to help every citizen (from the young to the old) thrive in the digital age and feel that he or she can engage in activities online in a safe, inclusive and empowering way. IMDA works actively with the public, people, and private sectors (3P) to dream, design, and deliver so as to strengthen the digital dimension of Singapore’s social compact, or Singapore’s digital future. This way, no citizen will be left behind and we can move forward as one nation. Role Overview We are looking for a Programme Head to lead the design, development, and delivery of digital engagement and adoption programmes that promote digital confidence and lifelong learning. The Programme Head will support the Senior Programme Head in driving community partnership development and programme development, ensuring successful execution across assigned regions whilst fostering strong relationships with Grassroots Organisations (GROs), Grassroots Leaders (GRLs), and local community stakeholders. You will help ensure that programmes meet the needs of target communities and are delivered effectively through strategic partnerships. Responsibilities Programme Design & Development Assist in the design and development of digital engagement and adoption programmes aimed at enhancing digital confidence and long-term adoption among diverse community groups. Help design and implement community partnership strategies for People and Local partners, including GROs and GRLs, to build and nurture relationships that support digital engagement and adoption initiatives at the community level. Help track and ensure that programmes are delivered effectively, fostering engagement and participation from community members to achieve programme outcomes. Programme Delivery & Coordination Coordinate the delivery of digital engagement programmes across assigned regions, ensuring programmes are tailored to meet local needs and are effectively implemented. Provide support for People sector, Grassroots Organisations, Grassroots Leaders, and community partners in the field to ensure successful programme execution. Help track and monitor the engagement and mobilisation of People and local partners to ensure successful programme delivery. Partnership Development & Stakeholder Engagement Assist in acquiring new community partners and renewing existing partnerships. Build and manage relationships with community stakeholders, including grassroots networks and local organisations, to ensure strong collaboration and support for programme delivery. Monitoring & Evaluation Monitor programme performance, including participation and outcomes. Provide feedback to senior leaders on programme effectiveness and areas for improvement. Collect community feedback and use it to refine programme strategies and improve delivery. Stakeholder Support Provide on-the-ground support to People and Local community stakeholders, ensuring they are equipped with the necessary tools and resources to implement programmes successfully. Help foster a collaborative environment where stakeholders are motivated and aligned with programme objectives. Requirements Minimum of 3-5 years of experience in programme management, programme coordination, community engagement, or digital adoption roles. Experience in working with grassroots organisations, community networks, and managing community outreach initiatives. Strong communication, organisational, and relationship management skills with the ability to engage a wide range of stakeholders and build partnerships. Ability to manage multiple projects and programmes simultaneously while meeting deadlines and objectives. Experience in monitoring programme performance and engaging stakeholders to ensure programme success. #LI-IT2

jobs.title: Senior/ Assistant Director, (Intelligence & Futures), Futures, Strategy & Plans
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 3 Jul 2026. Closing on 31 Aug 2026. Position Overview: As a dynamic and forward-thinking nation, Singapore strives to stay ahead of the curve by exploring new economic opportunities and understanding emerging risks. The ideal candidate should have a strong interest in the rigorous analysis of geoeconomic trends and conducting in-depth research to support emerging economic sectors. In this role, you will play a pivotal part in shaping Singapore’s economic strategy and inform leadership decision-making by assessing and analysing opportunities and risks. Through methodologies such as scenario planning, we help decision-makers make sense of an uncertain and volatile operating environment and question underlying assumptions. You will gain a mountain-top view of Singapore’s economic priorities and challenges and contribute to shaping its future by identifying and developing new growth areas. You will also build a broad skillset – including stakeholder management, research and analysis, and systems thinking. Key Responsibilities Some of the key roles played by the officer are: • Regular horizon scanning to gather economic intelligence and better understand our evolving operating context • Conducting in-depth research and analysis of geoeconomic trends, new growth areas, and emerging technologies, and translating insights into high-level recommendations • Conduct scenario planning to make sense of possible future trajectories and better prepare the system for shocks and surprises • Develop and refine the processes, tools and methodologies for horizon scanning and futures thinking, leveraging AI and digital tools to enhance the team’s capabilities • Collaborate with relevant ministries (e.g. PMO, MDDI) and agencies (e.g. EDB, EnterpriseSG) to provide strategic foresight • Support the work of key Ministerial and MTI management-level platforms. Requirements You should apply for this position if you have a passion and interest in geoeconomic issues, and have the following attributes: • Excellent communication and interpersonal skills, with the ability to write and present well • Strong ability to think deeply and broadly through different subjects, and to make connections between them – one of the major demands in this role is to frame and synthesise complex issues and differing opinions. • Strong analytical skills, including the ability to source and synthesise disparate trends to provide meaningful insights (creative storytelling using often imperfect data). • Fast learner with a curious mind, committed to driving innovation and improving efficiency • Self-starter with the tenacity to clarify ideas and create structure in situations that are novel, uncertain, and ambiguous

jobs.title: Senior Assistant Director/ Assistant Director (One Health Office)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 16 Jul 2026. Closing on 28 Jul 2026. The CDA is a new government agency that is being established by the Ministry of Health (MOH) to oversee Singapore’s communicable disease preparedness, prevention and control, surveillance, risk assessment and outbreak response. The One Health Office’s role is to drive and improve One Health coordination and collaboration at the national level with key stakeholders. As Senior/ Assistant Director for One Health Office of the Communicable Diseases Agency (CDA), you will work with the Director and Deputy Director/ One Health Office to establish and build Singapore’s One Health capabilities. If you are passionate about One Health, collaboration across sectors to achieve common objectives, and starting initiatives from ground-up, this may be the position for you. You will be working in a dynamic environment that would require the ability to manage multiple priorities and stakeholders at the same time. Implement, and review the overall framework for One Health plans of actions for communicable diseases to safeguard public health and reduce incidence and impact of endemic and emerging diseases in Singapore. Implement national One Health research and surveillance programmes of endemic and emerging communicable diseases of One Health priority, which will inform prevention and control policies and strategies. Implement capability development efforts in One Health workforce core competencies. Provide desktop research and technical advice on specified One Health related issues and matters,. Collaborate with One Health partners to spearhead the implementation and monitoring of progress. Stay abreast of trends, best practices, and directions relating to One Health Tertiary education in areas related to medicine, veterinary medicine, environmental health, public health, epidemiology. Relevant postgraduate qualifications (MSc, PhD) or certifications in public health or One Health related disciplines are advantageous. At least 5 years of relevant experience in managing public health or One Health related programmes and initiatives. Possess managerial and organizational skills to manage collaborative activities with a team of public health and One Health professionals effectively. Possess basic public or One Health expertise and knowledge to interact effectively with local and international experts and assess scientific evidence. Possess strong analytical capabilities and capacity to think about issues comprehensively and systematically, to overcome barriers and achieve One Health actionable strategies. Possess strong interpersonal, communication, and stakeholder management skills to drive effective collaborations with stakeholders within and outside of the agency. As part of the shortlisting process for this role, candidates may be required to complete a medical declaration and/or undergo further assessment.

jobs.title: Senior Assistant Director (TTI), STPD
jobs.inst: Ministry of Sustainability and the Environment
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 4 May 2026. Closing on 16 Aug 2026. The role is for a Senior Assistant Director in the Science and Technology Planning Division (STPD). You will support the strategy and master-planning work in Science & Technology (S&T), particularly efforts to translate R&D output into industrial impact. These efforts range from overseeing the Research, Innovation and Enterprise (RIE) Urban Solutions and Sustainability (USS) Domain funds, working with MSE Family (MSE-F) agencies to develop S&T masterplans with a good understanding of the industries and economics, reviewing RIE R&D proposals to enhance the translation proposition and to strengthen local sustainability industry in addressing national imperatives. STPD uses S&T as an enabler to support the overall MSE vision of creating a sustainable, resource-resilient and climate-resilient Singapore. S&T topics are broad, ranging from water resources, resource circularity, climate adaptation to food security. You will be part of a dynamic team that drives the strategic translation of MSE-F research output into commercial and operational applications, addressing Singapore’s national imperatives. As a member of the STPD team, your job scope includes: •Lead research translation strategy development through comprehensive reviews of business landscape, market trends and technology translation processes across MSE's sustainability domains. •Oversee the RIE Urban Solutions and Sustainability Domain funds in conjunction with NRF and MND. This includes secretariat work, R&D management and reviewing funding proposals. •Lead the planning of innovation and enterprise (I&E) efforts across MSE-F to maximise synergies and accelerate research-to-market pathways. •Facilitate the matching of R&D output to technology providers and support the adoptions through industry and regulators engagement. •Lead the productisation efforts of MSE-F R&D outputs through the USS Translation Fund and other funding mechanisms, for First-of-A-Kind (FOAK) deployment. •A degree in relevant fields such as engineering, natural sciences or sustainability areas. •2 or more years of experience in the S&T, business development or adjacent expertise in sustainability domains would be an advantage. •Strong analytical and strategic thinking skills with deep understanding of innovation ecosystem and technology translation processes. •Ability to apply systems thinking and understand complex technology-market dynamics across sustainability domains.

jobs.title: Senior/ Assistant Director, North East Asia Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 8 Apr 2026. The Ministry of Trade and Industry (MTI) seeks to transform Singapore into a leading global city of talent, enterprise and innovation. Our mission is to promote economic growth and create good jobs to enable Singaporeans to improve their lives. In MTI, you can expect an exciting, challenging and rewarding career! Our mission is to promote economic growth and create good jobs to enable Singaporeans to improve their lives. The Trade and International Economic Relations (TIER) cluster drives the formulation of Singapore's foreign economic policy and safeguards Singapore's economic interests in the international economic arena. As a TIER officer in the North East Asia (NEA) Division in MTI, you will join a team of dedicated and creative colleagues to: • Develop and implement Singapore's strategy of engaging the markets in NEA, including China. • Engage in international trade diplomacy • Support official trips, study visits and business missions. • Play an impactful role in helping Singapore build good relations with these countries and gain economically from the growth of the region. • Relevant field of study in Economics, Political Science, International Relations, or related disciplines. • Excellent analytical, communication, written and negotiation skills. • Prior experience in formulating and reviewing policy matters is preferred. • Willingness to travel. • Proficiency in Chinese language is preferred to engage the markets in NEA (including China).

jobs.title: Senior Assistant Director / Deputy Director, International Cyber Policy Office
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 26 Jun 2026. About CSA Established in 2015, the Cyber Security Agency of Singapore (CSA) seeks to keep Singapore’s cyberspace safe and secure to underpin our Nation Security, power a Digital Economy and protect our Digital Way of Life. It maintains an oversight of national cybersecurity functions and works with sector leads to protect Singapore’s Critical Information Infrastructure. CSA also engages with various stakeholders to heighten cyber security awareness, build a vibrant cybersecurity ecosystem supported by a robust workforce, pursue international partnerships and drive regional cybersecurity capacity building programmes. CSA is part of the Prime Minister’s Office and is managed by the Ministry of Digital Development and Information. For more news and information, please visit www.csa.gov.sg Responsibilities You will join and supervise a team to manage CSA’s international cyber policy work and cooperation, and in this role, engage both international parties and internal stakeholders on issues pertinent to CSA’s and Singapore’s cyber and digital security interests. Your role will involve: • Recommending CSA’s positions and responses with regard to international and regional developments that can impact Singapore’s strategic cyber and digital security interests and forging partnerships with other countries. • Implementation of cyber and digital security cooperation initiatives at both bilateral and regional platforms, and also build Singapore’s profile as a relevant player in international and regional cyber and digital security discussions. • Develop and drive the implementation of CSA’s Whole-of-Government International Engagement Strategy for Cyber and Digital Security, through the identification and conceptualisation of CSA’s strategic cyber and digital security-related interests, collaborate with international partners to design and deliver capacity-building programmes, and participate at international platforms to support the development of rules, norms, principles, and standards in cyberspace and security in the digital economy. • Your contributions will build Singapore’s overall cybersecurity capability and voice and influence in international cyber and digital security discussions. Requirements • Possess relevant tertiary qualifications. • Relevant experience international policy development/diplomacy experience. • Good understanding of geopolitical dynamics. • Strong analytical, writing and communication skills, a good command of English, and ability to engage with foreign counterparts. • Team player who possesses independent thinking and able to lead the team members to carry out the strategic directions set by Senior Management. • Keen interest in working in a fast-paced environment and be constantly exposed to emerging cyber and digital security issues. • Able to mentor and coach junior officers. If you share our passion to make a difference in the cyber security landscape, take up the challenge and apply now. All applicants will be notified of whether they are shortlisted or not within four weeks of the closing date of this job posting. For any issues with the application, you may drop your resume with us at csa_recruit@csa.gov.sg.

jobs.title: Deputy Director (Engagement)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 14 Apr 2026. Closing in 2 day(s). Reporting to the Director (Engagement and Partnership Division), the Deputy Director (Engagement) provides strategic leadership and oversight of the Ministry of Health's engagement and partnership efforts as part of the larger communications and engagement strategy. The Deputy Director (Engagement) leads a team to ensure that MOH's strategic engagement objectives are advanced through stakeholder engagement frameworks and strategies and a robust network of supportive stakeholders and third party advocates across the healthcare ecosystem. This includes setting strategic directions for the team and policy divisions to drive engagements in support of MOH’s strategic outcomes. • Drive and sustain stakeholder relationship building and managing, through effective mapping of stakeholders around relevant healthcare issues and proposing engagements to achieve intended policy outcomes. • Lead, mentor and develop team members • Drive a culture of engagement within MOH officers e.g. through capability building activities, creation of engagement tool-kits, providing guidance to divisions. Key Competencies Required: • Thinking Clearly & Making Sound Judgements – Strong analytical and critical thinking abilities to support the crafting of sound policy recommendations; ability to grasp timelines, operating context and ground realities. • Learning & Putting Skills into Action - Possesses the drive and curiosity to learn and apply new skills, knowledge and new ways of working. • Improving & Innovating – Ability to seek continual improvements, and to innovate and experiment while managing the risks involved. • Working Effectively with Stakeholders – Ability to mobilise and rally partners and stakeholders towards a collective purpose. • At least 11 years of experience in communications, public relations, or stakeholder engagement roles • Prior experience in developing stakeholder strategies, driving stakeholder engagements, sustaining stakeholder relationships and managing partnerships • At least 6 years of demonstrated experience in leading teams • Prior experience in the public sector or healthcare industry would be an advantage As part of the shortlisting process for this role, candidates may be required to complete a medical declaration and/or undergo further assessment.

jobs.title: Snr Associate /Assistant Director (Financial Systems and Services (Stat Boards))
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 10 Jun 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. jobs.title: Assistant Director, Service Archetype Lead, SIR/CCPO
jobs.inst: Ministry Of Social And Family Development
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 14 Apr 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. In your role as Service Archetype Lead, you are responsible for the product strategy and service design of a specific archetype—such as Financial Assistance, Family Services, Residential Care, Protection & Rehabilitation, or Enabling Services. You will harmonise processes across programmes within your archetype, maximise reuse of common modules, and deliver measurable improvements in caseworker productivity, service quality, and family outcomes. You operate at the intersection of policy, ops-policy, operations and product — ensuring that CaseCentral scales sustainably across programmes while balancing domain-specific needs with platform integrity. Archetype Strategy & Outcomes • Define the product vision and 2–3 year roadmap for your service archetype, aligned to CaseCentral’s product vision and MSF’s transformation goals. • Translate business and policy intent into clear OKRs and measurable impact (e.g. processing time reduction, caseload ratios). • Establish and maintain alignment between programme-level objectives and product strategy. Process Harmonisation & Service Design • Lead harmonisation of base and modular processes across programmes within your archetype. • Drive standardisation of terminology, workflows, and data models to mitigate fragmentation. • Champion reuse of existing CaseCentral modules before recommending any enhancements. Cross-Programme Prioritisation • Own prioritisation process across programmes within your archetype. • Escalate and resolve cross-division conflicts with documented rationale. Platform & Reusability Stewardship • Partner Blueprint and Engineering leads to: - Reduce onboarding time for new programmes - Improve maintainability and scalability • Prevent “Frankenstein” customisation that undermines long-term sustainability. Stakeholder Management Leadership • Act as the senior product interface for Division Directors and Programme Owners. • Facilitate domain working groups to drive consensus on harmonised designs. • Build trust with Social Service Agencies (SSAs) and ensure sector realities are reflected in product decisions. Adoption, Change & Sector Readiness • Work with Change Management to drive archetype-wide rollout readiness. • Monitor user sentiment, issue clusters and adoption metrics within your archetype. • Close feedback loops and drive continuous improvement of product. Data, Governance & Risk • Uphold data governance, privacy, quality and inter-agency sharing standards (e.g., Client/Household360). • Identify business related systemic risks early and propose mitigation plans. • 5+ years of experience in product management, service design, operations strategy, or large-scale transformation work. • Demonstrated experience managing complex, multi-stakeholder environments. • Strong systems thinking ability to understand how product decisions influence business units and the trade-offs needed to drive product success. • Experience in harmonising processes across multiple business units or programmes. • Ability to influence senior stakeholders (Director-level and above). • Strong analytical mindset with comfort defining metrics and interpreting data. • Excellent written and verbal communication skills. Nice-to-have: • Experience in case management, workflow or CRM platforms (e.g. Salesforce). • Experience in public sector or regulated environments. • Background in social services, healthcare, or public assistance domains. • Experience leading transformation across legacy systems. jobs.title: Deputy Director / Assistant Director, Enforcement (Investigator) (3-Yr Contract)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 30 Jun 2026. Closing on 30 Jul 2026. You will be an investigator in the Enforcement Department and your primary responsibility is to investigate into offences and/or regulatory breaches under all MAS-administered Acts. As an investigator in the Enforcement Department, your primary responsibility is to investigate into offences and/or regulatory breaches under all MAS-administered Acts. You will be gazetted as a Commercial Affairs Officer with criminal investigative powers under the MAS-CAD Joint Investigation Arrangement, where you will exercise comprehensive criminal investigative powers, including searching premises, seizures of evidence and effecting arrests during raid operations. Your responsibilities encompass thorough evidence gathering, conducting interviews, handling of bail procedures and preparing detailed case files as well as providing litigation support to the Attorney-General's Chambers in both criminal prosecutions and civil penalty court actions. Additionally, you will be involved in enforcing regulatory actions, such as issuing prohibition orders to not allow offenders from working in Singapore's financial industry and handling regulatory appeals. Specifically to the current role, you will specialise primarily in market abuse cases such as insider trading, market manipulation, and breaches of continuous disclosure requirements under Part XII of the Securities and Futures Act. As financial crimes are often complex and multi-faceted, you would be working closely with fellow investigation officers, and other specialists within the Enforcement Department such as market surveillance analysts, computer forensic specialists, legal officers, as well as officers from the Commercial Affairs Department, market experts/professionals and Deputy Public Prosecutors from the Attorney-General's Chambers. At least 3 years' working experience. Interest in investigation work. Strong analytical, communication and writing skills. Ability to be innovative and creative in solving problems. Prior relevant experience would be an added advantage. You will be working in a fast-paced environment that would require the ability to manage multiple priorities and needs of stakeholders, as well as the agility to respond to changes and developments. As part of the shortlisting process for this role, you may be required to complete a medical declaration and/or undergo further assessment. This is a 3-year contract . All applicants will be notified on whether they are shortlisted or not within 4 weeks of the closing date of this job posting.

jobs.title: Manager/Assistant Director (Engagement & Outreach)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 16 Jul 2026. Closing on 31 Jul 2026. The Municipal Services Office (MSO) was set up on 1 October 2014 under the Ministry of National Development to improve the Government’s coordination and delivery of municipal services. MSO works closely with public agencies and Town Councils to strengthen feedback management and improve service delivery in municipal matters. MSO continuously reviews and refines work processes to better manage and address public feedback on municipal issues so that cases can be resolved effectively and in a timely manner. It also coordinates across agencies and Town Councils to develop systemic solutions for complex issues that cut across multiple stakeholders, in order to better serve residents. The Engagement and Outreach unit supports MSO by: a. Developing and executing integrated marketing and communications strategies to enhance public understanding of municipal issues, promote awareness and use of the OneService channels (i.e. the OneService App and OneService Chatbot for reporting municipal issues), and grow the OneService community and culture of shared responsibility among residents, the community and public agencies. b. Developing engagement initiatives to strengthen community participation and manage MSO’s network of community stakeholders. You will play a key role in conceptualising, planning and implementing outreach and engagement initiatives that strengthen residents’ participation in municipal matters and grow the OneService community. You will also contribute to programmes and communications efforts that raise awareness of municipal issues, encourage responsible community behaviour and strengthen partnerships with community stakeholders and public agencies. REQUIREMENTS • Strong command of the English language. • Excellent writing, presentation, organisational and project management skills. • Good interpersonal skills and the ability to work effectively with stakeholders at all levels. • Strategic and creative thinking to design impactful communications and engagement initiatives. • Ability to take initiative and drive outreach and engagement initiatives from conceptualisation to implementation. • Ability to build partnerships and manage relationships with community stakeholders, grassroots organisations and public agencies. • Willingness to work after office hours and on weekends when necessary. • Experience in communications and content creation for diverse platforms, including editorial and visual formats (e.g. posters, videos), and/or community outreach and engagement initiatives will be an advantage.

jobs.title: Snr Policy Executive/ Assistant Director, Air Hub Division
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 10 Jul 2026. Closing in 4 day(s). Singapore is internationally renowned for our transport infrastructure and for being a global aviation and maritime hub. At the Ministry of Transport (MOT), we spearhead developments in our air, land and sea transport sectors to advance Singapore’s economic competitiveness and Singaporeans’ quality of life. We seek dynamic, analytical and committed individuals to join our able MOT team in realising our vision of a global interconnected transport hub. If you share our vision and possess the qualities we seek, do join us! Our air hub is of strategic and existential importance for Singapore. It supports economic links and people-to-people flows with the rest of the world, and catalyses growth opportunities for the Singapore economy. As competition from other air hubs in the region intensifies, we must ensure our air hub’s competitiveness for today and the future. You will be part of a team of officers that develops and reviews strategies and policies to strengthen the Singapore air hub’s competitiveness. This includes developing strategies to support Singapore’s air connectivity with other regions and countries, to deepen our passenger and cargo networks in a globally contestable market. This also includes reviewing the development and adoption of forward-looking air navigation concepts and systems, as well as policies to ensure the Singapore air hub remains safe, meets its sustainability goals, prioritises manpower productivity, and is future-ready. Looking to the future, the Changi East/ Terminal 5 development is a significant mega-project that will be operational by the mid-2030s. The team works closely with stakeholders on the planning and project management of this critical project. This includes planning for and reviewing its design, financing, and operations, to ensure that Changi will continue to remain competitive, efficient, and a place for Singaporeans. Beyond the Changi Air Hub, the team also collaborates closely with partner agencies to shape policy positions and develop strategies to develop a Low Altitude Economy in Singapore that supports citizens and businesses. In the course of your work, you will develop and review policies, which will include the collation and analysis of data, and a good understanding of the aviation ecosystem that supports our air hub. You will work closely with the Civil Aviation Authority of Singapore and other Government agencies. You will also be required to work with key industry stakeholders such as the Changi Airport Group, airlines, and ground handlers. a. Strong critical reasoning, with good analytical, numerical, and problem-solving skills. b. Excellent writing and communication. c. Good interpersonal skills, able to work well with diverse stakeholders with different interests, to drive collaboration, convergence and concrete outcomes. d. Comfortable with ambiguity, with a bias towards action, and a curiosity that drives learning. e. Good team player with a positive, can-do attitude.

jobs.title: Assistant Director/Senior Archivist, Declassification
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 27 Mar 2026. Closing on 10 Aug 2026. The National Library Board (NLB) nurtures Readers for Life, Learning Communities and a Knowledgeable Nation by promoting reading, learning and history through our network of 28 libraries across Singapore, the National Library and the National Archives of Singapore. At NLB, you can look forward to a flexible, hybrid work environment with rewarding, impactful deliverables that improve the lives of the community. With caring colleagues, you will be in an ideal environment for professional development, innovation and impact. We can offer you: • A chance to Impact the Community, bridge gaps and empower everyone with the opportunity for learning and discovery. • An opportunity to Innovate and Push Boundaries. We value ideas, curiosity and a passion for innovation to transform our libraries and archives. • An organisation that Cares for your Growth and Wellbeing through investment in staff development and wellbeing in a supportive and collaborative environment. The National Archives of Singapore (NAS) is mandated to collect, preserve and facilitate access to government records of national and historical significance. Government records serve as a valuable and authentic resource for understanding and appreciation of Singapore’s history. As the Assistant Director/Senior Archivist of the Declassification team in NAS, you will oversee and manage the team and the declassification function within NAS to facilitate the process of declassification of government files across the whole-of-government with the aim of ensuring access to these content while at the same time mitigating any risks to disclosure of sensitive information. I Oversee the Declassification Policy across the WOG a. Supervise the team in the articulation and operationalisation of the declassification policy and principles across all government agencies. b. Establish a clear policy and structured framework in place to facilitate declassification across WOG. c. Translate and align strategic requirements to be operationalised and carried out by the team. d. Oversee the proposed revisions to Instruction Manual on Management of Public Records (IM4L) to strengthen the declassification requirements. Work with the team to articulate the requirements in a clear and consistent manner. e. Work with Records Management department within NAS, to streamline between upstream (appraisal and transfer) and downstream (review and declassification) processes across the records lifecycle. f. Engage with key stakeholders e.g. MDDI (parent Ministry) and other government agencies to address their concerns, both at policy and operational level. g. Collaborate with other departments across NAS to review, propose and streamline declassification of government records across formats. II Manage Key Stakeholders on Declassification-Related Matters a. Oversee the secretariat and coordination of the National Records Advisory Committee (NRAC) and its meetings. • Plan the meeting agenda and propose key directions for the NRAC to ensure that it serves as an effective platform in advising agencies on declassification-related matters and on operationalising declassification principles. • Liaise with MDDI and respective NRAC members on the appointment and/or renewal of NRAC members’ appointments. • Monitor and propose key issues to be surfaced to NRAC for their guidance. • Plan and propose any special engagement sessions involving NRAC members. b. Oversee reporting of key updates to NLB senior management including monitoring and reporting of KPIs and progress/status updates of various workstreams of the declassification department. III Supervise and Manage Key Workstreams and Daily Operations including Review of Government Files a. Develop, monitor and update the declassification process and guidelines for review of government files. Propose recommendations to

jobs.title: Assistant / Deputy Director, Design Management - Workflow and Transactional Services Programmes
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 14 Apr 2026. GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity. At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round. Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today! Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg. The Workflow Programme focuses on products that support the design, automation, and management of complex approval and decision workflows. The Transactional Services Programme powers high-volume, citizen- and business-facing transactions across government. Together, these programmes form a core part of the public sector digital ecosystem, enabling agencies to launch, connect, and operate digital services with less reliance on custom development. As the Design Lead, you will be the most senior designer across both programmes, responsible for driving distinct design visions within each while ensuring coherence and quality within them. You will also define shared design principles, foster collaboration across teams, and strengthen design’s strategic influence alongside product and engineering leadership. The Design Lead will report to the Programme Director of both programmes. [What you will be working on] Define and drive the design vision within each programme, ensuring coherence across products while accounting for their distinct user needs and contexts. Balance capability growth and usability, guiding how new features and products are introduced without compromising clarity or simplicity. Shape programme and product roadmaps, partnering with product and engineering leads to embed design principles into strategic decisions. Mentor and empower designers to raise design standards, improve ways of working and encourage cross-team collaboration. [Who we are looking for] Seasoned design leader with experience leading the design of multiple complementary products or product suites. Strategic thinker who can balance near-term delivery priorities with long-term product and experience vision. Strong collaborator and influencer, adept at demonstrating the value of design to stakeholders through evidence and insight. Thought leader in design craft and strategy, who can establish and evolve design principles, standards and visions as products mature. Bonus points Experience designing low-code or no-code SaaS platform solutions, or multi-product ecosystems that empower users to self-serve through configuration. GovTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe it is key to innovation. Our employee benefits are based on a total rewards approach, offering a holistic and market-competitive suite of perks. We champion flexible work arrangements (subject to your job role) and trust you to manage your time to deliver your best. Learn more about life inside GovTech at go.gov.sg/GovTechCareers

jobs.jd: Posting Date: 22 May 2026. GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity. At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round. Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today! Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg. Description The Assistant Director / Deputy Director, Digital Strategy plays a key role in driving MOE’s digital transformation agenda through strategy formulation, portfolio management, workforce transformation and funding reform. The role supports the development and implementation of MOE’s Ministry Family Digitalisation Plan (MFDP), ensuring that digital priorities are anchored on clear business outcomes, sustainable operating models, realistic resourcing plans and shared business-technology ownership. The incumbent will work across MOE HQ divisions, product teams, technology teams and governance forums to translate digital priorities into actionable programmes, assess strategic value and readiness, and strengthen the shift towards product-centric, outcome-driven and user-centred delivery. [What you will be working on] Digital Strategy and Transformation Co-lead the development and refinement of MOE’s digital transformation strategies. Drive stronger shared ownership between business and technology teams, including establishing clear co-ownership models, decision rights and delivery accountability for digital priorities. Help define sustainable operating models for digital products, including long-term team structures, governance, platform dependencies, funding needs and transition from pilot to scaled implementation. Product Portfolio Management and Strategic Planning Support the stewardship of MOE’s digital portfolio, identifying opportunities to rationalise systems, reduce duplication and evolve key systems into scalable, high-value products. Apply portfolio management disciplines to assess product value, system health, strategic fit, user impact and resource requirements across MOE’s digital initiatives. Provide strategic input into product team structures, reporting lines and delivery models to support autonomy, accountability and sustained product ownership. Funding Proposal Evaluation and Investment Strategy Evaluate ICT and digital funding proposals to assess whether they are clearly anchored on user problems, measurable outcomes, strategic value and delivery readiness. Review cost justifications, funding models and investment trade-offs to ensure proposals move beyond feature-based or line-item justification towards outcome-based funding. Support the development of programme-based funding approaches where appropriate, including consolidated funding for shared platforms, cloud infrastructure, software licences and common digital services. Workforce Transformation and Operating Model Development Support DXD’s workforce transformation towards a more product-centric organisation, including the shift in workforce composition towards product, design and engineering capabilities. Work with teams to reorganise resources around user-centric problem spaces and programmes, while maintaining functional expertise development through communities of practice and the Product Development Office. Help define clear mission reporting lines, team accountabilities and role expectations across matrixed product teams. [What we are looking for] Education Degree in Public Policy, Technology Management, Business, Engineer

jobs.jd: Posting Date: 5 Mar 2026. About Government Technology Agency The Government Technology Agency (GovTech) is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity. At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better. We empower our people to master their craft through continuous and robust learning and development opportunities all year round. Our GovTechies embody our Agile, Bold and Collaborative values to deliver impactful solutions. GovTech aims to transform the delivery of Government digital services by taking an "outside-in" view, putting citizens and businesses at the heart of everything we do. Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today! Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg . How We Think About Product Operations Before describing the role, it is worth being clear about our beliefs — because they shape what we hire for, how we measure success, and what kind of leader we are looking for. Product Operations exists to make it easier for product teams to deliver citizen-centric outcomes at scale, with precision and reliability. Where Product Managers focus on "what" and "why", Product Operations focuses on "how well" and "how efficiently" we deliver. Role Overview As an Assistant Director / Deputy Director of Product Operations in DXD (Digital Excellence & Products Division), you are accountable for designing, implementing, and driving adoption of product operations capabilities within a core digital vertical portfolio. You are a senior leader who defines the vision and strategy for product operations across a cluster of agencies. You focus on operational excellence at scale — building frameworks that align with organisational strategy and enable efficient, consistent, and impactful product delivery. You partner with executive stakeholders, drive cross-functional alignment, mentor senior talent, and champion a culture of continuous improvement. In the GovTech context, this means shifting the organisation from ad-hoc coordination structures to sustained operating cadences — portfolio reviews, cross-agency alignment forums, and scaled feedback systems that continuously inform product strategy across policy cycles and legacy environments. Target Portfolio Scope You will be accountable for end-to-end product operations across a core digital vertical portfolio or cluster of agencies, which may include: Parents-facing applications and services Teacher professional learning and development platforms Student Learning Space (SLS) and related ecosystem products National placement and registration services (e.g. OnePlacement) Platform & Infrastructure (e.g. Data, AI and Identity Management) HQ functions (e.g. HR and Corporate Finance) Within this scope, you will partner closely with business owners (e.g. schools, HQ divisions, Academy of Singapore Teachers) and align with central DXD standards and practices. Key Responsibilities Organised across the 5 core pillars of Product Ops at GovTech: Strategic Partnership Define and drive the Product Ops vision and strategy across a cluster of agencies, partner with executive stakeholders to align operational priorities with agency and Smart Nation objectives Position Product Ops as a force multiplier for PMs — co-owning outcome metrics like PM Strategic Time Index, launch success rates, and Playbook Standardisation Rate Drive development and implementation of operational strategies aligned with the mission of a cluster of agencies, own funding and procurement strategies at the cluster level Drive cross-agency strate

The scope of coverage is multi-disciplinary, encompassing cybersecurity/data incidents, physical security breaches, public-facing service disruptions, and general operational failures . Process Improvement: Experience in operations design or process optimization (e.g., Lean, Six Sigma, Agile) to simplify reporting workflows and drive efficiency. Critical Skills & Competencies Executive Communication: Exceptional command of written and spoken English. Ability to translate highly technical, ambiguous, or complex incidents (like a zero-day exploit or a complex supply-chain failure) into concise, plain-language briefs for senior management.
