You will work closely with the CIO and senior leadership to deliver on ACRA's mission, corporate goals, and strategic IT direction — spanning transformation initiatives, product development, and day-to-day digital operations. At this level, you will be expected to identify strategic opportunities for the organisation, drive initiatives at a programme level over the longer term, and be accountable for realising outcomes that advance ACRA's transformation goals. You will collaborate with senior leadership in defining strategy and ensuring its alignment to organisational needs, while serving as a trusted thought leader within the programme and with key stakeholders. Strategic Planning and Transformation You will support the CIO in shaping and executing ACRA's strategic IT direction, including the development and refresh of the Digital Transformation Plan. This includes translating ACRA's corporate goals into a coherent digital strategy, identifying opportunities to leverage technology for regulatory excellence, and driving the organisation's transition from project-based delivery to a product-led model. You will be expected to think across the full horizon, from immediate operational needs to longer-term capability building and ensure that ACRA's digital investments are purposeful, prioritised, and aligned to mission outcomes. Programme Delivery and Oversight You will oversee the delivery of ACRA's digital programme, spanning product development, system modernisation, and service transformation initiatives including AI. This includes breaking down complex, broad problems into clear workstreams and roadmaps, orchestrating execution across multiple product and project teams, and maintaining visibility on delivery progress, risks, and outcomes. You will ensure that ACRA's portfolio of digital initiatives including key products such as CLAIR, Bizfile, and UDAP 2.0 progresses in a structured, outcome-driven manner. Product and Operations Leadership You will provide programme-level oversight across ACRA's product areas, supporting product teams in delivering continuous value to users and the business. This includes overseeing product operations ensuring that live products are performing, that feedback loops are in place, and that issues are resolved promptly. You will work with product owners and business owners to ensure that products remain anchored to business outcomes and that the organisation is deriving measurable value fr

jobs.title: Director / Deputy Director, Design Management - Data Programme
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 16 Apr 2026. GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity. At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round. Play a part in Singapore’s vision to build a Smart Nation and embark on your meaningful journey to build tech for public good. Join us to advance our mission and shape your future with us today! Learn more about GovTech at tech.gov.sg. [What you will be working on] GovTech builds the digital infrastructure behind Singapore's Smart Nation vision. The Data Programme is focused on one of the harder problems in that space: how analysts, engineers, and policymakers across government discover, access, and make sense of data that shapes real decisions. As a Director / Deputy Director, Design Management, you'd be the most senior design voice in the programme — setting direction, defining what good looks like, and building interaction models that don't really have a playbook yet. Lead discovery across the data ecosystem — uncovering where agencies get stuck accessing, understanding, and acting on government data Design interaction models that span the full stack: UI, APIs, CLI, and AI-driven interfaces, with coherence across all of them Define the principles and patterns for how data is surfaced, trusted, and used in high-stakes, sensitive environments Shape how the programme thinks about design — influencing product, engineering, and policy on what good looks like and why it matters Drive adoption of what gets built, treating usage and impact as design problems, not someone else's problem You'll thrive here if You think in systems and interaction paradigms, not just screens You're energised by problems that don't have established playbooks You hold a strong point of view and know how to bring senior stakeholders along with it You care as much about whether something gets used as whether it gets built [What we are looking for] 12+ years of experience, with a track record at principal level — shaping platforms, ecosystems, or systems-scale products i n multi-stakeholder environments Deep experience with data-heavy products: search, APIs, dashboards, or developer tooling Technical fluency — comfortable enough with APIs and data models to co-create with engineers, not just hand off to them A portfolio that shows paradigm-level thinking, not just execution; experience in AI-native interactions or regulated/public sector environments is a strong plus What you can expect A genuinely novel problem and the seniority to shape how it gets solved — this is not an execution role- Work with real consequences: the data infrastructure you help define underpins how government operates and makes decisions GovTech is an equal opportunity employer committed to fostering an inclusive workplace that values diverse voices and perspectives, as we believe it is key to innovation. Our employee benefits are based on a total rewards approach, offering a holistic and market-competitive suite of perks. We champion flexible work arrangements (subject to your job role) and trust you to manage your time to deliver your best. Learn more about life inside GovTech at go.gov.sg/GovTechCareers. Stay connected with us on social media at go.gov.sg/ConnectWithGovTech

jobs.title: Director / Deputy Director, Engineering Management (Product Development Office)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 28 Apr 2026. GovTech is the lead agency driving Singapore’s Smart Nation initiatives and public sector digital transformation. As the Centre of Excellence for Infocomm Technology and Smart Systems (ICT & SS), GovTech develops the Singapore Government’s capabilities in Data Science & Artificial Intelligence, Application Development, Smart City Technology, Digital Infrastructure, and Cybersecurity. At GovTech, we offer you a purposeful career to make lives better where we empower our people to master their craft through robust learning and development opportunities all year round. [Background] Product Development Offices (PDOs) is a new construct within the GDT CTOF to bring together Product, Design, and Engineering leadership to help Ministries and their agencies deliver digital products faster, more securely, and with better user outcomes. Each PDO partners closely with a Ministry CIO/CD, supports multiple agency product teams, and connects those teams back to GovTech’s shared platforms and practices. The PDO Engineering Leader represents modern *product engineering* - building teams that can move confidently from problem discovery and early experiments to scalable, reliable, user-centred digital services. They operate as the "Head of Engineering" for the Ministry, shaping outcome-focused delivery and platform-aligned technical decisions across agencies. Beyond guiding day-to-day delivery, PDO Engineering Leaders shape the Ministry’s technical direction through a product mindset — ensuring coherence across systems, promoting reuse and shared value, avoiding unnecessary complexity, and lifting engineering capability across both internal and vendor teams. They steer teams toward clear problem definitions, measurable outcomes, and evidence-based decisions. The Engineering Lead in a Product Development Office (PDO) is a modern technical leader - the "Head of Engineering" for a Ministry - someone who’s built, designed, or operated real systems, understands how technology decisions play out in practice, build capability, and can guide teams through the full journey from POC to scale. [What you will be working on] As Director/Deputy Director, you extend your leadership across multiple squads executing across multiple delivery threads (as manager of managers or across workstreams through tech leads) or a broader product (or infra) area, ensuring alignment, consistency, and impact across teams. You oversee multiple squads (including internal staff and/or vendor teams) by setting a unified technical vision and enforcing multi-squad standards that drive measurable impact. Your responsibilities include coaching EMs and Tech Leads, fostering cross-squad collaboration, and ensuring that your leadership structure remains lean and focused. You are accountable for delivering consistent outcomes and high-quality solutions by balancing innovative initiatives with essential BAU improvements, making strategic decisions that always benefit the organisation's long-term objectives Role and Responsibilities Establish a unified architectural vision and technical standards that meets organisational requirements. Set rigorous expectations for security, scalability, and advanced DevOps/SRE practices across squads. Define and champion platform-wide architecture, tools, and practices that improve org-wide scalability, security, and velocity. Ensure systems are sustainable, cost-effective, and adaptable to future needs. Oversee design reviews and drive large-scale initiatives (e.g., major refactoring, system migrations) that yield quantifiable improvements. jobs.title: Snr Assistant Director/ Deputy Director (In-Employment Upgrading & Job Redesign)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 3 Jul 2026. Reporting to the Director (Workforce Development), you will provide leadership and strategic direction to the In-Employment Upgrading team in the Workforce Development cluster in PSD. You will lead the In-Employment Upgrading team to work with public agencies to implement workforce development initiatives at the sectoral and agency level to help them transform the workforce and be more future-ready. The team works to enhance the relevance and diversity of public officers’ skills, partnering with public agencies and training providers to scope and implement in-employment skills training and conversion initiatives, competency-based HR management, and other workforce development strategies (e.g. job rotation). You will lead the team to: · Work with agencies to translate business priorities into workforce development strategies and implementation plans at the functional, sectoral and agency levels · Work with agencies to identify competencies needed for their jobs, and support the implementation and use of competencies across HR processes such as recruitment, in-employment training and performance management · Partner functional and sectoral leaders in the development and refresh of competency frameworks and supporting resources · Partner CSC at a strategic level to fund for outcomes and drive performance · Drive workforce initiatives by identifying internal workforce needs and working with partners to develop, implement and drive adoption of workforce development solutions, such as short-term immersion programmes, gig work, structured job rotation and talent attachment programme · Drive skills-based volunteering among public officers by partnering with people sector organisations to create and mainstream volunteering opportunities · Track progress and drive continuous enhancement of career development programmes to develop an agile and skilled workforce · Manage workforce development projects and partnerships Other Workforce Development Initiatives · Work with key stakeholders to curate developmental opportunities for public officers, including developing relevant policies and processes to facilitate implementation · Manage the communications and marketing of these developmental opportunities and attend to queries from public officers and agencies We are looking for self-motivated individuals who value integrity, teamwork and take pride in going the extra mile to provide excellent service. [Key Competencies] [Thinking Clearly and Making Sound Judgements] Strong analytical and critical thinking abilities. Able to make sound and timely decisions. Thinks ahead to anticipate issues, identify opportunities and appreciate implications. [Working Effectively with Stakeholders] : Work effectively with and mobilise stakeholders to act and deliver outcomes. Creates and promotes opportunities for collaboration across organisational boundaries. You should possess strong written and oral communication skills to influence stakeholders towards common outcomes. [Competency Framework Development & Application]: Develop and refresh competency frameworks to define observable behaviours that lead to effective performance on the job. Contextualise and apply centrally-developed competency frameworks to the relevant HR functions. [Career Development]: Develop and implement career development strategies, frameworks, and programmes with the aim of i) helping employees build meaningful careers as they journey within and beyond the Public Service, and ii) building workforce capabilities to achieve current and long-term organisational goals. Examples of such strategies or initiatives include managing reskilling, internal transfers, voluntary and involuntary exits, retirements and re-employments. [Learning & Development] : Identify learning needs and create/curate meaningful interventions that are aligned to organisational goals and strategies in order to develop a capable workforce. This might involve competency modelling, learning

jobs.title: Snr Assistant Director/Deputy Director (Career Development and Transition)
jobs.jd: Posting Date: 8 May 2026. The Career Development and Transition (CDT) team in the Workforce Development (WD) cluster is responsible for strengthening public officers’ career resilience and successfully transit from job to job in their Public Service career. This includes developing a robust and forward-looking workforce; effective programmes for skills conversion; as well as a robust “life-course” career management for public officers, such as the expansion and provision of career coaching services within the Public Service. Reporting to Deputy Director (Strategic Workforce Planning & Upskilling), you will be part of the CDT team. [Career Coaching] Coach and guide public service officers in navigating their career life stages, helping them identify their career interests and guiding them in career planning. Be a case manager for high-touch cases end-to-end per year. This includes (non-exhaustively) facilitating career transitions and job placements for individuals with more complex needs, settling the relevant administrative processes and linking up with necessary stakeholders. Serve as a Coach Buddy, to conduct mentoring sessions with new coaches and to uphold the professional standards set by the Public Service Career Coach Network (PSCCN). Facilitate Coach Learning sessions annually to promote continuous peer learning and professional growth among coaches. Support WOG and agency-level Brown Bag sessions organised by PSD to raise awareness on PSCCN and promote participation in career coaching. [Training and Development] Support the delivery of WOG Career Development Programmes annually, including CareerFitness foundational programme. Support and co-facilitate Train-the-Trainer (TTT) sessions to equip agency-nominated officers with the capability to deliver inhouse CareerFitness foundational programme. Guide newly inducted external and in-house trainers across agencies to deliver Career Development workshops, including CareerFitness foundational programme. Train PSCCN coaches in foundational coaching skills tailored for public service supervisors. [Advisory Role] Provide advisory and programme development support to CDT team, focusing on the following areas: Review and enhance WOG Career Development programmes, including interventions tailored for officers with extensive experience to ensure continued relevance and effectiveness. Develop training programme to equip supervisors with basic coaching skills. Review of the Public Service Retiree Network (PSRN), to incorporate new initiatives such as the Public Service alumni, and how to better use the PSRN to augment workforce needs during peacetime and crisis. We are looking for self-motivated individuals who value integrity, teamwork and take pride in going the extra mile to provide excellent service. The individual for this role should also be meticulous, have exceptional planning and execution skills, and the ability to handle ambiguity and change. If you are driven, resourceful and thrive in fast-paced environments, this is the role for you. [Competencies we look out for] [Career Coaching and Transition Guidance]: The ability to engage employees undergoing career transition and identify their needs and concerns. Based on a solid understanding of their needs, design and develop innovative initiatives to support a smooth career transition process for them. Help clients understand their career options within and outside of the organisation, take ownership and plan for their career. [Capability Development]: The ability to develop initiatives and programmes that uplift the capabilities of key stakeholders, such as employability training for transiting and retiring public officers, and career coaching training for the network of Public Service coaches. Work with key partners and subject matter experts in developing and delivering learning and development initiatives. Monitor and assess the impact of the initiatives on organisational outcomes [Working Effectively with Stakeholders]
